trait.name: Armored Berserker
entries: The pain caused by the iron plates bolted to the abaasy's body keep it on the edge of madness. Whenever the abaasy starts its turn with 60 hp or fewer, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 6, the abaasy goes berserk. While berserk, the abaasy has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

On each of its turns while berserk, the abaasy attacks the nearest creature it can see. If no creature is near enough to move to and attack, the abaasy attacks an object, with preference for an object smaller than itself. Once the abaasy goes berserk, it continues to do so until it is destroyed or regains all its hp.

trait.name: Dual Shields
entries: The abaasy carries two {@item shield|phb|shields}, which together give it a +3 bonus to its AC (included in its AC).

trait.name: Poor Depth Perception
entries: The abaasy has disadvantage on attack rolls against a target more than 30 feet away from it.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The abaasy makes three melee attacks, only one of which can be a Shield Shove attack. If the abaasy uses two hands to make a Spear attack, it can't make an Iron Axe attack that turn.

action.name: Iron Axe
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) slashing damage.

action.name: Shield Shove
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 4d4 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone} or pushed up to 15 feet away from the abaasy (the abaasy's choice).

action.name: Spear
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 15 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 3d6 + 5}) piercing damage, or 18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) piercing damage if used with two hands to make a melee attack.

action.name: Eyebeam {@recharge 5}
entries: The abaasy fires a beam of oscillating energy from its eye in a 90-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in the line must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 27 ({@damage 5d10}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

vulnerable.note: from magic weapons wielded by good creatures

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Abhaddanayla casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 21}):

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Abhaddanayla fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Limited Magic Immunity
entries: Abhaddanayla can't be affected or detected by spells of 8th level or lower unless he wishes to be. He has advantage on saving throws against all other spells and magical effects.

trait.name: Puppet Master
entries: When Abhaddanayla casts the charm person spell, he can target up to seven creatures. When he casts the dominate person spell, the spell's duration is concentration, up to 8 hours. When he casts the mass suggestion spell, the spell's duration is 30 days.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Abhaddanayla makes six Claw attacks or three Serpent Bow attacks. He can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting. If Abhaddanayla hits one creature with two Serpent Bow attacks, a swarm of snakes appears in the target's space, acting as an ally of Abhaddanayla and obeying his telepathic commands. The swarm remains for 1 minute, until Abhaddanayla dies, or until he dismisses it as a bonus action. He can have no more than four swarms active at a time.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) slashing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) psychic damage, and the target is cursed if it is a creature. The magical curse takes effect whenever the target takes a short or long rest, filling the target's thoughts with horrible images and dreams. The cursed target gains no benefit from finishing a short or long rest. The curse lasts until it is lifted by a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic.

action.name: Serpent Bow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 11} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) piercing damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) poison damage.

action.name: Burning Monkey Cloud {@recharge 5}
entries: Abhaddanayla conjures a cloud of quasi-real burning, screaming monkeys in a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on a point he can see within 60 feet of him. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 21} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 38 ({@damage 11d6}) fire damage and 38 ({@damage 11d6}) psychic damage and becomes {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition frightened}. A {@condition frightened} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Duplicitous Disguise
entries: Abhaddanayla covers himself and anything he is wearing or carrying with a illusion that makes him look like a Small or Medium Humanoid. The illusion ends if he takes a bonus action to end it or if he dies. The changes wrought by this effect fail to hold up to physical inspection. Otherwise, a creature must take an action to visually inspect the illusion and succeed on a {@dc 21} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check to discern that Abhaddanayla is disguised.

reaction.name: Sacrificial Monkey (3/Day)
entries: When Abhaddanayla is hit by a melee attack, a withered monkey composed of shadow appears and throws itself into the path of the attack, halving the damage Abhaddanayla takes before vanishing.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: Abhaddanayla makes one Claw or Serpent Bow attack.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Abhaddanayla uses Spellcasting.

legendary.name: Misleading Escape (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Abhaddanayla becomes {@condition invisible} at the same time that an illusory double of himself appears where he is standing. After the double appears, Abhaddanayla can move up to his speed. Both effects last until the start of his next turn, but the invisibility ends early if Abhaddanayla attacks or casts a spell.

This imperious archfiend has five heads–that of a mustachioed, blue-skinned demon, a tiger, a crocodile, a vulture, and an ape-and six arms, each ending in an unsettling backward-facing paw. Dressed in elegant clothes of the finest silk, he is seated on an ornate golden throne.

In a magnificent palace, perched precariously on a high mountaintop, lives Abhaddanayla, the Rakshasa Maharajah, a {@creature greater rakshasa|CCodex} so ancient he has been reincarnated seven times, growing in evil and power with each incarnation. He is revered–and feared–by his lesser brethren as the Lord of All Rakshasas.

entries.name: Rakshasa Gathering
entries: Once a year, Abhaddanayla summons all the world's rakshasas to his palace to instruct them in the parts they are to play in his schemes across the world. This sinister conference culminates in a grotesque and bloody feast for the guests, in which dozens of humanoid captives are devoured.

entries.name: Master of Schemes and Magic
entries: Abhaddanayla is a master of illusion, enchantment, and dark rakshasa magic he has acquired through centuries of studying loathsome tomes. He rarely leaves his palace, preferring to act through his many cat's paws. Abhaddanayla's spies and agents are everywhere, steering the rulers of cities and nations in the direction he wants them to go and eliminating those that stand in his path.

entries.name: Simian Scorn
entries: Over the centuries, Abhaddanayla has clashed many times with the {@creature Monkey King|CCodex}, who delights in wrecking the rakshasa lord's schemes. The first of these conflicts occurred when the {@creature Monkey King|CCodex|Monkey King's} army of apes and monkeys stopped an invading force of human soldiers acting under secret orders from Abhaddanayla. Later, when he was captured by the rakshasa lord, the {@creature Monkey King|CCodex} set fire to Abhaddanayla's palace with his tail and escaped. Abhaddanayla's hatred for the {@creature Monkey King|CCodex} leads him to delight in magic that depicts simian suffering.

entries.name: Abhaddanayla's Lair
entries: Abhaddanayla's lair is Sky Peak Palace, a magnificent building decorated with gold, marble, and jade and built on a mountaintop. The steep trail leading to the palace is treacherous and plagued by heavy snow, belying the dangers inside. Abhaddanayla can call upon a variety of rakshasa servants, and the palace is riddled with deadly traps. When encountered in his lair, Abhaddanayla has a challenge rating of 23 (50,000 XP).

trait.name: Insanity
entries: The abysswalker has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

trait.name: Poisonous Vapors
entries: When a creature enters a space within 5 feet of the abysswalker or starts its turn there, that creature must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes 13 ({@damage 2d12}) poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} until the start of its next turn. On a success, the creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition poisoned}.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the abysswalker has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The abysswalker makes one Bite attack, one Claw attack, and one Scimitar attack, or it makes one Bite attack and two Scimitar attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d4 + 4}) piercing and 5 ({@damage 2d4}) poison damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) bludgeoning, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}) if it is a Medium or smaller creature. Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, and the abysswalker can't make Claw attacks against other targets.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

monster.name: Adult Prismatic Dragon

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The prismatic dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 17}):

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the prismatic dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The prismatic dragon uses its Frightful Presence. It then makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d6 + 6}) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Frightful Presence
entries: Each creature of the prismatic dragon's choice that is within 120 feet of the dragon and aware of it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the dragon's Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Breath Weapon {@recharge 5}
entries: The prismatic dragon uses one of the following breath weapons:

items.name: Light Beam.
items.entry: The prismatic dragon emits a beam of white light in a 90-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a {@dc 19} Dexterity saving throw, taking 45 ({@damage 10d8}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

items.name: Rainbow Blast.
items.entry: The prismatic dragon emits multicolored light in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 19} Dexterity saving throw, taking 36 ({@damage 8d8}) damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The dragon splits the damage up among acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison, choosing a number of {@dice d8|d8s} for each damage type totaling {@dice 8d8}. It must choose at least two damage types.

legendaryHeader: The prismatic dragon can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from
the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time
and only at the end of another creature's turn. The dragon regains
spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: The prismatic dragon makes a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check.

legendary.name: Tail Attack
entries: The prismatic dragon makes a tail attack.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The prismatic dragon uses Spellcasting

legendary.name: Shimmering Wings (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The prismatic dragon beats its crystalline wings. Each creature within 20 feet of the dragon must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or take 11 ({@damage 2d10}) radiant damage and become {@condition blinded} until the start of the dragon's next turn.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the creature fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Sand Camouflage
entries: The sand dragon has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in sandy terrain.

trait.name: Sandy Nature
entries: The sand dragon is infused with elemental power, and it requires only half the amount of air, food, and drink that a typical dragon if its size needs.

trait.name: Stinging Sand
entries: The first time the sand dragon hits a target with a melee weapon attack, the target must succeed on a {@dc 21} Constitution saving throw or have disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The sand dragon uses its Frightful Presence. It then makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 7}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d6 + 7}) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d8 + 7}) slashing damage.

action.name: Frightful Presence
entries: Each creature of the sand dragon's choice that is within 120 feet of the creature and aware of it must succeed on a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the dragon's Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Breath Weapon {@recharge 5}
entries: The sand dragon uses one of the following breath weapons:

items.name: Sand Blast.
items.entry: The dragon exhales superheated sand in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 21} Dexterity saving throw, taking 27 ({@damage 5d10}) piercing damage and 27 ({@damage 5d10}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If a creature fails its saving throw by 5 or more, it suffers one level of {@condition exhaustion} as it dehydrates.

items.name: Blinding Sand.
items.entry: The dragon breathes fine sand in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a {@dc 21} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} for 1 minute. A {@condition blinded} creature can take an action to clear its eyes of sand, ending the effect for it.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: The sand dragon makes a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check.

legendary.name: Tail Attack
entries: The sand dragon makes a tail attack.

legendary.name: Wing Attack (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The sand dragon beats its wings. Each creature within 15 feet of the dragon must succeed on a {@dc 21} Dexterity saving throw or take 14 ({@damage 2d6 + 7}) bludgeoning damage and be knocked {@condition prone}. The dragon can then fly up to half its flying speed.

monster.name: Ahu-Nixta Mechanon
languages: understands Deep Speech and Void Speech but has limited speech

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The mechanon doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Critical Malfunction
entries: A critical hit against the mechanon has a {@chance 20} chance of striking the casing of the construct's soul chamber, stunning the mechanon until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Soul Reactivation
entries: A mechanon reduced to 0 hp stops functioning, becoming inert. For the next hour, if a Small or larger creature that isn't a Construct or Undead dies within 30 feet of a deactivated mechanon, a portion of the creature's soul is absorbed by the mechanon, and the construct reactivates, regaining all its hp plus additional hp equal to the dead creature's CR. If the mechanon remains inert for 1 hour, it is destroyed and can't be reactivated.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The mechanon makes two Utility Arm attacks or one Slam attack and one Utility Arm attack.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Utility Arm
entries: The mechanon has one of the following attack options:

items.name: Grabbing Claw.
items.entry: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it is {@condition grappled} by the mechanon (escape {@dc 13}). Until the grapple ends, the creature is {@condition restrained}, and the mechanon can't use its Grabbing Claw on another target.

items.name: Sonic Disruptor.
items.entry: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) thunder damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Telekinetic Projector.
items.entry: The mechanon fires a ray at a target it can see within 60 feet. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or the mechanon moves it up to 30 feet in any direction. If the target is an object weighing 300 pounds or less that isn't being worn or carried, it is moved up to 30 feet in any direction. The mechanon can use the projector to manipulate simple tools or open doors and containers.

action.name: Adapt Appendage
entries: The mechanon swaps its Utility Arm with the Utility Arm of any other mechanon within 5 feet of it.

action.name: Repair (2/Day)
entries: The mechanon touches an {@creature ahu-nixta|CCodex} or a Construct. The target regains 11 ({@damage 2d8 + 2}) hp.

trait.name: Elemental Weapons
entries: The jinnborn's weapon attacks are imbued with its elemental power. When the jinnborn hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} lightning damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Flamboyant Defense
entries: While the jinnborn pirate is wearing no armor and wielding no shield, its AC includes its Charisma modifier.

trait.name: Knows the Ropes
entries: The jinnborn pirate has proficiency with airships, sandships, or waterborne ships (the jinnborn's choice). It adds its proficiency bonus to any check it makes to control the chosen type of ship, and it adds its proficiency bonus to any saving throws made while on the chosen type of vehicle.

action.name: Rapier
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) lightning damage.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, ranged 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) lightning damage.

reaction.name: Parry
entries: The jinnborn pirate adds 2 to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it. To do so, the jinnborn must see the attacker and be wielding a melee weapon.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The akanka casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}):

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The akanka can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Web Sense
entries: While in contact with a web, the akanka knows the exact location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

trait.name: Web Walker
entries: The akanka ignores movement restrictions caused by webbing.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The akanka uses Mirrored Carapace and makes one Bite attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or fall {@condition unconscious} for 1 minute. The target wakes up if it takes damage or if another creature takes an action to shake it awake.

action.name: Mirrored Carapace
entries: The akanka projects an illusory duplicate of itself that appears in its space. Each time a creature targets the akanka, if its attack roll result is below 15, it targets the duplicate instead and destroys the duplicate. The duplicate can be destroyed only by an attack that hits it. It ignores all other damage and effects. A creature is unaffected by this action if it can't see, if it relies on senses other than sight, such as {@sense blindsight}, or if it can perceive illusions as false, as with {@sense truesight}. The akanka can't use this action while in darkness.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The akkorokamui casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 17}):

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The akkorokamui can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the akkorokamui fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The akkorokamui has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magic effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The akkorokamui's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Offering of Flesh
entries: The akkorokamui can spend 1 minute carefully detaching part, or all, of one of its 100-foot-long tentacles, dealing no damage to itself. The tentacle contains a magic-imbued fleshy hook and enough meat for 25 rations (if part of a tentacle) or 50 rations (if a full tentacle), if properly preserved. The fleshy hook remains imbued with magic for 4 hours or until a creature eats it. A creature that eats the magic-infused fleshy hook regains 50 hp, then it is cured of blindness, deafness, and all diseases or restores all lost limbs (the creature's choice). The limb restoration effect works like the {@spell regenerate} spell. The fleshy hook's magic works only if the akkorokamui offered the flesh willingly.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The akkorokamui regains 15 hp at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 hp.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The akkorokamui deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The akkorokamui makes three Tentacle attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting or Healing Touch.

action.name: Tentacle
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 4d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 17}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}. The akkorokamui has eight tentacles, each of which can grapple one target.

action.name: Radiant Wave {@recharge 6}
entries: The akkorokamui releases a burst of radiant water. Each hostile creature within 30 feet of the akkorokamui must make a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw, taking 36 ({@damage 8d8}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Undead have disadvantage on the saving throw.

action.name: Healing Touch (3/Day)
entries: The akkorokamui touches another creature. The target magically regains 20 ({@damage 3d8 + 2}) hp and is freed from any curse, disease, poison, blindness, or deafness.

reaction.name: Guardian's Grasp
entries: When a creature the akkorokamui can see within 30 feet of it is the target of an attack, the akkorokamui can pull the creature out of harm's way. If the creature is willing, it is pulled up to 10 feet closer to the akkorokamui, and the akkorokamui becomes the new target of the attack. If the creature isn't willing, this reaction fails.

legendary.name: Discern
entries: The akkorokamui makes a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) or Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check.

legendary.name: Jet
entries: The akkorokamui swims up to half its swimming speed without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The akkorokamui uses Spellcasting.

legendary.name: Tentacle Sweep (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The akkorokamui spins in place with its tentacles extended. Each creature within 20 feet of the akkorokamui that isn't {@condition grappled} by it must succeed on a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw or take 19 ({@damage 4d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage and be knocked {@condition prone}. Each creature {@condition grappled} by the akkorokamui must succeed on a {@dc 17} Strength saving throw or take 12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Bloodthirsty
entries: An alabroza must drink at least 1 pint of fresh blood or milk every 24 hours, or it suffers one level of {@condition exhaustion}. Each pint of blood or milk the alabroza drinks removes one level of {@condition exhaustion}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The alabroza makes two Talons attacks or one Talons attack and one Draining Fangs attack.

action.name: Draining Fangs
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) piercing damage, and the alabroza attaches to the target. While attached, the alabroza doesn't attack. Instead, at the start of each of the alabroza's turns, the target loses 6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) hp due to blood loss. The alabroza can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement. It does so after the target is reduced to 0 hp. A creature, including the target, can use its action to detach the alabroza.

action.name: Talons
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) slashing damage.

trait.name: Conditional Invisibility
entries: The alazai is {@condition invisible} in most situations. The following situations reveal the alazai's location and enough of its form that attack rolls against it don't have disadvantage while the situation lasts:

items: In temperatures lower than 50 degrees Fahrenheit or for 1 round after it takes cold damage, the alazai's natural heat outlines it in steam.
In darkness, the alazai's burning eyes shine visibly, marking its location.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The alazai doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Fire Form
entries: The alazai can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing. A creature that touches the alazai or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) fire damage.

trait.name: Iron Disruption
entries: An alazai struck by a {@item cold iron weapon|ToB3} becomes visible and can't use its Hurl Flame attack or its Scorching Aura until the start of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The alazai makes two Burning Slam attacks or three Hurl Flame attacks.

action.name: Burning Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage plus 11 ({@damage 2d10}) fire damage. If the target is a creature or flammable object, it ignites. Until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, the target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

action.name: Hurl Flame
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 9} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 4d6 + 5}) fire damage.

action.name: Scorching Aura {@recharge 6}
entries: The alazai increases the power of its inner fire, causing metal weapons and armor to burn red-hot. Each creature within 30 feet of the alazai that is in physical contact with a manufactured metal object, such as a metal weapon or a suit of heavy or medium metal armor, takes 22 ({@damage 5d8}) fire damage and must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or drop the object if it can. If it doesn't drop the object (or take off the armor), it has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the start of the alazai's next turn.

trait.name: Back Spikes
entries: Any Small or larger creature riding the alke against its will must make {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw at the start of its turn, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Keen Sight
entries: The alke has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

trait.name: Pounce
entries: If the alke moves at least 30 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Claws attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the alke can make one Beak attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The alke makes one Beak attack and two Claws attacks.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Piercing Roll {@recharge 4}
entries: The alke tucks in its head and throws itself spikes first into nearby foes. The alke moves up to 25 feet in a straight line and can move through the space of any Medium or smaller creature. The first time it enters a creature's space during this move, that creature must make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) bludgeoning and 14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}.

reaction.name: Repelling Spikes
entries: The alke adds 3 to its AC against one melee or ranged weapon attack that would hit it. To do so, the alke must see the attacker and not be {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Imposing Figure
entries: The alleybasher uses its Strength modifier instead of its Charisma modifier when making {@skill Intimidation} checks (included above).

trait.name: I Work for the Boss (3/Day)
entries: If the alleybasher is subjected to an effect that would force it to make an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw while it is within 30 feet of its superior, the ogre can use the superior's Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier for the saving throw instead of its own.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The alleybasher makes two Fist attacks or three Throw Brick attacks. If the ogre hits one Medium or smaller creature with both Fist attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Fist
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Throw Brick
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 6} to hit range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Handful of Sandy Rocks {@recharge 6}
entries: The alleybasher scatters rocks and blinding sand in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) bludgeoning damage and is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition blinded}.

reaction.name: Intimidating Rebuff
entries: When a creature the alleybasher can see within 30 feet of it targets it with an attack, the ogre can make a Strength ({@skill Intimidation}) check, taking half a step forward, snarling, or otherwise attempting to spook the attacker. If the check is equal to or higher than the attack roll, the attacker must reroll the attack roll, using the lower of the two results.
An ogre clad in spiked leathers and wearing brass knuckles turns the corner. It cracks its knuckles and smiles wickedly, punching a hole in a brick wall for violent emphasis.

Most ogres are simple giants, driven by foul tempers and large appetites. Theirs is an existence that is often cut short by infighting, bigger giants, and defiant heroes. Some of the bolder ogres seek out the bustling cities the smaller folk call home, where their talent for violence and intimidation makes it easy for them to enjoy a life of crime.

entries.name: Goon Squad Muscle
entries: A few alleybasher ogres take jobs as bouncers and muggers on their own, but more often a street gang, a shady performer, or thieves' guild employs them as muscle. Their cruelty and propensity for violence serve them well, and their massive size counts for far more than personality in criminal circles. Gangs and guilds teach alleybashers the tools of their trade: Brick, rocks, fists, and more.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the alpine creeper remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal patch of lichen.

trait.name: Mossy Carpet
entries: The alpine creeper can enter a hostile creature's space and stop there. It can move through a space as narrow as 1 foot wide without squeezing.

action.name: Gentle Dissolution
entries: Each creature in the alpine creeper's space must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The alpine creeper can choose not to harm friendly Beasts in its space. An {@condition unconscious} creature that takes damage from this action must make a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw, remaining {@condition unconscious} on a failed save, or waking on a successful one.

action.name: Sleep Spores
entries: The alpine creeper releases sleep-inducing spores. Each Humanoid and Giant within 20 feet of the alpine creeper must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or fall {@condition unconscious} for 1 minute. The effect ends for a creature if the creature takes damage, or another creature uses an action to wake it.

action.name: Cleaning Call (1/Day)
entries: The alpine creeper sprays pheromone-laced spores, calling nearby Beasts to the feast. The called Beasts arrive in {@dice 1d4} rounds and act as allies of the alpine creeper, attacking creatures within 10 feet of it. The Beasts remain for 1 hour, until the alpine creeper dies, or until the alpine creeper dismisses them as a bonus action. Choose one {@filter Beast of challenge rating 1 or lower|bestiary|type=beast|challenge rating=[&0;&1]} or roll a {@dice d100} and consult the Summoned Beasts table.

bonus.name: Lethargic Stupor
entries: One {@condition unconscious} Humanoid or Giant in the alpine creeper's space suffers one level of {@condition exhaustion}. A creature with more than half its hp maximum can't suffer more than one level of {@condition exhaustion} from this bonus action. These levels of {@condition exhaustion} last until the creature finishes a short rest.

footer.name: Summoned Beasts
entries: Use the Summoned Beasts table to determine what kind of beasts the alpine creeper's pheromones call. Beasts that arrive at the alpine creeper's location are aggressive and prioritize attacking active threats before feasting on creatures knocked out by the creeper's spores.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The amphibolt can breathe air and water.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the amphibolt is motionless, it is indistinguishable from a large rock formation.

trait.name: Lightning Leap
entries: The amphibolt's long jump is 30 feet with or without a running start. Creatures in the amphibolt's path must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) bludgeoning damage and 7 ({@damage 2d6}) lightning damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The amphibolt makes two Bite attacks, or it makes one Electric Tongue attack and uses Swallow.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) lightning damage.

action.name: Electric Tongue
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) lightning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}). Until the grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, and the amphibolt can't make an Electric Tongue attack against another target.

action.name: Swallow
entries: The amphibolt makes one Bite attack against a Medium or smaller target it is grappling. If the attack hits, the target is swallowed, and the grapple ends. The swallowed target is {@condition blinded} and {@condition restrained}, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the ambphibolt, and it takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) lightning damage at the start of each of the amphibolt's turns. The amphibolt can have only one target swallowed at a time.

If the amphibolt takes 15 damage or more on a single turn from the swallowed creature, the amphibolt must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or regurgitate the creature, which falls {@condition prone} in a space within 5 feet of the amphibolt. If the amphibolt dies, a swallowed creature is no longer {@condition restrained} by it and can escape from the corpse using 5 feet of movement, exiting {@condition prone}.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The prismatic dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 20}):

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the prismatic dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The prismatic dragon uses its Frightful Presence. It then makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 14} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 7}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 14} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d6 + 7}) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 14} to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d8 + 7}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Frightful Presence
entries: Each creature of the prismatic dragon's choice that is within 120 feet of the dragon and aware of it must succeed on a {@dc 19} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the dragon's Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Breath Weapon {@recharge 5}
entries: The prismatic dragon uses one of the following breath weapons:

items.name: Light Beam.
items.entry: The prismatic dragon emits a beam of white light in a 120-foot line that is 10 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a {@dc 23} Dexterity saving throw, taking 90 ({@damage 20d8}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

items.name: Rainbow Blast.
items.entry: The prismatic dragon emits multicolored light in a 90-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 23} Dexterity saving throw, taking 72 ({@damage 16d8}) damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The dragon splits the damage up among acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison, choosing a number of {@dice d8|d8s} for each damage type totaling {@dice 16d8}. It must choose at least two damage types.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: The prismatic dragon makes a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check.

legendary.name: Tail Attack
entries: The prismatic dragon makes a tail attack.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The prismatic dragon uses Spellcasting.

legendary.name: Shimmering Wings (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The prismatic dragon beats its wings. Each creature within 20 feet of the dragon must succeed on a {@dc 19} Wisdom saving throw or take 16 ({@damage 3d10}) radiant damage and become {@condition blinded} until the start of the dragon's next turn.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the sand dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Sand Camouflage
entries: The sand dragon has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in sandy terrain.

trait.name: Sandy Nature
entries: The sand dragon is infused with elemental power, and it requires only half the amount of air, food, and drink that a typical dragon if its size needs.

trait.name: Stinging Sand
entries: The first time the sand dragon hits a target with a melee weapon attack, the target must succeed on a {@dc 25} Constitution saving throw or have disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The sand dragon uses its Frightful Presence. It then makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 2d10 + 8}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d6 + 8}) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d8 + 8}) slashing damage.

action.name: Frightful Presence
entries: Each creature of the sand dragon's choice that is within 120 feet of the creature and aware of it must succeed on a {@dc 20} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the dragon's Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Breath Weapon {@recharge 5}
entries: The sand dragon uses one of the following breath weapons:

items.name: Sand Blast.
items.entry: The dragon exhales superheated sand in a 90-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 25} Dexterity saving throw, taking 44 ({@damage 8d10}) piercing damage and 44 ({@damage 8d10}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If a creature fails its saving throw by 5 or more, it suffers one level of {@condition exhaustion} as it dehydrates.

items.name: Blinding Sand.
items.entry: The dragon breathes fine sand in a 90-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a {@dc 25} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} for 1 minute. A {@condition blinded} creature can take an action to clear its eyes of sand, ending the effect for it.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: The sand dragon makes a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check.

legendary.name: Tail Attack
entries: The sand dragon makes a tail attack.

legendary.name: Wing Attack (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The sand dragon beats its wings. Each creature within 15 feet of the dragon must succeed on a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw or take 15 ({@damage 2d6 + 8}) bludgeoning damage and be knocked {@condition prone}. The dragon can then fly up to half its flying speed.

trait.name: Underworld Camouflage
entries: The angler dwarf has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks to hide in rocky underground terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The angler dwarf makes two Bite attacks, or it makes one Bite attack and uses Blazing Beacon.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 4d6 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Alluring Light
entries: The angler dwarf causes the orb at the end of its lure to glow. The angler dwarf can extinguish the light as a bonus action. While the light is active, when a creature enters a space within 90 feet of the angler dwarf for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there and can see the light must make a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by the angler dwarf until the light is extinguished.

While {@condition charmed} in this way, a creature is {@condition incapacitated} and ignores the lights of other angler dwarves. If a {@condition charmed} creature is more than 5 feet away from the dwarf, the creature must move on its turn toward the dwarf by the most direct route, trying to get within 5 feet. The {@condition charmed} creature doesn't avoid {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}, but before moving into damaging terrain, such as lava or a pit, and whenever it takes damage from a source other than the angler dwarf, the creature can repeat the saving throw. A creature can also repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns. On a successful save, the effect ends on it, and the creature is immune to the alluring light of all angler dwarves for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Blazing Beacon
entries: The angler dwarf summons a bright burst of light from its lure. Each creature that isn't an angler dwarf within 20 feet of the dwarf must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) radiant damage and is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition blinded}.
A pale light bobs gently on the end of a long tendril affixed to the forehead of a gray-skinned dwarf. Saliva drips from an enormous maw filled with dagger-like teeth.

Long ago, a clan of dwarves broke away from its brethren, choosing to embrace the challenge of living in the deepest, darkest caverns of the world. In time, even the deepest-dwelling clans lost contact with this clan as it delved deeper with each generation.

entries.name: Symbiotic Predators
entries: Over millennia under the earth, the angler dwarves developed a symbiotic union with tiny elementals that now reside in the tendrils that grow from angler dwarves' heads. When hunting for food, an angler dwarf stands still, camouflaged by the magic of its symbiotic elemental and leaving only an orb of pale light visible. When a creature—whether a monster, lost miner, or unlucky adventurer—steps too close to the light, the angler dwarf surprises and attempts to consume it.

entries.name: Remnants of the Clan
entries: In their gradual transformation from dwarf to angler dwarf, the symbiotic predators retained some of the clan structures of their dwarvish ancestors. Angler dwarves are mostly solitary creatures, but they maintain a scattered sense of community and tend to dwell within a few miles of each other. If the angler dwarves perceive a great threat, they temporarily band together in large numbers in their ancestral caverns to drive it away.

trait.name: Antimagic Susceptibility
entries: The instrument is {@condition incapacitated} while in the area of an {@spell antimagic field}. If targeted by {@spell dispel magic}, the instrument must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against the caster's spell save DC or fall {@condition unconscious} for 1 minute.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The instrument doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the instrument remains motionless and isn't flying, it is indistinguishable from a normal musical instrument.

action.name: Trouble Clef
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Orchestra Hit
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) thunder damage.

action.name: Spirited Solo {@recharge 5}
entries: The instrument improvises a tune to draw listeners into entrancing thought. Each creature within 30 feet of the animated instrument that can hear the song must make a {@dc 12} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature has an epiphany and gains advantage on Charisma ({@skill Performance}) checks for 1 day.

action.name: Courageous Anthem (1/Day)
entries: The instrument plays a song that bolsters its allies. Each friendly creature within 30 feet of the animated instrument that can hear the song has a +1 bonus to attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws until the song ends. The animated instrument must take a bonus action on subsequent turns to continue playing the song. It can stop playing at any time. The song ends if the animated instrument is {@condition incapacitated}. A creature can benefit from only one Courageous Anthem at a time.

variant.name: Instruments of Varying Sizes
entries: The statistics presented here describe a Tiny instrument, such as a flute, shawm, or tambourine, but instruments of any size can be animated. To create an animated instrument of a different size, use the statistics presented here except as described below.

entries.name: Small
entries: A Small instrument, such as a lute, sitar, tabor, or violin, is portable and often requires two hands to play. A Small animated instrument has 17 ({@dice 5d6}) hit points, and its Trouble Clef and Orchestra Hit each increase by one die type. It has a challenge rating of 1/4 (50 XP).

entries.name: Medium
entries: A Medium instrument, such as a cello, kettledrum, or marimba, is nearly the size of a human and often requires the musician to remain stationary to play it. A Medium animated instrument has 27 ({@dice 6d8}) hit points, and its Trouble Clef and Orchestra Hit each increase by two die types. It has a challenge rating of 1/2 (100 XP).

entries.name: Large
entries: A Large instrument, such as an alphorn, harp, or piano, is larger than a human, often requires multiple people or magic to reposition it, and, in some cases, can be played by more than one person. A Large animated instrument has a walking speed of 30 feet in addition to its flying speed and 27 ({@dice 5d10}) hit points, and its Trouble Clef and Orchestra Hit each increase by three die types. It has a challenge rating of 1/2 (100 XP).

items.name: Trouble Clef
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 4d4 + 1}) bludgeoning damage.

items.name: Orchestra Hit
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 4d6 + 2}) thunder damage.

items.name: Trouble Clef
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 3d4 + 1}) bludgeoning damage.

items.name: Orchestra Hit
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d6 + 2}) thunder damage.

items.name: Trouble Clef
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 2d4 + 1}) bludgeoning damage.

items.name: Orchestra Hit
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) thunder damage.

trait.name: Flowing Flesh
entries: The animated offal can move through spaces as narrow as 6 inches wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Healing Sense
entries: The animated offal can sense healing spells, effects, and potions within 120 feet of it. If the ooze is the target of a healing spell, if it consumes a healing potion, or if it is affected by a similar magical effect, it gains a +2 bonus to its AC, has advantage on Dexterity saving throws, and can use its Pseudopod attack as a bonus action for 1 minute.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The animated offal doesn't require sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The animated offal makes two Pseudopod attacks.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) necrotic damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 16}) if it is a Large or smaller creature and the animated offal doesn't have two other creatures {@condition grappled}. If the target is holding or carrying one or more healing potions, there is a {@chance 25} chance one potion shatters during the attack, allowing the animated offal to absorb the healing energy and gain the benefits of its Healing Sense.

bonus.name: Subsume
entries: The animated offal begins absorbing one creature it is grappling. The creature must make a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw, taking 18 ({@damage 4d8}) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The animated offal regains hp equal to half the damage dealt. If the animated offal is at its hp maximum, it gains temporary hp for 1 hour instead. The animated offal can add temporary hp gained from this trait to temporary hp gained earlier from this trait. Its temporary hp can't exceed 48. If its temporary hp would exceed 48, a new animated offal appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the animated offal. The new Ooze is Small, doesn't have this bonus action, and has 10 hp.
A creature killed by this bonus action is fully subsumed into the animated offal and can be restored to life only by means of a {@spell reincarnate} spell or similar magic.

trait.name: Antimagic Susceptibility
entries: The quartet is {@condition incapacitated} while in the area of an {@spell antimagic field}. If targeted by {@spell dispel magic}, the quartet must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against the caster's spell save DC or fall {@condition unconscious} for 1 minute.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The quartet doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the quartet remains motionless and isn't flying, it is indistinguishable from a group of musical instruments.

trait.name: Four-Part Harmony
entries: An animated quartet is always composed of four instruments that sit or hover close together, acting with singular intent. If an attack deals at least 25 damage to the quartet then one of the instruments falls {@condition unconscious}, causing the quartet to deal one die less of damage with its Trouble Clef and Orchestra Hit actions.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The quartet uses Musical Arrangement. It then makes two Trouble Clef or Orchestra Hit attacks.

action.name: Trouble Clef
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 4d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Orchestra Hit
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 6} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d6 + 4}) thunder damage.

action.name: Musical Arrangement
entries: The quartet plays one of the following songs:

items.name: Dreadful Dirge
items.entry: The quartet plays a hair-raising tune that evokes terror. Each hostile creature within 30 feet of the quartet must succeed on a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Oppressive Overture
items.entry: The quartet plays a heavy melody that reverberates through nearby creatures. Each hostile creature within 30 feet of the quartet must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

items.name: Seditious Sonata
items.entry: The quartet plays a song that incites disobedience and rebellion. Each hostile creature within 30 feet of the quartet must succeed on a {@dc 14} Charisma saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by the quartet for 1 minute. While {@condition charmed}, a creature refuses to attack other creatures or target other creatures with harmful abilities or magical effects, and it refuses to aid its allies. A {@condition charmed} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a {@condition charmed} creature takes damage, the effect ends for it.

trait.name: Antimagic Susceptibility
entries: The symphony becomes {@condition incapacitated} if it ends its turn in the area of an {@spell antimagic field}. If targeted by {@spell dispel magic}, the symphony must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against the caster's spell save DC or fall {@condition unconscious} for 1 minute. The symphony has advantage on this saving throw.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The symphony doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the symphony remains motionless and isn't flying, it is indistinguishable from a large collection of musical instruments and performance paraphernalia.

trait.name: Grand Composition
entries: While the symphony occupies another creature's space, that creature has disadvantage on Constitution saving throws to maintain concentration, and the creature can't cast spells with verbal components.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the symphony fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The symphony has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The symphony can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the symphony can move through any opening large enough for a Small musical instrument. Except for the Harmonize legendary action, the symphony can't regain hp or gain temporary hp.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The symphony uses Musical Arrangement. It then makes two Hammer Note or Pulsating Cacophony attacks.

action.name: Hammer Note
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}36 ({@damage 8d8}) bludgeoning damage, or 18 ({@damage 4d8}) bludgeoning damage if the symphony has half its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Pulsating Cacophony
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 11} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d10}) thunder damage plus 13 ({@damage 2d12}) psychic damage, or 11 ({@damage 2d10}) thunder damage plus 6 ({@damage 1d12}) psychic damage if the symphony has half its hit points or fewer.

action.name: Musical Arrangement
entries: The symphony plays one of the following:

items.name: Ballet of Quickening Steps.
items.entry: The symphony plays a lilting ballet that picks up pace in startling fashion, increasing its movement speed by 10 feet and allowing it to take the {@action Dodge} action as a bonus action on each of its turns. This song lasts 1 minute or until the symphony plays a different song.

items.name: Harrowing Hymn.
items.entry: The symphony plays a foreboding verse. Each creature within 30 feet of the symphony that can hear it must succeed on a {@dc 19} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the symphony's Harrowing Hymn for the next 24 hours.

items.name: Concerto for the Luckless.
items.entry: The symphony plays a compelling piece, pitting nearby listeners against their own misfortunes. Each creature within 60 feet of the symphony that can hear it must succeed on a {@dc 19} Charisma saving throw or be cursed for 1 minute. While cursed, a creature can't add its proficiency bonus to attack rolls or ability checks. If the cursed creature rolls a 20 on an attack roll or an ability check, the curse ends. Alternatively, the curse can be lifted early by a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic.

items.name: Four Winds Canon.
items.entry: The symphony trumpets gale force winds in a 90-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 19} Strength saving throw. On a failure, a creature is pushed up to 30 feet away from the symphony and knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature is knocked {@condition prone}, but it isn't pushed. The winds also disperse gas or vapor, and it extinguishes candles, torches, and similar unprotected flames in the area. It causes protected flames, such as those of lanterns, to dance wildly and has a {@chance 50} chance to extinguish them.

legendary.name: Move
entries: The symphony moves up to its speed without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

legendary.name: Harmonize (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The symphony tunes its worn-out instruments back to working harmony, regaining 20 hp and ending one condition affecting it.

legendary.name: Orchestral Flourish (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The symphony plays a short, fierce melody. Each creature within 10 feet of the symphony, including creatures in its space, must make a {@dc 19} Constitution saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary.name: Syncopated Melody (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: The symphony mimics a spell that was cast since the end of its last turn. It makes a {@skill Performance} check where the DC is the caster's DC + the level of the spell the symphony is trying to mimic. If successful, the symphony casts the spell, using the original caster's DC and spell attack bonus.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The aphasian abomination can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Aphasic Field
entries: The aphasian abomination generates a field of psychic distortion around itself. Each creature that starts its turn within 60 feet of the abomination must succeed on a {@dc 17} Intelligence saving throw or lose the ability to speak coherently, saying coherent words that make no sense in context instead of whatever it intends to say. If the creature attempts to cast a spell with verbal components, it fails, taking 9 ({@damage 2d8}) psychic damage per spell level of the spell it attempted to cast, and it expends the spell slot.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The aphasian abomination doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The aphasian abomination is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Made of Magic
entries: The aphasian abomination is formed of magical energy. It temporarily winks out of existence when inside an {@spell antimagic field}, instantly reappearing once the space it occupied is no longer within the area of effect. An aphasian abomination targeted by {@spell dispel magic} takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) damage plus an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) psychic damage for each spell level beyond 3rd, if the spell is cast using a higher spell slot.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The aphasian abomination has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The aphasian abomination makes three Force Blast attacks.

action.name: Force Blast
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft., one target. {@h}23 ({@damage 4d8 + 5}) force damage.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The apostle casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Divine Weapons
entries: When the apostle hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@dice 2d8} necrotic or radiant damage (included in the attack), the apostle's choice.

trait.name: Faith's Reward
entries: When the apostle casts the {@spell bless} spell, it gains the benefit of the spell even if it doesn't include itself as a target. In addition, when the apostle restores hp to another creature, it regains hp equal to half that amount.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The apostle makes three Mace or Divine Bolt attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Mace
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d6 + 1}) bludgeoning damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) necrotic or radiant damage (the apostle's choice).

action.name: Divine Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 7} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) necrotic or radiant damage (the apostle's choice).

action.name: Destroy Undead (2/Day)
entries: The apostle presents its holy symbol and intones a prayer. Each undead within 30 feet of the apostle that can see or hear it must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw, taking 28 ({@damage 8d6}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

monster.name: Arcane Leviathan
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The arcane leviathan doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Impregnable
entries: If the arcane leviathan is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a saving throw to take only half damage, the leviathan instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The arcane leviathan deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The arcane leviathan makes one Slam attack and two Claw attacks, or it makes four Lightning Bolt attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}26 ({@damage 4d8 + 8}) slashing damage. If the target is Huge or smaller, it is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 19}). The leviathan has two claws, each of which can grapple only one target.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d4 + 8}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 19} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Lightning Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 8} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 4d6 + 3}) lightning damage.

action.name: Pylon Discharge {@recharge 5}
entries: The leviathan discharges a surge of energy at a point the leviathan can see within 200 feet of it. Each creature within 30 feet of that point must make a {@dc 19} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 45 ({@damage 10d8}) lightning damage and is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition blinded}.

bonus.name: Arcane Barrage
entries: The arcane leviathan sends arcane energy toward a creature it can see within 120 feet of it. The target begins to glow with arcane energy, and at the end of the target's next turn, it must make a {@dc 19} Constitution saving throw, taking 35 ({@damage 10d6}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The damage is divided evenly between the target and all creatures within 10 feet of it, except the arcane leviathan.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The arcane scavenger casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: An arcane scavenger doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Eldritch Overload
entries: When the arcane scavenger is reduced to half its maximum hp or fewer, its speed is doubled, and it gains an additional action each turn. The action must be to {@action Dash}, {@action Disengage}, {@action Hide}, or {@action Use an Object} or to make one Grabbing Claws or Excavation Beam attack. This effect lasts for 3 rounds. At the end of its third turn, the scavenger takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) fire damage.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The arcane scavenger has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Ruinous Detonation
entries: When the arcane scavenger dies, it explodes, and each creature within 30 feet of it must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) force damage, is flung up to 40 feet away from the scavenger, and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't flung or knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The scavenger makes three Grabbing Claw or Excavation Beam attacks.

action.name: Grabbing Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) force damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 16}). The scavenger has eight claws, each of which can grapple only one target.

action.name: Excavation Beam
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 7} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}20 ({@damage 5d6 + 3}) fire damage.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the asp vine remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal cluster of vines.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The asp vine makes four Vine attacks.

action.name: Vine
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage and 5 ({@damage 2d4}) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. If the target is a creature, it is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 14}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained} and must succeed on a new saving throw each round it remains {@condition grappled} or take another 5 ({@damage 2d4}) poison damage. The asp vine can grapple up to four targets at a time, though it can still make vine attacks against other targets even if it has four {@condition grappled} opponents.

trait.name: Apposable Thumbs
entries: The astralsupial has advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to climb.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The astralsupial has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The astralsupial makes two Claws attacks and uses Astral Pouch.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Astral Pouch
entries: The astralsupial reaches into its extradimensional pouch and chooses a point it can see within 30 feet of it. Each creature within 10 feet of that point must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. Roll a {@dice d6} to determine which of the following effects happens. The astralsupial is immune to its own Astral Pouch effects.

items.name: Ball Bearings.
items.entry: Ball bearings roll out of the pouch, scattering at the end of the astralsupial's next turn. On a failed save, a creature is knocked {@condition prone}.

items.name: Cooking Utensils.
items.entry: Cast-iron cooking pans fly out of the pouch. On a failed save, a creature takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) bludgeoning damage.

items.name: Tangled Threads.
items.entry: A mass of thread and string bursts out of the pouch. On a failed save, a creature is {@condition restrained} until a creature takes an action to break the thread by succeeding on a {@dc 10} Strength check.

items.name: Astral Mirror.
items.entry: A mirror imbued with a trace of the Astral Plane's power flashes brilliantly. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 1d4} psychic damage and is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Smelly Trash.
items.entry: Handfuls of putrid trash spill from the pouch. On a failed save, a creature is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. The creature can make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

items.name: Magic Beans.
items.entry: Magical beans bounce out of the pouch. A creature takes 10 ({@damage 4d4}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

bonus.name: Astral Traveler (2/Day)
entries: The astralsupial briefly surrounds itself in a shower of glittering dust and teleports to an unoccupied space it can see within 30 feet.

reaction.name: Playing Dead
entries: When the astralsupial is reduced to 10 hp or less, it feigns death in hopes of deceiving its attackers. A creature that sees the astralsupial in this state can determine it is a ruse with a successful {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check. This ruse lasts until the astralsupial ends it (no action required), up to 8 hours. The first attack it makes within 1 round of ending the ruse has advantage.

trait.name: Malleable Physiology
entries: At the start of its turn, the atavist gains one of the following benefits until it ends the effect (no action required) or the start of its next turn: {@sense Darkvision} out to a range of 30 feet.
A climbing, flying, or swimming speed of 30 feet.
Advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.
Bony spikes sprout along its skin, and a creature that touches the atavist or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) piercing damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The atavist makes two Keratin Blade or Bone Shard attacks. If both attacks hit one target, the target takes an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage as bits of bone dig deeper into the target.

action.name: Keratin Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Bone Shard
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d4 + 2}) piercing damage.

monster.name: Aural Hunter
senses: blindsight 60 ft. or 20 ft. while {@condition deafened} (blind beyond this radius)

trait.name: Blind Senses
entries: The aural hunter can't use its {@sense blindsight} while {@condition deafened} and unable to smell.

trait.name: Keen Hearing
entries: The aural hunter has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing.

trait.name: Sonic Sensitivity
entries: When the aural hunter takes thunder damage, it becomes {@condition deafened} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The aural hunter makes two Claws attacks and one Rib Hooks attack. It can use Consume Sound in place of one attack.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Rib Hooks
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) piercing damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}) if it is a Large or smaller creature and it doesn't have another creature {@condition grappled}.

action.name: Consume Sound
entries: The aural hunter siphons energy from the audible vibrations surrounding a creature {@condition grappled} by it. The target must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the target takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) necrotic damage and becomes {@condition deafened} and unable to speak until the end of its next turn. On a success, the target takes half the damage and is able to hear and speak. The aural hunter regains hp equal to the damage dealt. Consume Sound has no effect on creatures that are already {@condition deafened} and unable to speak. The aural hunter can't use this action if it is {@condition deafened}.

action.name: Battleaxe
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) slashing damage, or 7 ({@damage 1d10 + 2}) slashing damage if used with two hands.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) slashing damage.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 2} to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d6}) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Dig In
entries: The avestruzii has resistance to one melee weapon attack that would hit it. To do so, the avestruzii must see the attacker and must not have moved during its previous turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The avestruzii champion makes two Greataxe attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Greataxe
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d12 + 3}) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be pushed 5 feet away from the avestruzii.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 3} to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d6 + 1}) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Dig In
entries: The avestruzii champion has resistance to one melee weapon attack that would hit it. To do so, the avestruzii champion must see the attacker and must not have moved during its previous turn.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The aziza casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 12}):

trait.name: Speak with Frogs and Toads
entries: The aziza can communicate with frogs and toads as if they shared a language.

action.name: Thorn Dagger
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d4 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 6} to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute.

action.name: Befuddle
entries: The aziza magically confuses one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Wisdom saving throw or be affected as though it failed a saving throw against the {@spell confusion} spell until the end of its next turn.

reaction.name: Dazzling Glow
entries: When a creature the aziza can see targets it with a melee attack, its skin briefly glows brightly, causing the attacker to have disadvantage on the attack roll.

trait.name: Fire Weakness
entries: If the bakery drake takes fire damage, it can't use Yeast Slurry on its next turn. In addition, if the drake fails a saving throw against a spell or magical effect that deals fire damage, it becomes {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The bakery drake has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dough drake makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Breath Weapon {@recharge 5}
entries: The bakery drake uses one of the following breath weapons.

items.name: Purifying Breath.
items.entry: The drake exhales the aroma of a warm, hearty meal in a 15-foot cone. All nonmagical food and drink in the area is purified and rendered free of poison and disease, and its flavor is enhanced in quality for 1 hour.

items.name: Yeast Slurry.
items.entry: The drake expels sticky yeast in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a target takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) bludgeoning damage and is {@condition restrained} for 1 minute. On a success, a target takes half the damage and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.
