On a failure, a creature takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) bludgeoning damage and 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage, is pushed up to 15 feet away from the dawnfly in a direction following the cone, and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't pushed or knocked {@condition prone}.

legendary.name: Snatch and Drop (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: The dawnfly flies up to half its flying speed, making one Tail attack at a creature within its reach along the way. If the attack hits, the creature is {@condition grappled} and carried with the dawnfly, which immediately drops the creature at the end of the movement. The creature takes falling damage, as normal.

trait.name: Detect Life
entries: The death worm can sense the general direction of creatures that aren't Constructs or Undead within 1 mile of it.

trait.name: Discharge
entries: When a creature ends its turn within 5 feet of a death worm, the creature takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) lightning damage.

trait.name: Dreaming Venom
entries: The death worm's poison influences the dreams of those poisoned by it. A creature that succumbs to this poison has disadvantage on Wisdom saving throws and suffers strange dreams. Every 24 hours that elapse, it must repeat the saving throw, reducing its hp maximum by 2 ({@damage 1d4}) on a failure. The poison is neutralized on a success. The target dies if dreaming venom reduces its hp maximum to 0. This reduction lasts until the poison is neutralized.

trait.name: Lightning Arc
entries: When a creature starts its turn within 15 feet of at least two death worms, the creature must succeed on a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw or take 5 ({@damage 2d4}) lightning damage. The creature has disadvantage on the saving throw if it is within 15 feet of three or more death worms.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The death worm regains 3 hp at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 hp.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) lightning damage and 4 ({@damage 1d8}) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or succumb to the worm's venom (see the Dreaming Venom trait).

action.name: Spit Poison
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 15/30 ft., one creature. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d8}) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or succumb to the worm's venom (see the Dreaming Venom trait).

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The desert slime can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the desert slime remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from ordinary sand.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The desert slime doesn't require sleep.

trait.name: Sandy Ooze
entries: The desert slime can occupy another creature's space and vice versa. Its space is difficult terrain for creatures traveling through it.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) acid damage.

action.name: Mire
entries: One creature in the slime's space must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. If a creature fails the saving throw by 5 or more, it is {@condition restrained} and knocked {@condition prone}. Otherwise, a creature that fails the save is {@condition restrained}, and the slime steadily creeps up the creature, dissolving and consuming its flesh. The {@condition restrained} creature must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of turns, being pulled {@condition prone} on a failure. A {@condition restrained} creature takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) acid damage at the start of each of the slime's turns. If the {@condition restrained} creature is also {@condition prone}, it is unable to breathe or cast spells with verbal components. The slime can have only one creature mired at a time, and a mired creature moves with the slime when it moves.

A creature, including a {@condition restrained} target, can take its action to pull the {@condition restrained} creature out of the slime by succeeding on a {@dc 13} Strength check. The creature making the attempt takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) acid damage.

bonus.name: Surging Sands {@recharge 4}
entries: The desert slime takes the {@action Dash} action.

trait.name: Limited Amphibiousness
entries: The desolation nymph can breathe air and water, but it needs to be submerged at least once every 4 hours to avoid {@quickref suffocating||2}.

trait.name: Shifting Camouflage
entries: The desolation nymph's carapace adapts to its current surroundings. The nymph has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in nonmagical, natural terrain.

action.name: Hinged Maw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 15 ft., one creature. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d10 + 3}) piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 13}) and pulled to within 5 feet of the nymph. Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, and the desolation nymph can't use its Hinged Maw on another target.

action.name: Swallow
entries: The desolation nymph makes one Hinged Maw attack against a Medium or smaller target it is grappling. If the attack hits, the target is also swallowed, and the grapple ends. While swallowed, the target is {@condition blinded} and {@condition restrained}, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the nymph, and it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) acid damage at the start of each of the nymph's turns. The nymph can have only one creature swallowed at a time.
If the desolation nymph dies, a swallowed creature is no longer {@condition restrained} by it and can escape from the corpse using 5 feet of movement, exiting {@condition prone}.

bonus.name: Water Jet
entries: The desolation nymph takes the {@action Disengage} action, and each creature within 5 feet of the nymph must succeed a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Despair and anger has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Ruled by Sorrow (Despair Only)
entries: Despair and anger has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} and {@condition frightened}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Despair and anger makes three Blazing Fist or Shadow Tendril attacks, or it makes three Divine Bolt attacks.

action.name: Blazing Fist (Anger Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6fire damage and 7 ({@damage 2d6}) radiant damage.

action.name: Shadow Tendril (Despair Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) necrotic damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 16}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}. Despair and anger has three shadow tendrils, each of which can grapple only one target. At the start of each of despair and anger's turns, each creature {@condition grappled} by it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) necrotic damage, and despair and anger regains hp equal to half the total necrotic damage dealt.

action.name: Divine Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 8} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d8 + 4}) necrotic damage (if despair is active) or radiant damage (if anger is active).

action.name: Burning Rage (Anger Only) {@recharge 5}
entries: Despair and anger releases a burst of burning anger. Each creature within 30 feet of it must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 21 ({@damage 6d6}) fire damage and 21 ({@damage 6d6}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Despairing Rejection (Despair Only) {@recharge 4}
entries: Despair and anger assaults the minds of up to three creatures it can see within 30 feet of it with cold despair. Each target must make a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 17 ({@damage 5d6}) cold damage and 17 ({@damage 5d6}) psychic damage and must use its reaction to move its full speed away from despair and anger by the safest available route, unless there is nowhere to move. This movement doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and doesn't move away from despair and anger.

bonus.name: Change Aspect
entries: Despair and anger changes which aspect is in control. It can have only one aspect active at a time. A creature {@condition grappled} by the despair aspect is no longer {@condition grappled} if anger becomes the aspect in control.

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with silvered weapons

trait.name: Devil's Sight
entries: Magical darkness doesn't impede the juggler's {@sense darkvision}.

trait.name: Dizzying Movement
entries: If the juggler moves at least 15 feet on a turn, each creature that can see the movement must succeed on a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The juggler has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The juggler's long jump is up to 30 feet and its high jump is up to 15 feet, with or without a running start.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The juggler makes three Deft Touch or Devilflame Sphere attacks. If the juggler hits one creature with two Deft Touch or two Devilflame Sphere attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 18} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Deft Touch
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 3d6 + 5}) slashing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) fire damage. Instead of dealing damage, the juggler can steal one item the target is wearing or carrying, provided the item weighs no more than 15 pounds and isn't wrapped around or firmly attached to the target, such as a shirt or belt.

action.name: Devilflame Sphere
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 9} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d8 + 4}) fire damage.

action.name: Fiery Flourish {@recharge 5}
entries: The juggler tosses a ball of hellfire at a creature it can see within 90 feet of it. The target must make a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw, taking 45 ({@damage 10d8}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The ball then splits and bounces to up to four creatures within 30 feet of the target. Each of these creatures must make a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw, taking 22 ({@damage 5d8}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. This fire ignites flammable objects that aren't being worn or carried and that are between each of the targets.

bonus.name: Nimble Leap
entries: The juggler takes the {@action Dash} or {@action Disengage} action.

reaction.name: Uncanny Dodge
entries: The juggler halves the damage from an attack that hits it. The juggler must be able to see the attacker.
A four-armed devil with hands for feet and a grinning face for a torso leaps to and fro, juggling balls of fire.

Insolent bards who die and end up in Hell become twisted caricatures of themselves called devilflame jugglers. Large but very nimble, these fiends bounce around on six hands attached to limbs that extend from a grinning, diabolical visage in its torso. From this torso, the creature belches orbs of hellfire fueled by the souls of entertainers who sold theirs for notoriety.

entries.name: Bound by Absurd Law
entries: Punished by eons-old infernal contracts, devilflame jugglers are victims in their own bodies, constantly on fire inside and compelled by contract to leap about for others' amusement. To cease moving spells disaster, as more powerful, and sadistic, devils descend upon it to force it back into capering and entertaining.

entries.name: Gifts Among Princes
entries: Devilflame jugglers serve as entertainers to demons, other devils, or {@creature efreeti} on the Plane of Fire, and popular ones are given as gifts. While performing, they are often ordered to distract their hosts to free up other devilish spies for diabolical plots.

monster.name: Devouring Angel
languages: understands Common and Celestial but can't speak

trait.name: Assisted Leaping
entries: The devouring angel can use its quasi-wings to fly up to 30 feet on its turn, but it must start and end its movement on a solid surface, such as a roof or the ground. If it is flying at the end of its turn, it falls to the ground and takes falling damage.

trait.name: Flexible Form
entries: The devouring angel can twist its body into unnatural positions, allowing it to easily move through any opening large enough for a Small creature. It can squeeze through any opening large enough for a Tiny creature. The angel's destination must still have suitable room to accommodate its volume.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The devouring angel has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Limited Telepathy
entries: The devouring angel can magically communicate simple ideas, emotions, and images telepathically with any creature within 100 feet of it that can understand a language.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devouring angel makes one Bite attack and four Claw attacks. It can replace one Claw attack with a Spiked Tongue attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) piercing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) acid damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) slashing damage.

action.name: Spiked Tongue
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 17}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained} and takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) piercing damage at the start of each of its turns, and the devouring angel can pull the creature up to 15 feet closer to it as a bonus action. The devouring angel can have only one target {@condition grappled} in this way at a time.

action.name: Shed Spines {@recharge 5}
entries: The devouring angel shakes its body, sending acid-coated spines outward. Each creature within 10 feet of the devouring angel must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw, taking 18 ({@damage 4d8}) piercing damage and 24 ({@damage 7d6}) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The diomedian horse has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The diomedian horse has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the horse's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Pounce
entries: If the diomedian horse moves at 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits with a Claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the diomedian horse can make one Bite attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The diomedian horse makes one Bite attack and one Claw attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d4 + 5}) slashing damage.

trait.name: Charge
entries: If the lionfish moves at least 30 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a Headbutt attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 9 ({@damage 2d8}) piercing damage.

trait.name: Coral Camouflage
entries: The lionfish has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in underwater terrain that includes plant life or coral reefs.

trait.name: Envenomed Spines
entries: A creature that touches the lionfish or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

trait.name: Poison Affinity
entries: The lionfish has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned}.

trait.name: Water Breathing
entries: The dire lionfish can breathe only underwater.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dire lionfish makes one Bite attack and one Headbutt attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d10 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Headbutt
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Forceful Spit {@recharge 4}
entries: The dire lionfish launches a stream of pressurized water from its mouth in a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) bludgeoning damage and is pushed up to 20 feet away from the lionfish and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and is pushed up to 10 feet away from the lionfish and isn't knocked {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: The dire owlbear has advantage on melee attack rolls against a creature that doesn't have all its hit points.

trait.name: Keen Sight and Smell
entries: The dire owlbear has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight or smell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dire owlbear makes one attack with its beak and one attack with its claws.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d10 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) slashing damage.

trait.name: Edged Scales
entries: A creature that touches the pangolin or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) slashing damage.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The pangolin has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The pangolin makes two Claws attacks and one Tail Slap attack.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail Slap
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Tuck In
entries: The dire pangolin curls its entire body, forming an armored ball. While an armored ball, it moves by rolling, has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage, is immune to the {@condition prone} condition, and it can't make Claw or Tail Slap attacks or climb. The dire pangolin can uncurl its body as a bonus action.

trait.name: Trampling Charge
entries: If the dire wildebeest moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Gore attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the wildebeest can make one Hooves attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dire wildebeest uses Intimidating Glare. It then makes one Gore attack and one Hooves attack.

action.name: Gore
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d8 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Hooves
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Intimidating Glare
entries: The dire wildebeest glares at one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. If the target can see the wildebeest, the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If the target's saving throw is successful or if the effect ends for it, the target is immune to the wildebeest's Intimidating Glare for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Noxious Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The dire wildebeest exhales noxious gas in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw, taking 21 ({@damage 6d6}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Incite Stampede (1/Day)
entries: The dire wildebeest moves up to 30 feet in a straight line and can move through the space of any Medium or smaller creature. Each friendly creature within 120 feet of the dire wildebeest can use its reaction to join the stampede and move up to 30 feet in a straight line and move through the space of any Medium or smaller creature. This movement doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

The first time a stampeding creature enters a creature's space during this move, that creature must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}. For each creature in the stampede after the first, the save DC increases by 1, to a maximum of {@dc 17}, and the damage increases by 3 ({@damage 1d6}), to a maximum of {@dice 8d6}.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The div casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 14}):

trait.name: Elemental Demise
entries: If the div dies, its body disintegrates into a pool of noxious sludge, leaving behind only equipment the div was wearing or carrying.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the div has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The div makes one Claw attack and two Scimitar attacks, or it makes three Noxious Blast attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) acid damage.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Noxious Blast
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 6} to hit, ranged 60 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) acid damage.

action.name: Noxious Sands {@recharge 4}
entries: The div vomits a cloud of tainted sand in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) slashing damage and 14 ({@damage 4d6}) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The gel can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The gel can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The diving gel doesn't require sleep.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Attach
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}The gel attaches itself to the head, face, or gills of a creature. If the creature is Large or smaller and can breathe air, it continues to breathe normally as the air-filled gel provides breathable air for the creature. If the creature can't breathe air, it must hold its breath or begin to suffocate. If the gel is attached to a creature, it has advantage on attack rolls against that creature. A creature, including the target, can take its action to detach the diving gel by succeeding on a {@dc 12} Strength check.

trait.name: Invisibility Hat
entries: The dokkaebi's hat allows it to turn {@condition invisible}. This item works for another creature only if the dokkaebi formally allows that creature to borrow the hat. A creature wearing the hat with the dokkaebi's permission can use the dokkaebi's Invisibility action. If the hat is not returned to the dokkaebi when requested, the hat loses all magical properties.

trait.name: Wrestler
entries: The dokkaebi has advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to grapple and on ability checks and saving throws made to escape a grapple.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dokkaebi makes three attacks with its club.

action.name: {@item Dokkaebi Bangmangi|ToB3}
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Invisibility
entries: The dokkaebi magically turns {@condition invisible} until it attacks, or until its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the dokkaebi wears or carries is {@condition invisible} with it. It can't use this action if it doesn't have its hat.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The doom creeper uses Deaths' Rose. It then makes two Decaying Vine attacks.

action.name: Decaying Vine
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 15 ft., one creature. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) slashing and 9 ({@damage 2d8}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Death's Rose
entries: The doom creeper shoots a glowing purple beam of magical energy at a creature it can see within 60 feet of it, choosing one of the following effects:

items.name: Disorienting Petal.
items.entry: The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Intelligence saving throw or be {@condition incapacitated} for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

items.name: Frightful Petal.
items.entry: The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw or be {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, with disadvantage if the target can see the doom creeper, ending the effect on itself on a success.

items.name: Slowing Petal.
items.entry: The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or its speed is halved, it takes a -2 penalty to AC and Dexterity saving throws, and it can't take reactions for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

items.name: Wasting Petal.
items.entry: The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or waste away for 1 minute. While wasting away, the target is vulnerable to necrotic damage and regains only half the hp when it receives magical healing. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The doppelixir can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the doppelixir remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from the liquid it imitates.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The doppelixir doesn't require sleep.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The doppelixir can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage, and the doppelixir attaches to the target. While attached, the doppelixir doesn't attack. Instead, at the start of each of the doppelixir's turns, the target loses 10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) hp due to blood loss.

The doppelixir can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement. It does so after it drains 20 hp of blood from its target or the target dies. A creature, including the target, can use its action to detach the doppelixir by succeeding on a {@dc 13} Strength check.

action.name: Telepathic Urge
entries: The doppelixir mentally urges one creature it can see within 60 feet of it to attempt to drink it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 11} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} for 1 minute. While {@condition charmed}, the target must move on its turn toward the doppelixir by the safest available route, trying to get within 5 feet of the doppelixir to drink it. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If the doppelixir attacks the target, the effect also ends. If the target attempts to drink it, the doppelixir can use a reaction to make one Slam attack with advantage against the target.
If the target's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the doppelixir's Telepathic Urge for the next 24 hours.

bonus.name: Imitative Liquid
entries: The doppelixir imitates one common or uncommon potion, oil, or other alchemical substance until it uses this bonus action again to end it or to imitate a different liquid. If the doppelixir takes no acid, fire, or poison damage on the round it is slain, a creature can collect its remains, which can be used as the liquid it was imitating before it died.

trait.name: Drake Mount
entries: The drake rider has formed a bond with a Medium or larger drake mount (found in this or other books or use the statistics of a giant lizard). Regardless of the drake's intelligence, it acts as a controlled mount while carrying a drake rider, obeying the rider's spoken commands. Mounting and dismounting the drake costs the drake rider only 5 feet of movement.

trait.name: Mounted Warrior
entries: While the drake rider is mounted, its mount can't be {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the drake rider has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

trait.name: Sure Seat
entries: While mounted and not incapacitated, the drake rider can't be knocked {@condition prone}, dismounted, or moved against its will.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The drake rider makes three Spear attacks. If the rider is mounted, its mount can then make one Bite, Claw, Slam, or Tail attack.

action.name: Spear
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) piercing damage, or 7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) piercing damage if used with two hands to make a melee attack.

action.name: Trained Tactics {@recharge 4}
entries: The drake rider commands its mount to move up to 30 feet in a straight line, moving through the space of any Large or smaller creature and performing one of the following tactical maneuvers. This movement doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

action.name: Barrel Roll
entries: The mount flies up and over one creature in the line, ending its movement at least 10 feet past the target. As the drake rider hangs upside down at the top of the loop, it makes one Spear attack against the target with advantage. On a hit, the rider rolls damage dice three times. The mount must have a flying speed to use this maneuver.

action.name: Corkscrew Roll
entries: The mount swims in a corkscrew around the creatures in the line. Each creature in the line must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or be {@condition incapacitated} with dizziness until the end of its next turn. The mount must have a swimming speed to use this maneuver.

action.name: Weaving Rush
entries: The mount weaves back and forth along the line. Each creature in the line must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or take 10 ({@damage 3d6}) bludgeoning damage and be knocked {@condition prone}.

reaction.name: Failsafe Equipment
entries: The drake rider wears wing-like arm and feet flaps folded on its back. If its mount dies or it is dismounted, the rider descends 60 feet per round and takes no damage from falling, if its mount was flying, or it gains a swimming speed of 30 feet for 1 minute, if its mount was swimming.
The kobold sits astride a drake, taking a brief respite. Words pass between them, and they laugh, their wings and scales shining.
Kobold drake riders specialize in training and riding drakes—whether through the skies, the seas, or the trees. Their skills are especially valued as scouts in times of war.

entries.name: Bonded Companions
entries: The bond of trust needed between a drake and rider to work effectively together takes time and effort to forge. Since there are so many types of drakes, these kobolds specialize, focusing their training on just a few types of drakes, either taken from tradition or from proximity. Drakes and their riders often meld their families together, creating successive generations of drakes and their riders.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting (Psionics)
headerEntries: The dread examiner casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 22}):

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the dread examiner fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The dread examiner has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Otherworldly Form
entries: The dread examiner's form is tentative in its cohesion, as well as its adherence to physical laws. It is immune to effects that cause the loss of limbs, such as the effects of a sword of sharpness or vorpal sword. It is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form, and it can move through a space as narrow as 1 foot wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Psychic Awareness
entries: If the dread examiner is being directly observed at the start of its turn, it can immediately make a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check to notice the observer. Once it has noticed the observer, it always knows the observer's exact location regardless of cover, obscurement, or invisibility, as long as the observer is within 120 feet of the examiner.

trait.name: Sense Magic
entries: The dread examiner senses magic within 120 feet of it at will. This trait otherwise works like the {@spell detect magic} spell but isn't itself magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dread examiner makes three Force Swipe or Force Blast attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Force Swipe
entries: {@atk ms} {@hit 14} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}43 ({@damage 8d8 + 7}) force damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 20} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 10 feet in a direction of the dread examiner's choosing.

action.name: Force Blast
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 14} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}43 ({@damage 8d8 + 7}) force damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 20} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

legendary.name: Teleport
entries: The dread examiner magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

legendary.name: Reform (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The dread examiner rearranges its disjointed parts and regains 36 ({@damage 8d8}) hp.

legendary.name: Psychic Surge (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: The dread examiner releases a wave of psychic energy. Each creature within 20 feet of it must make a {@dc 20} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) psychic damage, can't use reactions, and has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and ignores the other effects of this legendary action.

trait.name: Undead Creator
entries: When a creature dies while trapped inside a drudge pitcher's pitcher, the drudge pitcher regains 11 ({@damage 2d10}) hp, and the corpse of the creature rises as a zombie. This works like the animate dead spell, except the zombie stays under the drudge pitcher's control for {@dice 1d4} days. At the end of this duration or when the drudge pitcher is destroyed, the corpse melts into a puddle of necrotic slime.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The drudge pitcher makes five Vine Slam attacks. It can make one Pitcher Swallow attack in place of two Vine Slam attacks.

action.name: Vine Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Pitcher Swallow
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) necrotic damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw or be scooped up into the pitcher. A creature scooped up into a pitcher is {@condition blinded} and {@condition restrained}, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the pitcher, and it takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) necrotic damage at the start of each of the drudge pitcher's turns. The drudge pitcher has four pitchers, each of which can have only one creature scooped up at a time.

If the drudge pitcher takes 30 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside one of its pitchers, the drudge pitcher must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or spill that creature out of the pitcher. The creature falls {@condition prone} in a space within 5 feet of the drudge pitcher. If the drudge pitcher dies, a creature scooped up in a pitcher is no longer {@condition restrained} by it and can escape from the pitcher using 15 feet of movement.

trait.name: Memory of Shame
entries: When a Humanoid that can see the dubius starts its turn within 30 feet of the dubius, the dubius can force it to make a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw if the dubius isn't {@condition incapacitated} and can see the Humanoid. On a failed save, the Humanoid is {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. The Humanoid can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, with disadvantage if the dubius is within line of sight, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a Humanoid's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the Humanoid is immune to the dubius's Memory of Shame for the next 24 hours.

Unless surprised, a Humanoid can avert its eyes to avoid the saving throw at the start of its turn. If the Humanoid does so, it can't see the dubius until the start of its next turn, when it can avert its eyes again. If the Humanoid looks at the dubius in the meantime, it must immediately make the save.
If a dubius sees itself reflected on a polished surface within 30 feet of it and in an area of bright light, the dubius, due to its unique creation, is affected by its own Memory of Shame.

action.name: Doubt
entries: The dubius forces a creature it can see within 30 feet of it to recall all its doubts and fears. The target must make a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature {@condition frightened} by the dubius has disadvantage on the saving throw.

action.name: Loathing
entries: The dubius sows distrust and loathing in one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must make a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the target loathes another creature of the dubius's choice within 30 feet of it and must make one attack against that creature on its next turn, moving to the creature if necessary. On a success, the target distrusts its allies and can't give or receive aid from them on its next turn, including spells and the {@action Help} action.

reaction.name: Hesitation
entries: When a creature the dubius can see attacks it, the dubius can force the creature to roll a {@dice d6}, subtracting the result from the attack roll. If this causes the attack to miss, the attacker is {@condition stunned} until the start of the dubius's next turn.

reaction.name: Self-Pity
entries: If a creature the dubius can see within 30 feet of it regains hp, the dubius regains hp equal to half that amount.

trait.name: Extradimensional Bag
entries: The duffel crab uses an extra-dimensional space, such as a bag of holding, as its "shell." The bag can hold creatures in addition to the crab, based on the size of the extradimensional space. Breathing creatures inside the bag can survive up to a number of minutes equal to 10 divided by the number of creatures (minimum 1 minute), after which time they begin to suffocate. If the bag is overloaded, pierced, or torn, it ruptures and is destroyed. While this would normally cause the contents to be lost in the Astral Plane, the crab's half-in, half-out existence kept the bag constantly open, even when it fully withdrew, altering the bag, which simply spills out its contents into the area when the bag is destroyed or the crab dies. The bag becomes a standard bag of holding or similar magic item with extradimensional space 24 hours after the crab dies.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While motionless and withdrawn into its extradimensional bag, the duffel crab is indistinguishable from its extradimensional bag with an ajar lid or opening.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The duffel crab makes two Claw attacks, or it makes one Claw attack and uses Collect.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 13}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, and the crab can't attack another target with that claw. The crab has two claws, each of which can grapple only one target.

action.name: Collect
entries: The crab places one target it is grappling into its extradimensional space, provided there is room, and the grapple ends. The target is {@condition blinded} and {@condition restrained}, and it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the extradimensional space. A trapped creature can take its action to climb out of the bag by succeeding on a {@dc 13} Strength or Dexterity check (the creature's choice). If the crab dies, a trapped creature is no longer {@condition restrained} by the crab and can escape from the bag using 5 feet of movement, exiting {@condition prone}.
An enormous hermit crab pulls itself along the beach with its deep-red claws. Oddly, the bulk of its body is hidden inside a bag much too small to contain a creature of its size.

Duffel crabs are often found along coastlines and shipping routes where shipwrecks are common. Destroyed shipping vessels using extradimensional bags, chests, and other items to save on weight leave valuable trinkets for curious creatures. Duffel crabs come into existence when a hermit crab stumbles upon an extradimensional bag and takes it up as a shell. The latent magic of the bag causes the crab to grow to an enormous size.

entries.name: Food Hoarders
entries: The crab's bag provides an excellent storage solution to save food for a later date. A duffel crab's bag is often stocked with rotting sea creatures and the bodies of unfortunate sailors collected from shipwrecks. Living creatures are no exception in the crab's eyes, as they never live long in the suffocating interior of the extradimensional space.

entries.name: Risky Treasure
entries: To budding adventurers, duffel crabs present the opportunity to gain a unique treasure, if they are experienced and strong enough to face a hungry crab the size of a draft horse.

monster.name: Duskwilt
languages: understands Common and Undercommon but can't speak

trait.name: Light Absorption
entries: Light within 60 feet of the duskwilt is reduced. Bright light in the area becomes dim light and dim light in the area becomes darkness.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The duskwilt makes three Slam or Nether Bolt attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d4 + 4}) bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Nether Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Necrotizing Spores (3/Day)
entries: Each creature within 15 feet of the duskwilt and that isn't a Construct or Undead must make a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 15 ({@damage 6d4}) poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition poisoned}. The {@condition poisoned} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. In addition, each Undead within 15 feet of the duskwilt gains 7 ({@damage 2d6}) temporary hp.
This dark blue, walking mushroom is covered in purple and green protrusions and darkens the area with its very presence.

This ambulatory fungus is a relative of myconids and often found in their colonies. The presence of duskwilts is considered a favorable thing, as their ability to absorb light gives myconids a tactical advantage against surface dwellers.

entries.name: Defensive Fungi
entries: As part of a myconid colony, the duskwilt provides a first line of defense. They are sent in against creatures with light sources to impede their vision and progress, moving close enough to absorb light attack intruders at range. If intruders persist and close with the duskwilts, they release their spores and fight hand to hand until reinforcements arrive.

entries.name: Companions to Ghouls
entries: Like the {@creature deathcap myconid|ToB|deathcap myconids}, duskwilts sometimes ally with {@creature ghoul||ghouls} and the {@creature darakhul|ToB}. A duskwilt may be assigned to a particular troop or patrol, bolstering the ghouls with their spores.

trait.name: Death Spores
entries: When the dust grazer dies, it explodes, releasing a cloud of spores. Each creature within 20 feet of the dust grazer when it dies must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or take 14 ({@damage 4d6}) poison damage and become infected with the grazer spores disease. Creatures immune to the {@condition poisoned} condition are immune to this disease.

Until the disease is cured, the creature is {@condition poisoned} and can't regain hp except by magical means. Every 24 hours that elapse, the target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or take 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage, and its hp maximum is reduced by that amount. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest after the disease is cured. The target dies if this effect reduces its hp maximum to 0. Two days after the creature dies, it rises as a dust grazer of its size, growing to Large over the course of a week.

action.name: Tendril
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) acid damage. If the target is a creature, it is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 14}) if the grazer isn't already grappling a creature.

action.name: Absorb
entries: The dust grazer makes one Tendril attack against a Medium or smaller creature it is grappling. If the attack hits, the target is also absorbed into the dust grazer's body, and the grapple ends. While absorbed, a creature is {@condition blinded} and {@condition restrained}, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the dust grazer, and it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) acid damage at the start of each of the dust grazer's turns. A dust grazer can have only one creature absorbed at a time.

If the dust grazer takes 10 damage or more on a single turn from the absorbed creature, the grazer must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or expel the creature, which falls {@condition prone} in a space within 5 feet of the grazer. If the grazer is flying, the expelled creature takes falling damage, as normal. If the grazer dies, an absorbed creature is no longer {@condition restrained}, but it has disadvantage on the saving throw against the grazer's Death Spores.

trait.name: Elemental Weapons
entries: The jinnborn's weapon attacks are imbued with its elemental power. When the jinnborn hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} acid damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Flamboyant Defense
entries: While the jinnborn pirate is wearing no armor and wielding no shield, its AC includes its Charisma modifier.

trait.name: Knows the Ropes
entries: The jinnborn pirate has proficiency with airships, sandships, or waterborne ships (the jinnborn's choice). It adds its proficiency bonus to any check it makes to control the chosen type of ship, and it adds its proficiency bonus to any saving throws made while on the chosen type of vehicle.

action.name: Rapier
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) acid damage.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, ranged 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) acid damage.

reaction.name: Parry
entries: The jinnborn pirate adds 2 to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it. To do so, the jinnborn must see the attacker and be wielding a melee weapon.

trait.name: Fiery Nature
entries: The ember glider is infused with elemental power, and it requires only half the amount of air, food, and drink that a typical Monstrosity of its size needs.

trait.name: Glide
entries: An ember glider has membranes between its fore and hind limbs that expand while falling to slow its rate of descent to 60 feet per round, landing on its feet and taking no falling damage. It can move up to 5 feet horizontally for every 1 foot it falls. The ember glider can't gain height with its gliding membranes alone. If subjected to a strong wind or lift of any kind, it can use the updraft to glide farther.

trait.name: Glow
entries: The ember glider sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius.

trait.name: Heated Body
entries: A creature that touches the ember glider or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) fire damage.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The ember glider has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Brimstone Acorn
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) fire damage. If the target is a creature or a flammable object, it ignites. Until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, the target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

action.name: Blazing Tail {@recharge 5}
entries: The ember glider lashes its tail, releasing an arc of fire. Each creature in a 15-foot cone must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fire ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.

trait.name: Aura of Draconic Virtue
entries: Kobolds within 30 feet of an empyrean kobold have advantage on attack rolls and ability checks. At the start of each of its turns, the empyrean kobold can choose to exclude any number of kobolds from this aura (no action required).

trait.name: Elemental Weapons
entries: The empyrean kobold's weapon attacks are magical. When the kobold hits with any weapon, the weapon deal an extra {@dice 3d8} acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison damage (included in the attack), the kobold's choice.

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: The empyrean kobold doesn't require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The empyrean kobold has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The empyrean kobold makes three Claw attacks. It can replace one Claw attack with a use of Divine Command.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) slashing plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison damage (the kobold's choice).

action.name: Invoke the Dragon Gods {@recharge 5}
entries: The empyrean kobold channels the might of the dragon gods and exhales elemental energy in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 49 ({@damage 14d6}) acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison damage (the kobold's choice) on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Divine Command
entries: The empyrean kobold chooses a creature it can see within its Aura of Draconic Virtue and directs a kobold within the aura to attack the target. The kobold can make one weapon attack against the target as a reaction.

action.name: Invisibility
entries: The empyrean kobold magically turns {@condition invisible} until it attacks or until its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the empyrean kobold wears or carries is {@condition invisible} with it.

reaction.name: Draconic Ascension
entries: When a kobold it can see is reduced to 0 hp, the empyrean kobold can reincarnate the kobold as a wyrmling dragon of a type befitting that kobold and its virtues. The empyrean kobold can provide ascension to up to two kobolds each hour with this reaction.
This angelic kobold shimmers as its iridescent scales shift through the full color spectrum. It floats above the battlefield on ethereal, prismatic wings.

Kobold are known for fearlessly throwing themselves into battle against any foe large or small; however, few know the underlying source of their reckless approach to combat and life in general. The truth lies in kobold's desire to impress an empyrean kobold.

entries.name: Servants of the Dragon Gods
entries: Empyrean kobolds often work with, or operate on behalf of, {@creature zirnitran|ToB3|zirnitran angels}, though they operate independently until actively called upon by the angels. Similar to the {@creature zirnitran|ToB3|zirnitran angel's} observation of dragons, empyrean kobolds secretly watch the activities of kobolds, rarely choosing to reveal themselves. When an observed kobold dies while performing any action that embodies a true dragon spirit, an empyrean kobold carries out its sacred charge and reincarnates the heroic kobold as a dragon. The dragon type always matches the spirit the kobold lived and died to embody.

trait.name: Befouling Aura
entries: At the start of each of the equitox's turns, each creature within 30 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw or have disadvantage on its next attack roll or ability check as the moisture within it becomes diseased. If a creature spends at least 1 minute in the equitox's aura or drinks water within the aura's area, it must succeed on a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw or contract the gullylung fever disease (see the Gullylung Fever trait). Creatures immune to the {@condition poisoned} condition are immune to this disease.

trait.name: Gullylung Fever
entries: A creature infected with this disease manifests symptoms {@dice 1d4} days after infection, which include difficulty breathing, dehydration, and water-themed nightmares. Until the disease is cured, at the end of each long rest, the infected creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw or its Strength score is reduced by {@dice 1d4}. This reduction lasts until the creature finishes a long rest after the disease is cured. If the disease reduces the creature's Strength to 0, the creature dies. A creature that succeeds on two saving throws recovers from the disease.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The equitox has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The equitox makes two Gore attacks. If the equitox hits one creature with both attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw or contract the {@i gullylung fever} disease (see the Gullylung Fever trait).

action.name: Gore
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d12 + 5}) piercing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Evaporation Wave {@recharge 6}
entries: The equitox exhales hot, dry breath in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in the area that isn't a Construct or Undead must make a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw, taking 22 ({@damage 5d8}) fire damage and 22 ({@damage 5d8}) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. In addition, any water in the area that isn't being worn or carried evaporates.

legendary.name: Teleport
entries: The equitox magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

legendary.name: Gore (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The equitox makes one Gore attack.

legendary.name: Withering Gaze (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The equitox locks eyes with one creature within 60 feet that can see it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 18} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition stunned} by extreme thirst until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Arm Spine Regrowth
entries: The erina has twenty-four arm spines that it can use to make Throw Spine attacks. Used spines regrow when the erina finishes a long rest.

trait.name: Expert Wrestler
entries: The erina can grapple creatures two sizes larger than itself and can move at its full speed when dragging a creature it has {@condition grappled}. If the erina grapples a Small or smaller creature, the target has disadvantage on its escape attempts. In addition, the erina has advantage on ability checks and saving throws made to escape a grapple or end the {@condition restrained} condition.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The erina has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Hardy
entries: The erina has advantage on saving throws against poison.

trait.name: Spines
entries: A creature that touches the erina or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) piercing damage. In addition, a creature {@condition grappled} by or grappling the erina takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) piercing damage at the start of the erina's turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The erina tussler makes two Punching Spines or Throw Spine attacks. If the erina hits one creature with both Punching Spines attacks, the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 13}). The erina can grapple only one target at a time.

action.name: Punching Spines
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Throw Spine
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) piercing damage, and the spine sticks in the target until a creature uses an action to remove it. While the spine is stuck, the target has disadvantage on weapon attacks that use Strength.
Sharp spines cover the back, shoulders, and arms of this hedgehog humanoid. Leather armor protects its underbelly as it adopts a fighting stance.
Some {@creature erina|ToB} relish close-quarters combat. These tusslers groom their spines to painful effect and bind their sturdiest quills onto their wrists, using them like punching daggers.

entries.name: Fighting Dirty
entries: Tusslers are notable (and somewhat suspect) in the erina communities for their fighting prowess and unconventional methods. Tusslers disregard the common erina tactic of ambushing opponents from below by burrowing; they prefer more upfront tactics such as punching wildly and sticking to foes like thorny briars.

entries.name: Flying Spines
entries: Tusslers take extra care to groom sharp spines along their arms, which they can fling at foes who are out of reach of their punches. When these flung spines stick to their targets they are quite painful, disrupting a creature's ability to counterattack.

trait.name: Ethereal Sight
entries: The ethereal drake can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane when on the Material Plane and vice versa.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ethereal drake makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) force damage. The target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}) if it is a Large or smaller creature and the drake doesn't have another creature {@condition grappled}.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Phase Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The ethereal drake exhales a blue mist in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 27 ({@damage 6d8}) force damage and is magically shifted to the Ethereal Plane for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't magically shifted. A creature shifted to the Ethereal Plane in this way can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, magically shifting back to the Material Plane on a success.

bonus.name: Ethereal Step
entries: The ethereal drake magically shifts from the Material Plane to the Ethereal Plane, or vice versa. The drake can bring creatures {@condition grappled} by it into the Ethereal Plane with it. A creature not native to the Ethereal Plane returns to the Material Plane in {@dice 1d4} rounds if brought to the plane while {@condition grappled} by the drake.
This blue-green dragon has a deathly, almost spectral appearance. Its sunken eyes and exposed ribs resemble the living dead.
Despite their unhealthy appearance, ethereal drakes are living creatures, inhabitants of the Ethereal Plane that can pass easily from their home to the Material Plane in pursuit of prey.

entries.name: Ethereal Stalkers
entries: Ethereal drakes are not picky eaters, taking their meals where they can. They put their ability to phase between planes to good use, lurking along the edges of the Ethereal, catching their prey unawares and carrying it back home to be devoured.

entries.name: Ghost Hunters
entries: Ethereal drakes have adapted for survival on the Ethereal Plane by devouring {@creature ghost||ghosts}, {@creature specter||specters}, and other spirits, using the spirits' energies to sustain their bodies. Hunters of the living dead have tried to tame ethereal drakes or raise them from hatchlings for use as tools of the trade, with a dismal amount of success. Wily hunters often try to locate an ethereal drake before visiting a haunted locale, offering the drake its fill of spirits in return for its aid in clearing the haunting.

entries.name: Desperate Origins
entries: Ethereal drakes were formed from desperate and hungry drakes trapped in the Ethereal Plane. Those that survived did so by adapting to their new home, fully capable of surviving in such a barren realm.

monster.name: Faux-Garou
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The faux-garou doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Druidic Vengeance
entries: The faux-garou knows the scent and appearance of each creature it was created to kill. It has advantage on attack rolls against such creatures and on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) and Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks to find and track them.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The faux-garou is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The faux-garou has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Necrotic Weapons
entries: The faux-garou's weapon attacks are magical. When the faux-garou hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 3d6} necrotic damage (included in the attack).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The faux-garou uses Frightening Gaze. It then makes two Claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) slashing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Frightening Gaze
entries: The faux-garou fixes its gaze on one creature it can see within 60 feet of it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the faux-garou's Frightening Gaze for the next 24 hours.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The faux-garou transforms into a Large or smaller Beast matching the type of lycanthrope it resembles, such as a wolf for a faux-garou that resembles a werewolf, or back into its true form. Its statistics, other than its size and speed, are the same in each form. While transformed, it retains a constructed appearance and claws at the end of its forelimbs. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The field commander casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 16}):

trait.name: Aura of Courage and Protection
entries: As long as the field commander is conscious, all friendly creatures within 10 feet of it are immune to being {@condition frightened} and have advantage on all saving throws.

trait.name: Divine Weapons
entries: The field commander's weapon attacks are magical. When the commander hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@dice 3d8} necrotic or radiant damage (included in the attack), the commander's choice.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The field commander uses Battle Cry, if available. It then makes three Longsword attacks, or it makes two Longsword attacks and uses Spellcasting. If the commander hits one creature with two Longsword attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Longsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) slashing damage, or 9 ({@damage 1d10 + 4}) slashing damage if used with two hands, plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) necrotic or radiant damage (the commander's choice).

action.name: Battle Cry {@recharge 5}
entries: The field commander shouts a frightful and rallying battle cry. Each hostile creature within 30 of it that can hear the cry must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. A {@condition frightened} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Each friendly creature within 30 feet of the commander and that can hear the cry has advantage on the next attack roll it makes before the start of the commander's next turn.

reaction.name: Bolster Soldier
entries: When a friendly creature the field commander can see is hit with a weapon attack, the commander calls out encouragement, and the creature gains 5 ({@damage 1d10}) temporary hp.

trait.name: Elemental Weapons
entries: The jinnborn's weapon attacks are imbued with its elemental power. When the jinnborn hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} fire damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Flamboyant Defense
entries: While the jinnborn pirate is wearing no armor and wielding no shield, its AC includes its Charisma modifier.

trait.name: Knows the Ropes
entries: The jinnborn pirate has proficiency with airships, sandships, or waterborne ships (the jinnborn's choice). It adds its proficiency bonus to any check it makes to control the chosen type of ship, and it adds its proficiency bonus to any saving throws made while on the chosen type of vehicle.

action.name: Rapier
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, ranged 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage.

reaction.name: Parry
entries: The jinnborn pirate adds 2 to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it. To do so, the jinnborn must see the attacker and be wielding a melee weapon.

trait.name: Dwarven Fleet Foot
entries: When the firecracker takes fire damage, its speed increases by 10 feet until the end of its next turn. In addition, it can immediately reroll its initiative and choose to change its place in the initiative order in subsequent rounds to the result.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The firecracker deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The firecracker makes two Wrecking Maul or Fire Blast attacks.

action.name: Wrecking Maul
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage. The target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from the firecracker and knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Fire Blast
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 3d6 + 2}) fire damage.

action.name: Combustion Wave {@recharge 5}
entries: The firecracker slams its massive hammer into the ground, battering itself and its foes with a fiery shockwave. Each creature within 20 feet of the firecracker, including itself, must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) fire damage and 10 ({@damage 3d6}) thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Creatures behind cover have advantage on the saving throw.
A soot-covered dwarf with a wicked-eyed gleam and a smoking maul strides onto the field.
Dwarven firecrackers are dwarves with an affinity for fire and are a devastating force on the battlefield.

entries.name: Reckless Fighters
entries: These burly fanatics smash through the opposition and are all too willing to risk their own wellbeing in the process. Even when they are caught in their own blast, they do not falter. Fire, whether their foe's or their own, only propels them onward.

entries.name: Fiery Mauls
entries: Their mauls are hollow and composed of several parts that operate like a piston, slamming foes away with explosive force. Whether these marvelous weapons are fueled by sorcery or alchemy is a well-kept dwarven secret, and the mauls seem to work only in the hands of the firecrackers.

entries.name: Dwarven Firecracker Mercenaries
entries: A mercenary group called the Firecrackers is headquartered in the dwarven mountain strongholds. Led by foreman and inventor Morko Gorlandsen, the Firecrackers' career began as prospectors who defended their stake with a large application of mining explosives. When raiders from a neighboring clanhold made an attempt on Morko's Mine, he and his crew enacted set off an explosion that rocked the local valley for miles and trapped the raiders between an enormous, ornamental anvil and a sheer rock face. Now the Firecrackers hire out their services as sappers and demolition experts. Armed with unique weapons designed and constantly tweaked by their industrious leader, the Firecrackers are a frightening force on the battlefield often precluded by loud bangs and wisps of dark smoke on the horizon.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The firestorm giant makes two Obsidian Axe or Firestorm Bolt attacks.

action.name: Obsidian Axe
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 3d8 + 6}) slashing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) cold damage and 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Firestorm Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 6} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d6 + 3}) fire damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) cold damage.

action.name: Lava Geyser {@recharge 5}
entries: The firestorm giant gestures at a point on the ground it can see within 60 feet of it. A fountain of molten rock erupts from that point in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder. The area is difficult terrain for 1 minute. Each creature in the cylinder must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 24 ({@damage 7d6}) fire damage and is coated in hardened lava. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't coated in hardened lava. A creature coated in hardened lava has its speed halved while it remains coated. A creature, including the coated creature, can take its action to break and remove the hardened lava, ending the effect.
The huge giant with a wedge-shaped head snarls and extends a hand. A geyser of molten lava bursts out from the ground as the gesture ends.

Firestorm giants originated with the rare offspring of a {@creature frost giant} and a {@creature fire giant}. Now their own people, firestorm giants maintain their distance from both sides of their heritage, living and celebrating their own unique culture.

entries.name: Quake Walkers
entries: Firestorm giants dwell in cold and arctic climates, and they instinctively follow fault lines and glacial valleys as their clans migrate. Occasionally, a clan settles near a volcano or large hot spring.

entries.name: Natural Protectors
entries: The fire and ice swirling through firestorm giants' veins war constantly within them, and they find solace in the rhythmic chaos of the natural world, particularly geologically active areas. Because of this, they viciously protect nature from destruction and despoilment. They harbor a deep hatred of magical winters, poisonous mine waters, dam construction, and other disruptions to nature.

entries.name: Unexpected Hospitality
entries: Firestorm giants understand the dangers of their arctic and volcanic homes and are often sympathetic to outsiders braving such dangers. Their sympathy and hospitality last only so long as the outsiders don't cause magical or unnatural disruptions to the giants' homes.

entries.name: Firestorm Giants in Midgard
entries: Firestorm giants dwell on the frozen glaciers of the Northlands where their migrations follow subterranean ley lines. Most of the clans view the God of the North Wind as a perversion of the natural order, actively working against Boreas and his frost giant clans. A few follow Loki as a figure of conjoined frost and flame, rather different than the human view.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The first servant casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 17}):

trait.name: Armor of Foresight
entries: While the first servant is wearing no armor and wielding no shield, its AC includes its Dexterity and Wisdom modifiers (included above).

trait.name: Divine Weapons
entries: The first servant's weapon attacks are magical. When the first servant hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@dice 3d8} necrotic or radiant damage (included in the attack), the first servant's choice.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The first servant uses its Awe-Inspiring Presence. It then makes three Divine Burst attacks or one Blinding Rod attack and two Divine Burst attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Blinding Rod
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d6 + 1}) bludgeoning damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) necrotic or radiant damage (the first servant's choice), and the target must succeed on a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} and {@condition deafened} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Divine Burst
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft., one target. {@h}23 ({@damage 4d8 + 5}) necrotic or radiant damage (the first servant's choice).

action.name: Awe-Inspiring Presence
entries: Each creature of the first servant's choice that is within 30 feet of the first servant and aware of it must succeed on a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw or be unable to use bonus actions or reactions until the start of the first servant's next turn.

bonus.name: Healing Hands {@recharge 5}
entries: The first servant touches a creature within 5 feet of it, and the creature regains 14 ({@damage 4d6}) hp.

trait.name: Ritual Cleansing
entries: The fixer can take 10 minutes to perform a cleansing ritual on a creature, object, or an area within 30 feet that is no larger than a 20-foot cube. For the next 8 hours, the affected creature or object is hidden from divination magic, as if it was under the effects of the {@spell nondetection} spell. In an area that is ritually cleansed, divination spells of 4th level or lower don't function, and creatures within the area can't be scried.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The fixer makes two Shortsword or Sling attacks.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Hand Crossbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) poison damage.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The floe hag casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}):

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The floe hag can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Ice Walk
entries: The floe hag can move across and climb icy surfaces without needing to make an ability check. Additionally, difficult terrain covered in ice or snow doesn't cost her extra movement.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The floe hag makes two Claw attacks or three Ice Bolt attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) slashing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) cold damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}) if the hag doesn't have another creature {@condition grappled}.

action.name: Ice Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 3d6 + 2}) cold damage.

action.name: Stash
entries: The floe hag stashes a Medium or smaller creature {@condition grappled} by her or that is {@condition incapacitated} within 5 feet of her inside an extradimensional pocket in her parka, ending the {@condition grappled} or {@condition incapacitated} condition. The extradimensional pocket can hold only one creature at a time. While inside the pocket, the creature is {@condition blinded} and {@condition restrained}, and it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the pocket. The trapped creature can take its action to escape the pocket by succeeding on a {@dc 15} Strength check and using 5 feet of movement, falling {@condition prone} in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the hag. If the floe hag dies, a trapped creature is freed, appearing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the hag's body.

action.name: Distracting Knock {@recharge 5}
entries: The floe hag raps her knuckles on the ice, creating a magical, echoing knock. Each creature within 30 feet of the hag must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw.
