On a failure, a creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) psychic damage and is {@condition incapacitated} for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition incapacitated}. While {@condition incapacitated}, a creature moves toward the hag by the safest available route on each of its turns, unless there is nowhere to move. An {@condition incapacitated} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
A woman with long, lank hair, green-scaled skin, and elongated fingers hums eerily as it emerges from a hole in the ice.
The floe hag is found in freezing cold seas. People living in the fishing villages of the far north know to limit their time on the ice to avoid a floe hag grabbing them.

entries.name: Tell-Tale Signs
entries: The floe hag clings to the underside of ice floes and knocks on the ice to attract the attention of hunters and fishermen. When a victim gets too close, the hag seizes it and stashes it inside an extradimensional pocket stitched into the back of her sealskin parka. Once she has a captive, she brings it to her lair beneath the ice.

entries.name: Eternal Captive
entries: Captives brought to the floe hag's lair are never seen again. Some say the hag is lonely and wants company, freezing the captives into statues for companionship and decoration. Others say she stays young by slowly absorbing her captives' life energy. On rare occasions, a captive that survives an extended stay in the hag's lair transforms into a new floe hag and leaves the lair to claim its own victims and lair.

monster.name: Forgotten Regent

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with silvered weapons

languages: any languages it knew in life

trait.name: Frozen Reign
entries: When a friendly Undead within 30 feet of the regent hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 4 ({@damage 1d8}) cold damage.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The regent can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the regent fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Turning Defiance
entries: The regent and any Undead within 30 feet of it have advantage on saving throws against effects that turn Undead.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The regent doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The forgotten regent makes two Frozen Rune Maul or Frost Blast attacks.

action.name: Frozen Rune Maul
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) bludgeoning plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) cold damage.

action.name: Frost Blast
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 9} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 4d6 + 5}) cold damage.

action.name: Glacier Imprisonment {@recharge 6}
entries: One creature the regent can see within 60 feet of it must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. If the saving throw fails by 5 or more, the creature is instantly {@condition petrified} in a block of magical ice. Otherwise, a creature that fails the saving throw begins to freeze and is {@condition restrained}. The {@condition restrained} creature must repeat the saving throw at the end of its next turn, becoming {@condition petrified} in a block of magical ice on a failure or ending the effect on a success. The petrification lasts until the creature is freed by the {@spell greater restoration} spell or other magic. Alternatively, the ice block can be attacked and destroyed (AC 15; hp 30; vulnerability to fire damage; immunity to poison and psychic damage), freeing the creature. When the ice block takes damage that isn't fire, the {@condition petrified} creature takes half the damage dealt to the ice block.

action.name: Frozen Citizenry (1/Day)
entries: The forgotten regent magically calls {@dice 2d4} {@creature skeleton||skeletons} or {@creature zombie||zombies} (the regent's choice) or two {@creature glacial corrupter|ToB2|glacial corrupters}. The called creatures arrive in {@dice 1d4} rounds, acting as allies of the regent and obeying its spoken commands. The Undead remain for 1 hour, until the regent dies, or until the regent dismisses them as a bonus action.

legendary.name: Army of the Damned
entries: The regent raises an arm into the air, sending out a chill wind that empowers Undead allies. Each friendly Undead within 30 feet of it gains 10 temporary hp.

legendary.name: Frozen Rune Maul (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The regent makes one Frozen Rune Maul attack.

legendary.name: Teleport (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The regent magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

trait.name: Burning Cold
entries: A creature with resistance to cold damage doesn't have resistance to the cold damage dealt by the frost-afflicted. A creature with immunity to cold damage is unaffected by this trait.

trait.name: Icy Nature
entries: The frost-afflicted is infused with minor elemental power, and it requires only half the amount of food and drink that a typical humanoid of its size needs.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The frost-afflicted makes two Frigid Punch or Frost Bolt attacks.

action.name: Frigid Punch
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) bludgeoning damage and 5 ({@damage 2d4}) cold damage.

action.name: Frost Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) cold damage.

reaction.name: Frigid Flare
entries: When a creature hits the frost-afflicted with a weapon attack, ice bursts from the frost-afflicted. Each creature within 5 feet of the frost-afflicted must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw, taking 5 ({@damage 2d4}) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with cold iron weapons

trait.name: Chilling Presence
entries: At the start of each of the frostjack's turns, each creature within 15 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or take 5 ({@damage 2d4}) cold damage. For each minute a creature spends within 15 feet of the frostjack, it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or suffer one level of {@condition exhaustion} from cold exposure. Unprotected, nonmagical flames within 15 feet of the frostjack are extinguished. Any spells of 3rd level or lower that provide resistance to cold damage and that are within 15 feet of the frostjack immediately end. Water freezes if it remains within 15 feet of the frostjack for at least 1 minute.

trait.name: Ice Walk
entries: The frostjack can move across and climb icy surfaces without needing to make an ability check. Additionally, difficult terrain composed of ice or snow doesn't cost it extra movement.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The frostjack has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The frostjack makes two Ice Blade attacks and one Winter's Touch attack.

action.name: Ice Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) piercing damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) cold damage.

action.name: Winter's Touch
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d4 + 4}) slashing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) cold damage, and the target must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature begins to freeze and has disadvantage on weapon attack rolls or ability checks that use Strength or Dexterity. It must repeat the saving throw at the end of its next turn, becoming incapacitated and unable to move or speak as it freezes solid on a failure or ending the effect on a success. The creature remains frozen until it spends at least 12 hours in a warm area thawing out, or until it takes least 10 fire damage.

action.name: Icicle Barrage {@recharge 5}
entries: The frostjack launches icicles from its hand in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 17 ({@damage 5d6}) piercing damage and 17 ({@damage 5d6}) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

reaction.name: Hoarfrost Warding (3/Day)
entries: When the frostjack takes fire damage, it gains resistance to fire damage, including against the triggering attack, until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The garmvvolf has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Multiple Heads
entries: The garmvvolf has three heads. While it has more than one head, the garmvvolf has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition blinded}, {@condition charmed}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition frightened}, {@condition stunned}, and knocked {@condition unconscious}. Whenever the garmvvolf takes 25 or more damage in a single turn, one of its heads dies. If all its heads die, the garmvvolf dies.

At the end of its turn, the garmvvolf grows two heads for each of its heads that died since its last turn, unless it has taken fire damage since its last turn. The garmvvolf regains 10 hp for each head regrown in this way.

trait.name: Trampling Charge
entries: If the garmvvolf moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Bite attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the garmvvolf can make one Bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Wakeful
entries: While the garmvvolf sleeps, at least one of its heads is awake.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The garmvvolf makes as many Bite attacks as it has heads.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d6 + 5}) piercing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Tripartite Howl {@recharge 5}
entries: The garmvvolf's heads exhale a three-part howl, one filled with poisonous spittle, one a thunderous bellow, and one a frightful bay, in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 17 ({@damage 5d6}) poison damage and 17 ({@damage 5d6}) thunder damage and becomes {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition frightened}. A {@condition frightened} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The gearmass can move through a space as narrow as 1 foot wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Clanging Mass
entries: The gearmass is filled with metal objects that clang, scrape, and clink together as it moves. When it moves, it has disadvantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks to stay silent until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The gearmass doesn't require sleep.

trait.name: Rust Metal
entries: Any nonmagical weapon made of metal that hits the gearmass corrodes. After dealing damage, the weapon takes a permanent and cumulative -1 penalty to damage rolls. If its penalty drops to -5, the weapon is destroyed. Nonmagical ammunition made of metal that hits the gearmass is destroyed after dealing damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gearmass makes two Gear Slam or Cog Toss attacks. If it hits one creature with both Gear Slam attacks, the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 14}), and the gearmass uses Engulf on the target.

action.name: Gear Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Cog Toss
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 20/60 ft., one creature. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Engulf
entries: The gearmass engulfs a Medium or smaller creature {@condition grappled} by it. The engulfed target is {@condition blinded}, {@condition restrained}, and unable to breathe, and it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw at the start of each of the gearmass's turns or take 14 ({@damage 4d6}) acid damage.

Any nonmagical ferrous metal armor, weapons, or other items the target is wearing corrode at the start of each of the gearmass's turns. If an object is either metal armor or a metal shield being worn or carried, it takes a permanent and cumulative -1 penalty to the AC it offers. Armor reduced to an AC of 10 or a shield that drops to a +0 bonus is destroyed. If an object is a metal weapon, it rusts as described in the Rust Metal trait.
If the gearmass moves, the engulfed target moves with it. The gearmass can have only one creature engulfed at a time.

trait.name: Blessed Weapons
entries: The ghost hunter's weapon attacks are magical. When the hunter hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 3d8} radiant damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Ethereal Sight
entries: The ghost hunter can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane when it is on the Material Plane, and vice versa.

trait.name: Hidden Hunter
entries: While the ghost hunter remains motionless, it is {@condition invisible} to Undead.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The ghost hunter has advantage on attack rolls against a target if at least one of the hunter's allies is within 5 feet of the target and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the ghost hunter has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

trait.name: Undead Hunter
entries: The ghost hunter has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) and Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks to find and track Undead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ghost hunter makes three Shortsword or Hand Crossbow attacks. It can replace one attack with a Flame Jet attack.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d6 + 6}) piercing damage plus 13 ({@damage 9d8}) radiant damage.

action.name: Hand Crossbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 10} to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d6 + 6}) piercing damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) radiant damage.

action.name: Flame Jet
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d6 + 4}) fire damage. If the target is a creature or a flammable object that isn't being worn or carried, it ignites. Until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, the target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

action.name: Holy Strike {@recharge 5}
entries: The ghost hunter flips up its eyepatch to reveal a holy relic embedded within the empty socket. Each creature within 30 feet of the ghost hunter must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw, taking 36 ({@damage 8d8}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If an Undead fails the saving throw, it is also {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Grappling Hook {@recharge 4}
entries: The ghost hunter launches its grappling hook at a Large or larger object or structure or at a Huge or larger creature it can see within 60 feet of it and is pulled to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

bonus.name: Elusive Hunter
entries: The ghost hunter takes the {@action Dodge} or {@action Hide} action.

reaction.name: Flame Burst
entries: When a hostile creature enters a space within 5 feet of the ghost hunter, the hunter can release a burst of fire from its clockwork hand. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw or take 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage and have disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes against the ghost hunter before the end of the ghost hunter's next turn.

trait.name: Charge
entries: If the ghost knight templar is mounted and moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a shadow blade attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 10 ({@damage 3d6}) slashing damage.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The ghost knight templar can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Turning Defiance
entries: The ghost knight templar and any undead within 30 feet of it have advantage on saving throws against effects that turn undead.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The ghost knight templar doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ghost knight templar makes three Shadow Blade attacks.

action.name: Shadow Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d8 + 5}) slashing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Stride of Damnation {@recharge 5}
entries: The ghost knight templar moves up to its speed through its enemies. This movement doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}. Each creature through which the templar passes must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 35 ({@damage 10d6}) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The templar can't use this action while mounted.

bonus.name: Ghostly Mount
entries: The ghost knight templar can summon or dismiss a {@creature ghostly mount|ToB3}, mounting or dismounting it as part of this bonus action without spending movement. The mount uses the statistics of a {@creature warhorse skeleton}, except it has the Incorporeal Movement trait, a flying speed of 60 feet, 40 hit points, and resistance to cold and necrotic damage and bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. If the mount is slain, it disappears, leaving behind no physical form, and the templar must wait 1 hour before summoning the mount again.

items.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The mount can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Blood Sense
entries: The giant flea can pinpoint, by scent, the location of creatures that have blood within 60 feet of it.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The giant flea has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The giant flea's long jump is up to 30 feet and its high jump is up to 15 feet, with or without a running start.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage, and the flea attaches to the target. While attached, the flea doesn't attack. Instead, at the start of each of the flea's turns, the target loses 4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) hp due to blood loss. The flea can detach itself by spending 5 feet of movement. It does so after draining 12 hp of blood from the target or the target dies. A creature, including the target, can take its action to detach the flea by succeeding on a {@dc 11} Strength check.

bonus.name: Leaping Escape
entries: The giant flea leaps up to 15 feet without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

trait.name: Fluorescing Challenge
entries: The giant mantis shrimp has advantage on Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks while agitated, as its natural fluorescence flashes in a disorienting array.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The giant mantis shrimp makes two Sonic Claw attacks.

action.name: Sonic Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) piercing damage. The target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 14}) if it is a Medium or smaller creature and the giant mantis shrimp doesn't have another creature {@condition grappled}. Whether or not the attack hits, the target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or take 5 ({@damage 1d10}) thunder damage and be {@condition deafened} and {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Water Jet
entries: Each creature within 5 feet of the shrimp must succeed on a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the start of its next turn. The shrimp then swims up to half its swimming speed without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

variant.name: Siege Mantis Shrimp
entries: Some giant mantis shrimp wield claws that resemble clubs more than spears. These mantis shrimp are especially devastating to terrain and structures, and are sometimes enchanted to serve deep ones or {@creature sahuagin}. A siege mantis shrimp gains the following additional trait:

entries.name: Siege Monster
entries: The mantis shrimp deals double damage to objects and structures.
In addition, the giant mantis shrimp has the following action option in place of Multiattack and Sonic Claw:

entries.name: Sonic Smash
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage. In addition, either nonmagical armor or a nonmagical shield worn or carried by the target (the shrimp's choice) is partly destroyed and takes a permanent and cumulative −1 penalty to the AC it offers. Armor reduced to an AC of 10 or a shield that drops to a +0 bonus is destroyed. Whether or not the attack hits, the target and any creature within 5 feet of it, other than the shrimp, must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or take 11 ({@damage 2d10}) thunder damage and be {@condition deafened} and {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Siege Monster
entries: The mantis shrimp deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Sonic Smash
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage. In addition, either nonmagical armor or a nonmagical shield worn or carried by the target (the shrimp's choice) is partly destroyed and takes a permanent and cumulative −1 penalty to the AC it offers. Armor reduced to an AC of 10 or a shield that drops to a +0 bonus is destroyed. Whether or not the attack hits, the target and any creature within 5 feet of it, other than the shrimp, must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or take 11 ({@damage 2d10}) thunder damage and be {@condition deafened} and {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Beast of Burden
entries: The giant mole lizard is considered to be a Huge Beast for the purpose of determining its carrying capacity.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the lizard has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lizard makes two Claw attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Constricting Tail.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Constricting Tail
entries: A Large or smaller creature within 5 feet of the lizard must succeed on a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw or be {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 14}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, can't breathe, and begins to suffocate, and the giant mole lizard can't use Constricting Tail on another target.

bonus.name: Mass Shove
entries: Each Large or smaller creature within 10 feet of the giant mole lizard and that isn't {@condition grappled} by it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from the lizard and knocked {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Spiny Body
entries: A creature that touches the giant pufferfish or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) piercing damage. While the pufferfish is puffed up, this damage increases to 5 ({@damage 2d4}).

trait.name: Water Breathing
entries: The giant pufferfish can breathe only underwater.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) piercing damage, or 12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) piercing damage while puffed up.

action.name: Spine
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) piercing damage, or 8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) piercing damage while puffed up. The target must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw, taking 5 ({@damage 2d4}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If the target fails the saving throw by 5 or more, it takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) poison damage at the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Burst of Speed {@recharge 5}
entries: The giant pufferfish takes the {@action Dash} action.

bonus.name: Puff Up (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: For 1 minute, the giant pufferfish increases in size by filling its stomach with air or water. While puffed up, the pufferfish is Medium, doubles its damage dice on Bite and Spine attacks (included in the attacks), and makes Strength checks and Strength saving throws with advantage. If the pufferfish lacks the room to become Medium, it attains the maximum size possible in the space available.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the giant walking stick remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from normal forest vegetation, such as a small tree or a large branch.

trait.name: Forest Camouflage
entries: The giant walking stick has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in forested terrain.

trait.name: Trampling Charge
entries: If the giant walking stick moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Ram attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the walking stick can make one Spine attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The giant walking stick makes two Spine attacks or one Ram attack and one Spine attack.

action.name: Ram
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d10 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Spine
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Deadfall {@recharge 5}
entries: If the giant walking stick is at least 15 feet off the ground, it can fall to the ground, landing on its feet in a space it can see within 20 feet of it that contains one or more other creatures. Each of those creatures must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage, isn't knocked {@condition prone}, and is pushed 5 feet out of the walking stick's space into an unoccupied space of the creature's choice. In addition, each creature within 10 feet of the walking stick when it lands must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take 10 ({@damage 3d6}) bludgeoning damage and be knocked {@condition prone}.

bonus.name: Startling Display
entries: Each creature within 5 feet of the walking stick must succeed on a {@dc 13} Intelligence saving throw or be {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn. The walking stick then flies up to its flying speed without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

trait.name: Telescoping Eyes
entries: The gigantura has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight, and magical darkness doesn't impede the gigantura's {@sense darkvision}. In addition, it has disadvantage on ability checks and saving throws against being {@condition blinded}.

trait.name: Water Breathing
entries: The gigantura can breathe only underwater.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gigantura uses Horrific Gaze. It then makes one Bite attack and two Tail Slap attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}25 ({@damage 3d12 + 6}) piercing damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw or be swallowed by the gigantura. A swallowed creature is {@condition blinded} and {@condition restrained}, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the gigantura, and it takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) acid damage at the start of each of the gigantura's turns. The gigantura can have up to two Huge, four Large, six Medium, or eight Small creatures swallowed at one time.

If the gigantura takes 30 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the gigantura must succeed on a {@dc 20} Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, which fall {@condition prone} in a space within 10 feet of the gigantura. If the gigantura dies, a swallowed creature is no longer {@condition restrained} by it and can escape from the corpse by using 20 feet of movement, exiting {@condition prone}.

action.name: Tail Slap
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Horrific Gaze
entries: The gigantura's telescoping eyes swirl disconcertingly in the direction of one creature it can see within 60 feet of it. If the target can see the gigantura, the target must succeed on a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn. A target that successfully saves is immune to this gigantura's Horrific Gaze for the next 24 hours.

trait.name: Acidic Coating
entries: A creature that touches the glacial crawler or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) acid damage.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The glacial crawler can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Snow Camouflage
entries: The glacial crawler has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in snowy terrain.

trait.name: Tunneler
entries: The glacial crawler can burrow through ice, snow, and permafrost, and leaves a 5-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The glacial crawler makes two Bite attacks and one Tail Spike attack, or it makes three Superheated Acid Spit attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) acid damage.

action.name: Tail Spike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. {@h}19 ({@damage 4d6 + 5}) piercing damage. The target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 17}) if it is a Large or smaller creature and the crawler doesn't have another creature {@condition grappled}.

action.name: Superheated Acid Spit
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 7} to hit, range 60 ft., one creature. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) acid damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) fire damage.

action.name: Acidic Spray {@recharge 5}
entries: The glacial crawler spews superheated digestive juices in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 18 ({@damage 4d8}) acid and 18 ({@damage 4d8}) fire damage and is coated in heated acid. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't coated in acid. A creature coated in heated acid takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) acid damage and 4 ({@damage 1d8}) fire damage at the start of each of its turns. A creature, including the coated target, can take its action to wash or scrub off the acid, ending the effect.

fly.condition: (must start and end its movement on a solid surface)

trait.name: Aura of Hope
entries: The gnyan is surrounded by an aura of uplifting courage. Each friendly creature within 30 feet of the gnyan has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}.

trait.name: Glorious Milk
entries: A gnyan can spend 1 minute slowly drinking from a bowl of melted ice water. When it stops, the bowl is filled with a pale milk. A creature that drinks the milk regains 7 ({@damage 2d6}) hit points, and its {@condition exhaustion} level is reduced by up to two levels. After a gnyan's milk has restored a total of 20 hit points or reduced a total of four {@condition exhaustion} levels in creatures, the gnyan can't create milk in this way again until it finishes a long rest.

trait.name: Pounce
entries: If the gnyan moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the gnyan can make one Bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Snow Camouflage
entries: The gnyan has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in snowy terrain.

trait.name: Snow Strider
entries: The gnyan can move across icy surfaces without needing to make an ability check, and difficult terrain composed of ice or snow doesn't cost it extra movement. In addition, it leaves no tracks or other traces of its passage when moving through snowy terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gnyan makes one Bite attack and one Claw attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) cold damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) slashing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) cold damage.

action.name: Avalanche's Roar {@recharge 6}
entries: The gnyan looses a terrible blasting roar of ice and snow in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) cold damage and is {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn as frost and snow coats its limbs. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition restrained}.

bonus.name: Snow Step
entries: The gnyan teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. The origin and destination spaces must contain snow.

monster.name: Goblin Siege Engine

immune.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with adamantine weapons

languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Adaptable Locomotion
entries: Moving through difficult terrain doesn't cost the siege engine extra movement. In addition, it has advantage on ability checks and saving throws made to escape a grapple or end the {@condition restrained} condition.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The siege engine doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Defensive Mount
entries: The siege engine can serve as a mount for one Large creature, up to four Medium creatures, or up to six Small or smaller creatures. While mounted, creatures riding in the siege engine's turret gain half cover.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The siege engine is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The siege engine has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The siege engine deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The siege engine makes three Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}22 ({@damage 3d10 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Acid Jet {@recharge 5}
entries: The siege engine sprays a jet of acid in a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw, taking 40 ({@damage 9d8}) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The godslayer doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Divine Sense
entries: The godslayer can pinpoint the location of Celestials, Fiends, divine avatars, and deities within 120 feet of it and can sense the general direction of such creatures within 1 mile of it. This sense extends into the Ethereal and Shadow Planes.

trait.name: Divine Slayer
entries: The godslayer's attacks affect immortal beings, such as gods. Celestials, Fiends, divine avatars, and deities don't have resistance to the damage from the godslayer's attacks. If such a creature would normally have immunity to the damage from the godslayer's attacks, it has resistance instead. If the godslayer reduces a Celestial, Fiend, divine avatar, or deity to 0 hp, it absorbs the target's divine energy, preventing the target from reviving or being resurrected until the godslayer is destroyed.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The godslayer is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Inscrutable
entries: The godslayer is immune to any effect that would sense its emotions or read its thoughts, as well as any divination spell that it refuses. Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks made to ascertain the godslayer's intentions or sincerity have disadvantage.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the godslayer fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The godslayer has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The godslayer deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The godslayer uses Arcane Lexicon. It then makes three Energy Glaive or Rune Discus attacks.

action.name: Energy Glaive
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 19} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}48 ({@damage 7d10 + 10}) force damage.

action.name: Rune Discus
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 19} to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}41 ({@damage 7d8 + 10}) force damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 25} Wisdom saving throw or spells and magical effects are suppressed on the target and the target can't cast spells for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Arcane Lexicon
entries: Glyphs on the godslayer's body cast ghostly copies into the air, forming into eldritch incantations. The godslayer chooses up to three creatures it can see within 90 feet of it, choosing one of the following options for each target. A creature can't be targeted by more than one effect at a time, and the godslayer can't use the same option on more than one target.

items.name: Death Glyph.
items.entry: The target must succeed on a {@dc 25} Wisdom saving throw or be marked for death until the start of the godslayer's next turn. While marked for death, the target takes an extra 11 ({@damage 2d10}) force damage each time the godslayer hits the target with an Energy Glaive attack.

items.name: Glyph of Despair.
items.entry: The target must succeed on a {@dc 25} Charisma saving throw or be overwhelmed with despair for 1 minute. While overwhelmed with despair, the target has disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.

items.name: Glyph of Pain.
items.entry: The target must succeed on a {@dc 25} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn as it is overwhelmed by pain. This glyph has no effect on Undead or Constructs.

items.name: Glyph of Summoning.
items.entry: The target must succeed on a {@dc 25} Wisdom saving throw or be magically teleported to an unoccupied space within 15 feet of the godslayer.

items.name: Retributive Glyph.
items.entry: The target must succeed on a {@dc 25} Dexterity saving throw or be marked with a retributive glyph until the end of its next turn. While marked, the creature takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) force damage each time it hits a creature with a weapon attack.

items.name: Stupefying Glyph.
items.entry: The target must succeed on a {@dc 25} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} and {@condition deafened} until the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Hunting Step
entries: The godslayer magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space within 15 feet of a Celestial, Fiend, divine avatar, or deity it senses with Divine Sense, magically shifting from the Material Plane to the Ethereal or Shadow Planes or vice versa. The godslayer has advantage on the next attack roll it makes against the target before the start of its next turn. Glowing glyphs appear at the origin and destination when it uses this bonus action.

reaction.name: Parry Spell
entries: If the godslayer succeeds on a saving throw against a spell of 8th level or lower that targets only the godslayer, the spell has no effect. If the godslayer succeeds on the saving throw by 5 or more, the spell is reflected back at the spellcaster, using the slot level, spell save DC, attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the caster.

legendary.name: Arcane Word
entries: The godslayer uses one glyph from its Arcane Lexicon on a target it can see within 90 feet of it.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The godslayer makes one Energy Glaive or Rune Discus attack.

legendary.name: Move
entries: The godslayer moves up to its speed without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

legendary.name: Rejuvenating Repair (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The godslayer regains 65 ({@damage 10d12}) hp.

trait.name: Cheek Worm Regrowth
entries: The gravid has twenty worms stored in its cheeks. Used worms regrow when the grivid finishes a long rest.

trait.name: Keen Sight
entries: The grivid has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

trait.name: Parasitic Cheek Worm
entries: The grivid produces parasitic worms in its cheeks, and it expels these worms into other creatures when it attacks. The worm burrows into the flesh of the target, and that creature is {@condition poisoned} while infested with at least one worm. At the start of each of the infested creature's turns, it takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) poison damage. Any creature can take an action to remove one worm with a successful {@dc 12} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check. An effect that cures disease removes and kills one worm infesting the creature. When a grivid dies, all worms currently infesting other creatures die with it, ending the infestation in all infested creatures.

trait.name: Worm Regeneration
entries: If it has at least 1 hp, the grivid regains 5 hp for each worm infesting another creature at the start of its turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The grivid makes one Bite attack and two Kicks attacks, or it makes three Spit Worm attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or be infested with a parasitic cheek worm (see the Parasitic Cheek Worm trait), if the grivid has at least 1 cheek worm.

action.name: Kick
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Spit Worm
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 7} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one creature. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or be infested with a parasitic cheek worm (see the Parasitic Cheek Worm trait), if the grivid has at least 1 cheek worm.

bonus.name: Consume Worms
entries: The grivid consumes up to 3 cheek worms, regaining 5 ({@damage 2d4}) hit points for each worm consumed.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The grolar bear has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The grolar bear makes one Bite attack and one Claw attack. If it hits one creature with both attacks, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or take 7 ({@damage 2d6}) bludgeoning damage and be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d8 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) slashing damage.

trait.name: Dismembering Strike
entries: If the grolar bear scores a critical hit against a {@condition stunned} creature, it tears off one of the creature's limbs. A creature is immune to this effect if it is immune to slashing damage.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The grolar bear has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Forest and Snow Camouflage
entries: The grolar bear has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in forest terrain while its fur is brown and snowy terrain while its fur is white.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The grolar bear makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks. If it hits one creature with two attacks, that creature takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) bludgeoning damage and must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 1d8 + 7}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d6 + 7}) slashing damage.

bonus.name: Versatile Coat
entries: The grolar bear changes its fur from white to brown or vice versa.

monster.name: Gryllus Swarm
languages: understands the languages of its creature but can't speak

trait.name: Book Bound
entries: The swarm's hp maximum is reduced by 1 for every minute it is further than 60 feet from its book. When its hp maximum reaches 0, it dies. If the swarm's book is destroyed or moved to another plane of existence separate from the swarm, the swarm must succeed on a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw or be reduced to 10 hp. The swarm can bind itself to a new book by spending a short rest in contact with the new book.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The swarm of gryllus doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The swarm is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: In the Margins
entries: The swarm can spend half its movement to enter or exit its book. While inside its book the swarm is indistinguishable from illustrations on a page.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny construct. The swarm can't regain hp or gain temporary hp.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The swarm of gryllus makes two Go for the Knees or Go for the Eyes attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Timber.

action.name: Go for the Knees
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm's space. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) slashing damage, or 8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) slashing damage if the swarm has half of its hp or fewer. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Go for the Eyes
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 7} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) piercing damage, or 8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hp or fewer. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Timber
entries: The swarm calls out in synchronous, tiny voices before chopping away at the feet of one creature in the swarm's space. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is knocked {@condition prone}, the swarm can then make one Go for the Knees attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The archaeopteryx doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks} when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The archaeopteryx has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The guardian archaeopteryx makes one Beak attack and one Talons attack, or it makes two Spit Thorn attacks. If the archaeopteryx hits one creature with two attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw or take 2 ({@damage 1d4}) poison damage and be {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d4 + 2}) piercing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) poison damage.

action.name: Talons
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d4 + 2}) slashing damage.

action.name: Spit Thorn
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) poison damage.

bonus.name: Imbue Poison
entries: The guardian archaeopteryx chooses a friendly creature it can see within 30 feet of it and imbues that creature's attacks with magical poison. The next time the friendly creature hits with an attack before the start of the guardian archaeopteryx's next turn, the target of the attack takes an extra 2 ({@damage 1d4}) poison damage and must succeed on a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn.
An enormous archaeopteryx, easily the size of a small dog, stares down from its high perch. Its plumage is a muted blue, and its eyes shine with intelligence.
Guardian archaeopteryxes are enormous feathered dinosaurs bred by ancient druids to guard sacred sites. Their glowing green eyes hint at the ancient magic that still suffuses their forms.

entries.name: Prehistoric Guardians
entries: In a time before writing, ancient druids infused {@creature archaeopteryx|CCodex} with magic and bred them to guard the druids' most sacred sites. The resulting creatures could spit enchanted thorns and poison foes beset by their lesser kin.

entries.name: Flock Leaders
entries: Guardian archaeopteryxes gather flocks of their smaller brethren to help perform their duties. The mundane {@creature archaeopteryx|CCodex} alert the guardians to intruders, scare off smaller creatures, and hunt for the shared nest, ensuring the flock grows strong. When a significant threat arrives, the guardian peppers foes with thorns and imbues the flock with magical poison.

entries.name: Made Obsolete by Time
entries: In the millennia since the guardians were first created, many of the sites they once guarded have been destroyed, profaned, or changed biome from the passage of time. Additionally, when a flock grows too large for a sacred site to support, the youngest guardian archaeopteryx leaves with a section of the flock, looking for a new site to guard. All of these processes create 'orphaned' flocks that guard ruins or verdant bits of forest, unrelated to their ancient purpose.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The gullkin can hold its breath for 15 minutes.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Javelin
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one creature. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Tempest Breath (Recharges on a Short or Long Rest)
entries: The gullkin exhales a lungful of air in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a {@dc 11} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from the gullkin.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The gullkin can hold its breath for 5 minutes.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gullkin hunter makes one Beak attack and one Shortsword attack or two Shortbow attacks.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Tempest Breath (Recharges on a Short or Long Rest)
entries: The gullkin hunter exhales a lungful of air in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a {@dc 12} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from the gullkin.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The troll can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The troll has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Psychoactive Sweat
entries: Any creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of the troll must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The troll regains 10 hp at the start of its turn. If the troll takes acid or fire damage, this trait doesn't function at the start of the troll's next turn. The troll dies only if it starts its turn with 0 hp and doesn't regenerate.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The troll makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks. It can replace its Bite attack with a Sticky Tongue attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Sticky Tongue
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 15 feet toward the troll.
A hunched figure climbs out of the ditch and creeps slowly down the street. It has a toad-like face, drab leathery skin covered in warts, and webbed feet. Its hands end in long, razor-sharp claws.

Standing just over six feet tall, gutter trolls are smaller, toad-like cousins of the common {@creature troll}. They make their homes in sewer tunnels beneath cities or under city bridges, creeping out at night to prey on the local population.

entries.name: Fey Origins
entries: The first gutter troll is said to have spawned from a {@creature troll} slain in battle with the {@creature Toad King|ToB2}, one of the fey animal lords. The troll took a big bite out of the {@creature Toad King|ToB2} before the animal lord and his servants took it down. The fey-touched flesh the {@creature troll} had swallowed changed when it regenerated, and it returned to life as a toad-like, wart-covered gutter troll. Like {@creature frog||frogs} and toads, gutter trolls cannot attack the {@creature Toad King|ToB2} if they encounter him, unless magically compelled.

entries.name: City Dwellers
entries: Although gutter trolls can still sometimes be found dwelling in swamps and bogs or near riverside villages, they are most common in cities and towns where they can find more plentiful food. Cowards at heart, the trolls avoid drawing unwanted attention from city authorities by hunting their prey in the worst parts of town, where beggars and street urchins regularly go missing.

entries.name: Sticky Tongues and Psychoactive Sweat
entries: Gutter trolls are stealthy hunters, emerging from ditches or sewers, grabbing victims with their long sticky tongues, and dragging their prey down into their lairs. Their warty skin secretes sweat with psychoactive properties, which weaken the victim's ability to fight back.

trait.name: Earth Climb
entries: The haakjir can climb difficult earth or stone surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Earth Glide
entries: The haakjir can burrow through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone. While doing so, the haakjir doesn't disturb the material it moves through.

trait.name: Earthen Claws
entries: The haakjir's claws easily meld through stone and metal. When the haakjir makes a Claw attack against a creature wearing nonmagical metal armor or wielding a nonmagical metal shield, the attack ignores the AC bonus provided by the armor or shield. If the target is a construct made of stone or metal, the attack ignores the AC bonus provided by the target's natural armor, if any.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The haakjir has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the haakjir's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Stone Camouflage
entries: The haakjir has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in rocky terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The haakjir makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, 5 ft reach, one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) slashing damage.

variant.name: Haakjir Variant: Vektyr
entries: It took several generations for the ancient deep gnomes to get a domesticated guardian creature for their enclaves. Vektyrs, early progenitors to the haakjir, were particularly vicious and unsociable. Vektyrs replace the haakjir's Pack Tactics trait with the following trait:

entries.name: Pounce
entries: If the vektyr moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the vektyr can make one Bite attack against it as a bonus action.

items.name: Pounce
entries: If the vektyr moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the vektyr can make one Bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Angelic Weapons
entries: Hala'ath's weapon attacks are magical. When Hala'ath hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 6d8} radiant damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Divine Assistance
entries: A creature of good alignment that starts its turn within 120 feet of Hala'ath can take the {@action Help} action as a bonus action.

trait.name: Divine Awareness
entries: Hala'ath knows if it hears a lie.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: Hala'ath doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks} when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: Hala'ath doesn't require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Hala'ath fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Hala'ath has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Spirit of Fortification
entries: While within 500 feet of Hala'ath, objects and structures have a damage threshold of 10. If an object or structure has a damage threshold higher than 10, its damage threshold is doubled instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Hala'ath makes three Titanium Wings or Bolt of Law attacks.

action.name: Titanium Wings
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) slashing damage plus 27 ({@damage 6d8}) radiant damage.

action.name: Bolt of Law
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 14} to hit, range 150 ft., one target. {@h}25 ({@damage 4d8 + 7}) radiant damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) thunder damage.

action.name: Righteous Blast {@recharge 5}
entries: Radiant energy erupts from a point Hala'ath can see within 150 feet of it. Each creature within 20 feet of that point must make a {@dc 23} Dexterity saving throw, taking 36 ({@damage 8d8}) radiant and 36 ({@damage 8d8}) thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Creatures of lawful or good alignment have advantage on the saving throw, and creatures of lawful good alignment are immune to the damage from Righteous Blast.

legendary.name: Move
entries: Hala'ath moves up to half its speed.

legendary.name: Summon Haladrons
entries: Hala'ath summons {@dice 2d4} {@creature haladron|ToB3|haladrons}. The {@creature haladron|ToB3|haladrons} appear in an unoccupied space within 150 feet of Hala'ath and act on its turn. Once summoned, the {@creature haladron|ToB3|haladrons} remain on the same plane as Hala'ath for 1 hour, until destroyed, or until Hala'ath dies. Hala'ath can have no more than 20 {@creature haladron|ToB3|haladrons} summoned in this way at one time.

legendary.name: Attack (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Hala'ath makes one Titanium Wings or Bolt of Law attack.

legendary.name: Glorious Surge (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: Hala'ath bolsters up to two creatures it can see within 60 feet of it. Each target regains 20 ({@damage 4d8 + 2}) hp and has advantage on its next attack roll, saving throw, or ability check.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The haladron doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks} when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: The haladron doesn't require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The haladron is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

action.name: Bolt of Law
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) radiant damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) thunder damage.

action.name: Stitch (3/Day)
entries: The haladron repairs a single break or tear in an object it touches, leaving no trace of the former damage. If the haladron uses this feature on a creature, the creature regains 3 ({@damage 1d6}) hp.

trait.name: Beast of Burden
entries: The harvest horse is considered one size larger for the purpose of determining its carrying capacity.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The harvest horse doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Poor Traversal
entries: The harvest horse must spend two additional feet of movement to move through difficult terrain instead of one additional foot.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The harvest horse deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d4 + 2}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Harvester's Stampede {@recharge 5}
entries: The harvest horse moves up to its speed in a straight line and can move through the space of any Large or smaller creature. The first time the harvest horse enters a creature's space during this move, that creature must make a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) slashing damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, it takes half as much damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}. When the harvest horse moves in this way, it doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

trait.name: Death Malison
entries: When the hellfire giant dies, the runes on its body flash a bright green then turn to mundane malachite. Each creature within 20 feet of the giant must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or be cursed for 7 days or until it is lifted by a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. While cursed, the creature has disadvantage on saving throws and on the first attack roll it makes on each of its turns. Fiends have disadvantage on the saving throw.

trait.name: Rune-Powered Weapons
entries: The hellfire giant's weapon attacks are magical. When the giant hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 2d8} fire damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Stone Camouflage
entries: The giant has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in rocky terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The giant makes two Greatclub or Runic Blast attacks.

action.name: Greatclub
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 3d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) fire damage.

action.name: Runic Blast
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 7} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}25 ({@damage 4d10 + 3}) force damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or its speed is halved until the end of its next turn. Fiends have disadvantage on the saving throw.

action.name: Invisibility Rune
entries: The hellfire giant magically turns {@condition invisible} until it attacks, or until its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the giant wears or carries is {@condition invisible} with it.

reaction.name: Runic Shield
entries: The hellfire giant adds 4 to its AC against one attack that would hit it as green runes encircle the giant. To do so, the giant must see the attacker and can't be {@condition invisible}.
Clad in a golden breastplate, a stone-skinned giant holds a ball of crackling green eldritch energy in the palm of its hand.

Hellfire giants were once {@creature stone giant||stone giants} who were enslaved by fiends. These powerful soldiers eventually rose against their masters and escaped their hellish prison. Hellfire giants now take brutal reprisals against anyone they deem allies to fiends.

entries.name: Rune Powered
entries: To empower their giant slaves, fiends etched malachite runes into the stony skin of the giants. Though these runes gave the giants new power, it also marked them with a hellish taint. Hellfire giants often take great pleasure in using their runic powers against fiends.

entries.name: Vengeful Hunters
entries: Feeling removed from their stone giant brethren, many hellfire giants have become wanderers who pursue lives as bounty hunters. However, even the most dedicated bounty hunting hellfire giant will abandon or delay its bounties if it believes it can strike at fiends.

entries.name: Fiendish Bloodline
entries: Much to the dismay of the first hellfire giants, the hellish traits and runes pass along to the giants' offspring. Seeing their power as a curse, many of the surviving giants choose not to have children, while others teach their children the importance of utilizing the fell power for the good of giantkind. Most hellfire giants have mixed opinions on {@race tiefling||tieflings}. Some despise them, viewing them as the fell children of their former masters, while others feel a kinship with the {@race tiefling||tieflings}. After all, like the giants, they were altered against their will by the machinations of fiends.

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with silvered weapons

trait.name: Insanity
entries: The hellforged has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

trait.name: Hellfire Weapons
entries: The hellforged's weapon attacks are magical. When the hellforged hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 2d6} fire damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the hellforged has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hellforged makes three Battleaxe or Hurl Hellfire attacks.

action.name: Battleaxe
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) slashing damage, or 8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) slashing damage if used with two hands, plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Hurl Hellfire
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 3d6 + 2}) fire damage. If the target is a creature or a flammable object, it ignites. Until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, the target takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

reaction.name: Voice of Authority {@recharge 5}
entries: When a creature hits the hellforged with an attack, the hellforged shrieks a one-word command. The attacker must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or carry out this command on its next turn. This reaction works like the {@spell command} spell, except the attacker doesn't have to understand the hellforged's language. The hellforged must see the attacker and be able to speak to use this reaction.

trait.name: Herd-Hidden (Herd Animal Form Only)
entries: The herd skulker has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) and Charisma ({@skill Deception}) checks to blend into the herd or convince those observing the herd that it is part of the herd.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The herd skulker has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: One of the Herd
entries: A domesticated herd animal, such as a cow, horse, sheep, or chicken, that can see the herd skulker treats it as a member of the herd, regardless of the form the skulker takes. When such an animal sees a herd skulker attack or feed, it becomes immune to the herd skulker's One of the Herd for the next 24 hours, acting as it normally would when confronting a predator. Creatures immune to the {@condition charmed} condition are immune to this trait.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The herd skulker transforms into a Large or smaller domesticated herd animal it can see, such as a cow, horse, sheep, or chicken, or back into its true, canine form. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

bonus.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The herd skulker takes the {@action Disengage} or {@action Hide} action.

trait.name: Aura of Misfortune
entries: Each creature within 15 feet of the hinderling treats any roll of a 20 on an attack roll as a 19 and can't gain the benefit of advantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws.

trait.name: Rejuvenation
entries: While the hinderling curse remains on a victim, a slain hinderling returns to life in 1 day, regaining all its hp and becoming active again. The hinderling appears within 100 feet of the victim.

action.name: Stolen Belonging
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Hurl Stolen Belonging
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Mad Dash
entries: The hinderling moves up to twice its speed and can move through the space of any creature that is Medium or larger. When the hinderling moves through a creature's space, the creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or fall {@condition prone}. This movement doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

bonus.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The hinderling takes the {@action Disengage} or {@action Hide} action.

reaction.name: Run and Hide
entries: When a creature the hinderling can see targets it with a weapon attack, the hinderling chooses another creature within 5 feet it to become the target of the attack, then it moves up to half its speed without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The hippopotamus can hold its breath for 10 minutes.

action.name: Gore
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Thunderous Bray {@recharge 5}
entries: The hippopotamus emits a resounding bray in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) thunder damage and is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

monster.name: Hirudine Stalker
languages: telepathy 120 ft. (only with other hirudine stalkers)

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The hirudine stalker can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Blood Scent
entries: The hirudine stalker has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) and Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks to find or track a creature that doesn't have all its hp.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hirudine stalker makes two Bite or Spit Tooth attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) necrotic damage, and the hirudine stalker regains hp equal to the necrotic damage dealt.

action.name: Spit Tooth
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or suffer one of the following effects (the hirudine stalker's choice): Anesthetic. On a failed save, the target is unaware of any damage it takes from the hirudine stalker's Spit Tooth attack for 1 hour, but it is still aware of other sources of damage.

action.name: Debilitating
entries: On a failed save, the target is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn. If the target fails the saving throw by 5 or more, it also suffers one level of {@condition exhaustion}. A creature can't suffer more than three levels of {@condition exhaustion} from this attack.

action.name: Magebane
entries: On a failed save, the target has disadvantage on Constitution saving throws to maintain its concentration for 1 hour. If the target fails the saving throw by 5 or more, it also loses its lowest-level available spell slot.

bonus.name: Hidden Predator
entries: The hirudine stalker takes the {@action Hide} action.

monster.name: Howler of the Hill
languages: and Void Speech but can't speak

trait.name: Hungry Void Traveler
entries: The howler of the hill doesn't require air, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Inscrutable
entries: The howler is immune to any effect that would sense its emotions or read its thoughts, as well as any divination spell that it refuses. Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks made to ascertain the howler's intentions or sincerity have disadvantage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The howler of the hill uses Gloaming Howl. It then makes three Claw or Psychic Bolt attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) slashing damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) psychic damage.

action.name: Psychic Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 9} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}26 ({@damage 5d8 + 4}) psychic damage.

action.name: Gloaming Howl
entries: The howler emits a magical howl that changes in melody and frequency, depending on the light surrounding the howler. Each creature of the howler's choice that is within 120 feet of it and can hear the howl must succeed on a {@dc 18} Wisdom saving throw or succumb to the effects of one of the following howls. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the howl's effect ends for it, the creature is immune to that particular howl for the next 24 hours.

items.name: Bright Howl.
items.entry: When the howler is in bright light, each target that fails the saving throw is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Dim Howl.
items.entry: When the howler is in dim light, each target that fails the saving throw is {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Dark Howl.
items.entry: When the howler is in darkness, each target that fails the saving throw drops whatever it is holding and is {@condition paralyzed} with fear for 1 minute. A {@condition paralyzed} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Otherworldly Hunter
entries: The howler can transport itself to a different plane of existence. This works like the {@spell plane shift} spell, except the howler can affect only itself and can't use this action to banish an unwilling creature to another plane.

trait.name: Overpowering Stench
entries: Each creature other than an alliumite or garlicle within 5 feet of the alliumite when it takes damage must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn. On a successful saving throw, the creature has advantage against the Overpowering Stench of all alliumites for 1 minute.

trait.name: Plant Camouflage
entries: The alliumite has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks it makes in any terrain with ample obscuring plant life.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The husker alliumite makes two Gnarly Club attacks, or it makes one Gnarly Club attack and uses Taunting Threat.

action.name: Gnarly Club
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Taunting Threat
entries: The husker throws a series of rude and menacing gestures at one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must make a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the target takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) psychic damage and has disadvantage on all attacks not made against the husker until the end of its next turn. On a success, the target takes half the damage and doesn't have disadvantage on attacks not made against the husker.

trait.name: Echolocation (Whale Form Only)
entries: The hvalfiskr can't use its {@sense blindsight} while {@condition deafened}.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The hvalfiskr can hold its breath for 1 hour.

trait.name: Speak with Cetaceans
entries: The hvalfiskr can communicate with dolphins, porpoises, and whales as if they shared a language.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hvalfiskr makes four Bite or Slam attacks, or it makes three Chained Anchor attacks.

action.name: Bite (Whale Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}23 ({@damage 5d6 + 6}) piercing damage.

action.name: Chained Anchor (Giant or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 15 ft. or range 30/60 ft., one target. {@h}28 ({@damage 5d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage, and the anchor hooks around the target. While hooked by the anchor, the target and the hvalfiskr can't move further than 60 feet from each other. A creature, including the target, can take its action to detach the anchor by succeeding on a {@dc 19} Strength check. Alternatively, the anchor's chain can be attacked and destroyed (AC 18; hp 50; vulnerability to thunder damage; immunity to piercing, poison, and psychic damage), dislodging the anchor into an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target and preventing the hvalfiskr from using Reel Anchor.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}24 ({@damage 4d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Whale Song {@recharge 5}
entries: The hvalfiskr sings a magical melody. Each hostile creature within 60 feet of the hvalfiskr that can hear the song must make a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw.
