On a failure, a creature takes 45 ({@damage 10d8}) psychic damage and is {@condition charmed} for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition charmed}. A {@condition charmed} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature fails the saving throw by 5 or more, it also suffers {@table short-term madness}.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The hvalfiskr transforms into a Huge frost giant, a Huge whale, or back into its true form, which is a whale-giant hybrid. Its statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

bonus.name: Reel Anchor
entries: The hvalfiskr pulls on the chain connected to its anchor, returning the anchor to its empty hand. If the anchor is hooked around a creature, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 19} Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 30 feet toward the hvalfiskr.

trait.name: Fiery Blood
entries: When the ibexian takes damage, each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage. The fire ignites flammable objects within 5 feet of the ibexian that aren't being worn or carried.

trait.name: Fiery Charge
entries: If the ibexian moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a Ram attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 10 feet away and knocked {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: An ibexian has advantage on attack rolls if at least one of the ibexian's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ibexian makes one Ram attack and one Hooves attack, or it makes two Spit Fire attacks.

action.name: Ram
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Hooves
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Spit Fire
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 4d6 + 2}) fire damage.

action.name: Pyroclasm (1/Day)
entries: The ibexian moves up to 30 feet in a straight line toward a creature and can move through the space of any Medium or smaller creature, stopping when it moves within 5 feet of the target. Each friendly ibexian within 50 feet of the ibexian can use its reaction to also move up to its speed in a straight line toward the target, stopping when it moves within 5 feet of the target. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

The target and each creature within 20 feet of it must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. For each ibexian in the Pyroclasm after the first, the fire damage increases by 3 ({@damage 1d6}), to a maximum of 28 ({@dice 8d6}).

immune.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with adamantine weapons

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The ice golem doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Ice Cavity
entries: The ice golem's torso is a block of ice that it can liquefy and refreeze, trapping and preserving creatures inside it. If the golem takes 15 fire damage or more on a single turn, the cavity liquefies if it was frozen.
When the cavity is frozen, a creature that touches the golem or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) cold damage.

When the cavity is liquefied, a creature within 5 feet of the golem can take an action to pull a {@condition petrified} creature out of the golem, if the golem has one inside it. Doing so requires a successful {@dc 16} Strength check, and the creature making the attempt takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) cold damage.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The ice golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The ice golem has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ice golem makes two Slam attacks, or it makes one Slam attack and uses Preserve Creature.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 3d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) cold damage. The target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 16}) if it is a Medium or smaller creature and the golem doesn't have another creature {@condition grappled}.

action.name: Preserve Creature
entries: The ice golem preserves a Medium or smaller creature {@condition grappled} by it. The preserved creature can't breathe and is {@condition restrained} as it starts to freeze. The {@condition restrained} creature must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw at the end of its next turn. On a failure, it takes 18 ({@damage 4d8}) cold damage, becomes {@condition petrified} in ice in the golem's Ice Cavity, and has total cover from attacks and other effects outside the golem. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 hp, the creature automatically becomes stable. A {@condition petrified} creature removed from the golem's Ice Cavity thaws, ending the petrification, in {@dice 1d4} rounds or immediately after taking fire damage. On a success, the creature takes half the damage and is ejected, landing {@condition prone} in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the golem. If the golem moves, the preserved creature moves with it. The golem can have only one creature preserved at a time. The golem can't use Preserve Creature if its Ice Cavity is frozen.

reaction.name: Freeze or Liquefy Cavity
entries: The ice golem freezes or liquefies its Ice Cavity.
This barrel-chested humanoid has metal straps leading from its shoulders to legs made from the same metal. The metal in its torso holds a block of ice in place.
Created to incapacitate and capture creatures on behalf of its controller, an ice golem follows the target assigned to it until it captures its quarry and returns its controller.

entries.name: Icy Hunters
entries: Ice golems operate best in cold climates or during winter. While their creation allows them to withstand some heat, they can't carry out their tasks effectively in constant temperatures above 100 degrees Fahrenheit.

entries.name: Rogue Golems
entries: Uncontrolled ice golems continue following their main directive, but they focus on capturing whenever they have an empty chest cavity. These golems kidnap their victims and deposit their captives relatively unharmed in a location they have declared as their home bases. Fortunately, the golems have no concept of concealing their movement, making them and their targets easy to track.

trait.name: Fear of Fire
entries: An ice urchin is inherently fearful of fire. If presented forcefully with fire, or if it is dealt fire damage, the ice urchin must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} until the end of its next turn. Once it has been {@condition frightened} by a specific source of fire (such as a torch), the ice urchin can't be {@condition frightened} by that same source again for 24 hours.

trait.name: Ice Slide
entries: The ice urchin's speed increases to 60 feet when it moves on snow or ice. Additionally, difficult terrain composed of ice or snow doesn't cost it extra movement.

trait.name: Melting Away
entries: If the ice urchin takes fire damage, its AC is reduced by 2, and it has disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Spiny Defense
entries: A creature that touches the ice urchin or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) piercing and 2 ({@damage 1d4}) cold damage.

trait.name: Venomous Spine Regrowth
entries: An ice urchin has twelve venomous spines. Used spines regrow when the ice urchin finishes a long rest.

action.name: Ice Spine
entries: {@atk mw} +4, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) piercing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) cold damage

action.name: Venomous Spine
entries: {@atk rw} +3, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) piercing plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) cold damage and 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the ice willow remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ice-covered willow tree.

trait.name: Ice Melt
entries: If the ice willow takes fire damage, its icicles partially melt. Until the end of the ice willow's next turn, creatures have advantage on saving throws against the willow's Icicle Drop and Icicle Spray.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ice willow makes two Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) bludgeoning plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) cold damage.

action.name: Icicle Drop {@recharge 4}
entries: The ice willow shakes several spear-like icicles loose. Each creature within 10 feet of the willow must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 9 ({@damage 2d8}) piercing damage and 7 ({@damage 2d6}) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Icicle Spray {@recharge 6}
entries: The ice willow flings icicles in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 18 ({@damage 4d8}) piercing damage and 14 ({@damage 4d6}) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

reaction.name: Melting Icicles
entries: When the ice willow takes fire damage, it can immediately use Icicle Drop, if available.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The iceworm can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Cold Physiology
entries: The iceworm can't abide constant warmth. Each hour it spends in an area with a temperature above 40 degrees Fahrenheit, the ice worm must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or suffer one level of {@condition exhaustion} that can't be removed until it finishes a long rest in an area with a temperature below 40 degrees.

trait.name: Heat Sensitivity
entries: The iceworm has disadvantage on attack rolls when within 5 feet of a strong source of heat that isn't a warm‑blooded creature's natural warmth, such as a torch or campfire. In addition, the iceworm can pinpoint the location of warm-blooded creatures within 90 feet of it and can sense the general direction of such creatures within 1 mile of it.

trait.name: Slippery
entries: The iceworm has advantage on saving throws and ability checks made to escape a grapple.

action.name: Icy Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) cold damage.

action.name: Icy Spit
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one creature. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) cold damage, and the target is coated in freezing, viscous slime. While coated in the freezing slime, a creature's speed is reduced by 10 feet, and it has disadvantage on the first attack roll it makes on each of its turns. A creature, including the target, can take its action to clean off the slime, ending the effect.

monster.name: Iilari'jil, the Fire of Forgotten Hope

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Iilari'jil casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 21}):

trait.name: Angelic Weapons
entries: Iilari'jil's weapon attacks are magical. When Iilari'jil hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 6d8} radiant damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Divine Awareness
entries: Iilari'jil knows if it hears a lie.

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: Iilari'jil doesn't require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Iilari'jil fails a saving throw, they can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Iilari'jil has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: Iilari'jil deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Iliari'jil makes three Radiant Fists or Radiant Blasts attacks.

action.name: Radiant Fists
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 15} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}25 ({@damage 5d6 + 8}) bludgeoning damage plus 27 ({@damage 6d8}) radiant damage.

action.name: Radiant Blast
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 13} to hit, range 150 ft., one target. {@h}28 ({@damage 5d8 + 6}) radiant damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Turn the Tide {@recharge 6}
entries: A carpet of golden flame erupts from the ground on a point Iilari'jil can see within 120 feet of them. Each hostile creature within 20 feet of the point must make a {@dc 21} Dexterity saving throw, taking 56 ({@damage 16d6}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Each friendly creature within 20 feet of the point regains 28 ({@damage 8d6}) hp.

action.name: Rouse to Action (5/Day)
entries: Iilari'jil inspires up to 4 creatures they can see within 60 feet of them. For 1 minute, whenever a target makes an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, it can add a d6 to the roll, provided it can see or hear Iilari'jil. A creature can benefit from a Rouse to Action die only once each turn.

legendaryHeader: Iilari'jil can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Iilari'jil regains spent legendary actions at the start of their turn.

legendary.name: Fly
entries: Iilari'jil flies up to half their flying speed without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 2 Action)
entries: Iilari'jil uses Spellcasting.

legendary.name: Celestial Shield (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Iilari'jil creates a shield of radiance around a creature of their choice within 30 feet of them. The target's AC increases by 4 until the start of Iilari'jil's next turn.

legendary.name: Radiant Fists (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Iilari'jil makes one Radiant Fists attack.

legendary.name: Restore Vigor (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: Iilari'jil ends the {@condition blinded}, {@condition charmed}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition paralyzed}, {@condition petrified}, or {@condition poisoned} conditions on all creatures of their choice within 15 feet of them.

legendary.name: Revive (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: Iilari'jil returns a creature they can see within 30 feet of them to life with 10 ({@damage 3d6}) hp, provided the target has been dead no longer than 1 minute.

legendaryGroup.name: Iilari'jil, the Fire of Forgotten Hope

_monsterFluff.name: Iilari'jil, the Fire of Forgotten Hope

trait.name: Essential Oiliness
entries: The imperator has advantage on saving throws and ability checks made to escape a grapple or end the {@condition restrained} condition.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The imperator can hold its breath for 1 hour.

trait.name: Penguin Telepathy
entries: The imperator can magically command any penguin within 120 feet of it, using a limited telepathy.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The imperator deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The imperator makes one Beak attack and two Wing Slap attacks.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}24 ({@damage 3d12 + 5}) piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw or be swallowed by the imperator. A swallowed creature is {@condition blinded} and {@condition restrained}, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the imperator, and it takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) acid damage at the start of each of the imperator's turns.

If the imperator takes 30 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the imperator must succeed on a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, which fall {@condition prone} in a space within 10 feet of the imperator. If the imperator dies, a swallowed creature is no longer {@condition restrained} by it and can escape from the corpse by using 15 feet of movement, exiting {@condition prone}.

action.name: Wing Slap
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Pelagic Bile {@recharge 6}
entries: The imperator regurgitates its stomach contents in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 17 ({@damage 5d6}) acid damage and 17 ({@damage 5d6}) poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition poisoned}. A {@condition poisoned} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Swallowed creatures are then regurgitated, falling {@condition prone} in a space within 10 feet of the imperator.

action.name: Toboggan Charge {@recharge 5}
entries: The imperator moves up to 30 feet in a straight line over ice or snow and can move through the space of any Large or smaller creature. The first time it enters a creature's space during this move, that creature must make a {@dc 17} Strength saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 36 ({@damage 8d8}) bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}.

reaction.name: Muster the Legions (1/Day)
entries: When the imperator is reduced to half its hp or lower, it magically calls {@dice 1d4} swarms of penguins. The penguins arrive on initiative count 20 of the next round, acting as allies of the vampire and obeying its telepathic commands. The penguins remain for 1 hour, until the imperator dies, or until the imperator dismisses them as a bonus action.

trait.name: Aqueous Form
entries: The incandescent one can enter a hostile creature's space and stop there. It can move through a space as narrow as 1 foot wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The incandescent one can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The incandescent one doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks} when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: The incandescent one doesn't require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Luminous
entries: The incandescent one sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The incandescent one has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The incandescent one's weapon attacks are magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The incandescent one makes two Slam attacks or three Astral Bolt attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) cold damage.

action.name: Astral Bolt.
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 8} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d6 + 4}) radiant damage, and the next attack roll made against the target before the end of the incandescent one's next turn has advantage.

bonus.name: Celestial Inspiration
entries: The incandescent one inspires one creature it can see within 60 feet of it. Whenever the target makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the start of the incandescent one's next turn, the target can roll a {@dice d4} and add the number rolled to the attack roll or saving throw.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The inciter demon can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Aura of Distrust
entries: The inciter demon radiates a psychic aura that causes creatures to doubt the intentions of their friends. Each creature within 30 feet of the inciter that isn't a Fiend has disadvantage on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks. When a creature enters the aura's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that creature must make a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw. On a failure, any spell cast by the creature can't affect allies or friendly creatures, including spells already in effect, such as bless, while it remains in the aura. In addition, the creature can't use the {@action Help} action or accept assistance from a friendly creature, including assistance from spells, such as bless or cure wounds, while it remains in the aura.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The inciter demon makes two Claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Inspire Violence {@recharge 5}
entries: The inciter demon whispers to a creature within 5 feet of it that can hear it, stoking paranoia and hatred. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw or it uses its next action to make one attack against a creature that both the target and the inciter demon can see. A creature immune to being {@condition charmed} isn't affected by the inciter demon's Inspire Violence.

bonus.name: Invisibility
entries: The inciter demon magically turns {@condition invisible} until it attacks, or until its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the inciter wears or carries is {@condition invisible} with it.
A humanoid wisp of flame and smoke briefly appears and then vanishes again. It slips through the gathered assembly, sowing discord and inspiring violence with its hissing words.

Inciter demons are the embodiment of malicious mischief. They are wicked fiends whose only love is to disrupt the social ties between other creatures. Their whispers spoil trust and goodwill, and domineering mages employ them to ruin important alliances and turn former friends toward infighting and hostility.

entries.name: Flame Form
entries: An inciter demon's true form is that of a slender humanoid figure of cold flames and smoke. The only sign that an inciter is to blame for a breakdown in diplomacy is the flicker of fire that appears before they vanish, as they retreat to delight in what they have wrought.

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with silvered weapons

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The infernal tutor casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 18}):

trait.name: Devil's Sight
entries: Magical darkness doesn't impede the infernal tutor's {@sense darkvision}.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The infernal tutor has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Phrenic Weapons
entries: The infernal tutor's weapon attacks are magical. When the tutor hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 4d6} psychic damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Weaken Resolve
entries: The infernal tutor's speech is laced with subtle enchantments that make its words seem logical or profound, regardless of the actual words used. It has advantage on Charisma ({@skill Deception}) and Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks against Humanoids.

In addition, if the infernal tutor spends at least 1 minute conversing with a Humanoid, that creature has disadvantage on saving throws against the infernal tutor's Fiendish Indoctrination and against enchantment spells the tutor casts.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The infernal tutor makes three Claw or Tutor's Baton attacks, or it makes one Claw attack and two Tutor's Baton attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Claw (True Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) slashing damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) psychic damage.

action.name: Tutor's Baton
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) psychic damage. Instead of dealing psychic damage, the infernal tutor can cause the target to become {@condition incapacitated} until the end of the target's next turn.

action.name: Fiendish Indoctrination {@recharge 5}
entries: The infernal tutor speaks fiendish ideals couched in persuasive, scholarly language. Each creature within 30 feet of the tutor must make a {@dc 18} Charisma saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 45 ({@damage 13d6}) psychic damage and is {@condition charmed} for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition charmed}. A {@condition charmed} creature isn't under the tutor's control, but it regards the tutor as a trusted friend, taking the tutor's requests or actions in the most favorable way it can. A {@condition charmed} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

A creature that fails the saving throw by 5 or more is {@condition charmed} for 1 day instead. Such a creature can repeat the saving throw only when it suffers harm or receives a suicidal command, ending the effect on a success.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The infernal tutor magically transforms into a Small or Medium Humanoid, or back into its true, fiendish form. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

reaction.name: Strength of Character
entries: When the infernal tutor succeeds on a saving throw, the tutor responds with a scathing magical insult, if the source of the effect is a creature within 60 feet of the tutor. That creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Wisdom saving throw or take 14 ({@damage 4d6}) psychic damage and have disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against a spell cast by the infernal tutor.

trait.name: Evasion
entries: If the infested duelist is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, the duelist instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half the damage if it fails.

trait.name: Parasite Sense
entries: The infested duelist has advantage on attack rolls against {@condition poisoned} creatures.

trait.name: Plant-Powered Mobility
entries: {@action opportunity attack||Opportunity attacks} made against an infested duelist have disadvantage. If the duelist is {@condition prone} at the start of its turn, it can immediately stand without costing movement.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The infested duelist makes one Slam attack and two Shortsword attacks, or it makes two Thorn Shot attacks.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d6 + 6}) piercing damage.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the target takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. On a success, the target takes half the damage and isn't {@condition poisoned}. The {@condition poisoned} target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Thorn Shot
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d4 + 5}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

reaction.name: Spore Cloud
entries: When the duelist takes damage from a ranged attack, it emits a burst of thick, yellow spores. The spores surround the infested duelist and heavily obscure the area within 5 feet of it. The spores dissipate at the start of the duelist's next turn.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The infiltrator casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}):

trait.name: Knowledge Charm
entries: The infiltrator carries a minor magical charm gifted to it by the organization. While wearing or carrying the charm, the infiltrator has proficiency in any two skills and is fluent in any one language (not included above). This proficiency and fluency last until the charm is lost or destroyed or until the infiltrator performs a 10-minute ritual to change the proficiency and fluency provided by the charm. If the infiltrator dies, the charm becomes nonmagical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The infiltrator makes two Shortsword attacks and one Dagger attack. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Light Crossbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Cunning Action
entries: The infiltrator takes the {@action Dash}, {@action Disengage}, or {@action Hide} action.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The ion slime can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Ionic Form
entries: Whenever the ion slime is subjected to lightning damage, it takes no damage and instead regains hp equal to the lightning damage dealt. The slime's Supercharge action then recharges. If the ooze takes cold damage while supercharged, it must roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1 or 2, it loses the supercharged state.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The ion slime doesn't require sleep.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The ion slime can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ion slime makes two Pseudopod attacks.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) bludgeoning plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) lightning damage.

action.name: Discharge (Supercharged Only)
entries: The ion slime sends a shock of lightning through its supercharged pseudopod at one creature it can see within 5 feet of it, ending the supercharged state. The target must make {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking 27 ({@damage 6d8}) lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Three bolts of lightning then leap to as many as three targets, each of which must be within 20 feet of the first target. A target can be a creature or an object and each can be targeted by only one of the bolts. Each target must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking 13 ({@damage 3d8}) lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Charged Motion (Supercharged Only)
entries: The ion slime takes the {@action Dash} or {@action Dodge} action.

bonus.name: Supercharge {@recharge 5}
entries: The ion slime gathers ambient electricity to supercharge itself for 3 rounds. While supercharged, the ion slime gains a +2 bonus to its AC, and it gains an additional action on each of its turns. At the end of the third round, if the slime hasn't used the Discharge action, it suffers feedback, taking 18 ({@damage 4d8}) force damage. Its supercharged state then ends.

monster.name: Iorvensiav, Lady of the Unforgiving Cold

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with silvered weapons

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: Iorvensiav can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Death Throes
entries: When Iorvensiav dies, she explodes, and each creature within 30 feet of her must make a {@dc 21} Constitution saving throw, taking 70 ({@damage 20d6}) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The explosion freezes objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried, encapsulating them in ice.

trait.name: Freezing Weapons
entries: Iorvensiav's weapon attacks are magical. When she hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 2d6} cold damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Iorvensiav fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Iorvensiav has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Iorvensiav makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks. She can replace one Claw attack with a Freezing Chain attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}20 ({@damage 2d10 + 9}) piercing damage and 7 ({@damage 2d6}) cold damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d6 + 9}) slashing damage and 7 ({@damage 2d6}) cold damage.

action.name: Freezing Chain
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d6 + 9}) slashing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) cold damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 21}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained} and takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) cold damage at the start of each of its turns, and Iorvensiav can't make Freezing Chain attacks against other targets.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d8 + 9}) slashing damage and 7 ({@damage 2d6}) cold damage. This attack ignores resistance to slashing damage, and if Iorvensiav rolls a 20 on the attack roll, she cuts off one of the target's heads. The target dies if it can't survive without the lost head. If the target doesn't have a head or can't be decapitated, it takes an extra 27 ({@damage 6d6}) slashing damage instead.

action.name: Thundering Ice Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: Iorvensiav exhales a booming, frigid spray in a 90-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 21} Constitution saving throw, taking 45 ({@damage 8d10}) cold damage and 45 ({@damage 8d10}) thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that fails the saving throw by 5 or more is {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: Iorvensiav magically transforms into any Beast, Fiend, or Humanoid that is Huge or smaller or back into her true form. Her statistics, other than her size, are the same in each form. Any equipment she is wearing or carrying transforms with her or is borne by the new form (Iorvensiav's choice). She reverts to her true form if she dies.

legendaryHeader: Iorvensiav can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Iorvensiav regains spent legendary actions at the start of her turn.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: Iorvensiav makes a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check.

legendary.name: Tail Attack
entries: Iorvensiav makes a tail attack.

legendary.name: Chilling Wings (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Iorvensiav beats her wings, releasing a blast of icy wind. Each creature within 15 feet of her must succeed on a {@dc 21} Constitution saving throw or take 14 ({@damage 4d6}) cold damage and be {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn. Iorvensiav can then fly up to half her flying speed.

legendaryGroup.name: Iorvensiav, Lady of the Unforgiving Cold

entries: This serpentine fiend sprouts four pairs of limbs along her length, and horns of faceted ice adorn her dragon-like head. When moving, her scales scintillate in patterns of frostbite black, glacier blue, and snowy white. A glittering diamond point marks the end of her thrashing tail, and a spiked chain of frosty steel hangs from her clawed hands.

Iorvensiav, Lady of the Unforgiving Cold, is master of the frozen hells and queen of frigid deaths. In those infernal places where fires do not burn, the cold is so terrible it can set mortal bones and flesh ablaze.

entries.name: Last of Her Kind
entries: Even Iorvensiav no longer recalls her origins. Some in the hells whisper she was once a dragon, tortured and reshaped by her own request to become the frigid power she is today. Others say she is the last of a species that once roamed the frozen wildlands deep within various hellscapes, the others having long since turned to dust.

entries.name: Cold and Calculating
entries: Iorvensiav often holds herself aloof, watching from a distance and gauging the odds of her opportunities. She watches for any options that give her a firmer hold on her growing power in the hells. The chill she wears like a cloak is only a mask, however. Inside, the Lady of Unforgiving Cold is a raging inferno, capable of sudden violence when she is displeased.

entries.name: Her Shiny Hoard
entries: Similar to dragons, Iorvensiav keeps a hoard. Instead of coins, jewels, and other wealth, she gathers frozen souls to her lair. These souls are strewn throughout her mountain kingdom as frozen carvings, depicting how the creatures appeared in life.

entries.name: Children of Iorvensiav
entries: Throughout history, Iorvensiav has spread her influence amongst mortals. Those gifted with her power or bloodline produce descendants with fiendish traits. The skin of these tieflings is bluish-white like ice or blackish-purple like frostbite, and their horns are always crystalline, as if they were sculpted from ice. The tieflings may have patches of scales or be scaled from head to toe. To create a {@race tiefling (iorvensiav)|ToB3|tiefling} character from Iorvensiav's lineage, replace the base {@race tiefling||tiefling's} Hellish Resistance and Infernal Legacy traits with the following traits:

entries.name: Frozen Resistance
entries: You have resistance to cold damage.

entries.name: Iorvensiav's Legacy
entries: You know the {@spell message} cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the {@spell hellish rebuke} spell, dealing cold damage in place of fire damage, as a 2nd‑level spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the {@spell alter self} spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Iorvensiav in Midgard
entries: Iorvensiav rules a portion of the plane known as Efrizarr. Other arch-devils that share the realm with her regard her as an ally at best or competition at worst. Some view her as a pet who was let off its leash. The latter are the fiends Iorvensiav works hardest to bring down, crushing them beneath her machinations.

entries.name: Iorvensiav's Lair
entries: A mountain of ice rises up from a hellish plain of frost. Within the mountain, at the center of a fiend-riddled labyrinth, a glimmering, cylindrical chamber of cyclopean proportions reaches from the mountain's depths to its peak. Iorvensiav resides in this chamber, hoarding souls as she grows in power.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The jeholopterus doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks} when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Keen Sight
entries: The jeholopterus has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Blood Drain
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) piercing damage, and the jeholopterus attaches to the target. While attached, the jeholopterus doesn't attack. Instead, at the start of each of the jeholopterus's turns, the target loses 8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) hp due to blood loss.

The jeholopterus can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement. It does so after it drains 15 hp of blood from the target or the target dies. A creature, including the target, can take its action to detach the jeholopterus by succeeding on a {@dc 13} Strength check.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) slashing damage.

variant.name: Jeholopterus Familiars
entries: Some jeholopterus are willing to serve spellcasters as a familiar. Such jeholopterus have the following trait.

entries.name: Familiar
entries: The jeholopterus can serve another creature as a familiar, forming a magic, telepathic bond with that willing companion. While the two are bonded, the companion can sense what the jeholopterus senses as long as they are within 1 mile of each other. While the jeholopterus is within 10 feet of its companion, the companion shares the jeholopterus's Keen Sight trait. At any time and for any reason, the jeholopterus can end its service as a familiar, ending the telepathic bond.
This small flying creature is covered in hair-like fibers, and its mouth resembles that of a rattlesnake.

The jeholopterus is an acrobatic flying reptile about the size of a goose that dwells in warm swamps and jungles and along tropical coastlines. It feasts on insects, especially those attracted to bloody wounds on other creatures. A particularly hungry jeholopterus might take this a step further by making its own fresh wounds. After landing on a suitable target and securing itself with its hand and foot claws, the jeholopterus latches on with its fangs. It then uses its plier-like jaws to squeeze the wound and keep the blood flowing to attract insects.

items.name: Familiar
entries: The jeholopterus can serve another creature as a familiar, forming a magic, telepathic bond with that willing companion. While the two are bonded, the companion can sense what the jeholopterus senses as long as they are within 1 mile of each other. While the jeholopterus is within 10 feet of its companion, the companion shares the jeholopterus's Keen Sight trait. At any time and for any reason, the jeholopterus can end its service as a familiar, ending the telepathic bond.

trait.name: Shrill Screech
entries: When agitated, the jubjub bird makes a nearconstant shrill and high scream, giving it advantage on Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks.

trait.name: Stubborn
entries: The jubjub bird has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed}. In addition, Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling}) checks are made with disadvantage against the jubjub bird.

trait.name: Unpredictable
entries: {@action opportunity attack||Opportunity attacks} against the jubjub bird are made with disadvantage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The jubjub bird makes two Bite attacks, or it makes one Bite attack and one Constrict attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Constrict
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d10 + 3}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 13}). Until this grapple ends, the creature is {@condition restrained}, and the jubjub bird can't constrict another target.

reaction.name: Impassioned Riposte
entries: When a creature the jubjub bird can see misses it with an attack while within 10 feet of it, the jubjub bird can make one Bite attack against the attacker.

trait.name: Aura of Honesty
entries: A creature that enters a space within 10 feet of the juniper sheriff for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw or be unable to speak a deliberate lie until it starts its turn more than 10 feet away from the sheriff. Creatures affected by this trait are aware of it, and the juniper sheriff knows whether a creature in the area succeeded or failed on its saving throw.

trait.name: Sheriff's Duty
entries: The juniper sheriff knows if it hears a lie and can't speak a deliberate lie.

trait.name: Unfailing Memory
entries: The juniper sheriff remembers every creature that has ever spoken to it. If a creature has spoken to the sheriff and speaks to the sheriff again while polymorphed or disguised, the sheriff has advantage on Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}), Wisdom ({@skill Insight}), and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks to identify or recognize that creature.

action.name: Saber
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Bitter Truth
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) psychic damage, and the target has disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against the juniper sheriff's Aura of Honesty within the next 1 minute.

bonus.name: Hop Away
entries: The juniper sheriff takes the {@action Dash} or {@action Disengage} action.

trait.name: Scent of Death
entries: Any creature that isn't Undead and that starts its turn within 5 feet of the kadag ogre must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the start of its next turn. On a successful saving throw, the creature is immune to the kadag ogre's Scent of Death for 1 hour.

trait.name: Undead Affinity
entries: An Undead has disadvantage on attack rolls against the kadag ogre if the ogre hasn't attacked that Undead within the past 24 hours. In addition, each skeleton and zombie within 30 feet of the kadag ogre that has no master follows the ogre's verbal commands until a master asserts magical control over it.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kadag ogre makes three Spear attacks.

action.name: Spear
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) piercing damage, or 14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) piercing damage if used with two hands to make a melee attack.

action.name: Master of Death {@recharge 5}
entries: The kadag ogre empowers up to three friendly Undead it can see within 30 feet of it. Each empowered Undead can use a reaction to move up to half its speed and make one melee weapon attack. In addition, an empowered Undead gains 5 ({@damage 2d4}) temporary hit points for 1 minute. The kadag ogre can't empower Undead with an Intelligence score of 10 or higher.
This gray-skinned {@creature ogre} is clad in worked leather and stands straighter than its brethren. The stench of rotting flesh radiates from the creature.

Occasionally, an {@creature ogre} will seclude itself and master its hunger. Claiming a cave atop a desolate mountainside or deep in the badlands, the {@creature ogre} becomes a hermit and leaves its caves only to ritualistically murder whatever it finds. {@creature ogre||Ogres} that stick through the process and resist the urge to feast eventually become kadag ogres, strengthened by murder and masters of their own urges. They are smarter and tougher than most of their kin but are much less sociable.

entries.name: Masters of Death
entries: While not true necromancers, the rites performed by kadag ogres make them kin to the undead. Their flesh is hardened against death magic, their mere presence sickens the living, and they can command lesser undead in their vicinity. These rites require sacrifices of intelligent creatures, and kadag ogres often prey upon travelers or nearby villagers for the materials they need. Some kadag ogres corral masterless undead, using them to guard territory, gather sacrifices, and protect the ogre's isolation.

entries.name: Cryptic Monsters
entries: The secretive lives of these ogres means that most of their victims don't know what they face. Villages tell tales of the Monster in the Wastes, or a petty necromancer with a hulking servant, or the giant that eats those who intrude upon its secret lair. Other ogres speak reverently of the wise death hermits, teaching that any {@creature ogre} can become a kadag with enough dedication. Occasionally, some warlord or necromancer seeks out a kadag ogre and entreats it into service. Most fail, but some manage to bully, bribe, or trick the ogre into service, where they serve as surly lieutenants over undead and other {@creature ogre||ogres}.

entries.name: Mountain Ambushers
entries: Kadag ogres prefer the narrow passes and limited sightlines of the high mountains, both for the isolation and the advantages it provides over their prey. They enjoy ambushing groups passing in front of their cave mouths and flinging those capable of fighting back off the side of the mountain.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The kalkydra casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 19}):

trait.name: Angelic Weapons
entries: The kalkydra's weapon attacks are magical. When the kalkydra hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 4d8} radiant damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: The kalkydra doesn't require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The kalkydra has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kalkydra makes two Bite or Sunray attacks. It can replace one attack with a Constrict attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) piercing damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) radiant damage.

action.name: Constrict
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) bludgeoning damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) radiant damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 18}). Until this grapple ends, the creature is {@condition restrained}, and the kalkydra can't constrict another target.

action.name: Sunray
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 11} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}24 ({@damage 4d8 + 6}) radiant damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) fire damage.

action.name: Song of Sunrise (1/Day)
entries: The kalkydra sings a song to welcome the dawn, causing sunlight to fill the area in a 120-foot radius around it. This effect lasts for 1 minute.

reaction.name: Solar Nimbus
entries: When the kalkydra is hit by an attack, it surrounds itself in a fiery nimbus, searing the attacker. The attacker takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) fire damage and 9 ({@damage 2d8}) radiant damage, and the nimbus sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Until the start of the kalkydra's next turn, a creature within 5 feet of the kalkydra that hits it with a melee attack takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) fire and 9 ({@damage 2d8}) radiant damage.

variant.name: Fallen Kalkydra
entries: There are times when the rare kalkydra falls from grace, forsaking its deity to consort with evil. Such fallen angels shroud the land in darkness so that evil may better execute its devious machinations. For a fallen kalkydra, replace {@spell daylight} with {@spell darkness} on its list of spells, and replace Song of Sunrise with the following action:

entries.name: Sing Out the Sun (1/Day)
entries: The kalkydra sings a song that accompanies the sunset, draining the light from the area and creating an area of magical darkness in a 120-foot radius around it. This effect lasts for 1 minute.

items.name: Sing Out the Sun (1/Day)
entries: The kalkydra sings a song that accompanies the sunset, draining the light from the area and creating an area of magical darkness in a 120-foot radius around it. This effect lasts for 1 minute.

trait.name: Impaling Charge
entries: If the karkadann moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a Horn attack on the same turn, the karkadann impales the target on its horn, grappling the target if it is a Large or smaller creature (escape {@dc 15}). Until the grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, takes 18 ({@damage 4d8}) piercing damage at the start of each of its turns, and the karkadann can't make horn attacks against other targets.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The karkadann has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The karkadann makes two Hooves attacks or one Horn attack and one Hooves attack.

action.name: Hooves
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Horn
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}24 ({@damage 4d8 + 6}) piercing damage.

action.name: Toss
entries: One Large or smaller creature impaled by the karkadann is thrown up to 30 feet in a random direction and knocked {@condition prone}. If a thrown target strikes a solid surface, the target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it was thrown. If the target is thrown at another creature, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take the same damage and be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Healing Touch (3/Day)
entries: The karkadann touches another creature with its horn. The target magically regains 22 ({@damage 4d8 + 2}) hp and is cured of all diseases and poisons afflicting it.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The khamaseen doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Stinging Dust
entries: The khamaseen is surrounded by a cloud of swirling dust and small stones. A creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of the khamaseen must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or have disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of its next turn. On a successful saving throw, the creature is immune to the khamaseen's Stinging Dust for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) bludgeoning damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4}) lightning damage.

action.name: Choking Cloud {@recharge 6}
entries: The khamaseen surrounds itself with a large cloud of dust and debris. Each creature within 10 feet of the khamaseen must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition incapacitated} for 1 minute. While {@condition incapacitated}, the creature is unable to breathe and coughs uncontrollably. An {@condition incapacitated} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

reaction.name: Shock
entries: If a creature wearing metal armor or wielding a metal weapon or shield moves within 5 feet of the khamaseen, it must succeed on a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw or take 5 ({@damage 2d4}) lightning damage.

monster.name: Khargi
languages: understands Abyssal and Infernal but can't speak

trait.name: Deadly Infestation
entries: A creature that touches the khargi or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage as stinging insects rise up to protect the khargi.

trait.name: Death Swarms
entries: When the khargi dies, the insects crawling across and within it burst from its body, forming {@dice 2d4} swarms of insects that appear in unoccupied spaces within 5 feet of the khargi's space.

trait.name: Infested Weapons
entries: The khargi's weapon attacks are magical. When the khargi hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 2d6} poison damage (included in the attack).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The khargi makes one Bite attack and two Leg attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Legs
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d4 + 5}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Swarming Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The khargi exhales biting, stinging insects in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw, taking 31 ({@damage 9d6}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

reaction.name: Adaptive Carapace
entries: When the khargi takes acid, cold, force, lightning, or thunder damage, it can magically attune itself to that type of damage. Until the end of its next turn, the khargi has resistance to damage of the triggering type, and when it hits with any weapon, the target takes an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) damage of the triggering type.

trait.name: Bickering Heads
entries: A kobold ettin's constant bickering can be easily heard and proves a tough habit to disrupt. The kobold ettin has disadvantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks to stay silent, but it has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks. In addition, it has advantage against being {@condition blinded}, {@condition charmed}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition frightened}, {@condition stunned}, and knocked {@condition unconscious}.

trait.name: Might of Giants
entries: Though squat by ettin standards, kobold ettins are as much giant as they are kobold. A kobold ettin is a Large Giant for the purpose of determining its carrying capacity.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The kobold ettin has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the kobold ettin's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the kobold ettin has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kobold ettin makes two Greatclub or Spear attacks.

action.name: Greatclub
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Spear
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) piercing damage, or 7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) piercing damage if used with two hands to make a melee attack.

action.name: Echoing Burps {@recharge 5}
entries: The kobold ettin tries to let loose double roar but instead sends forth a series of obnoxious, smelly belches in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 10 ({@damage 4d4}) thunder damage and is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

A stout, scaly figure with two squabbling kobold heads walks among smaller kobolds, its brethren giving it considerable berth. Abruptly, the double heads stop arguing to cut loose with a double belch that shakes the cavern walls.

The origins of the first kobold ettin are a total mystery. Hags may have cursed two disruptive kobold minions to inhabit the same body as punishment. Or {@creature ettin||ettins} bargained with dragons for power and received only boons that twisted their forms to resemble the kobolds they had bullied. Few who meet a kobold ettin consider their origins. While not as physically imposing as a true {@creature ettin}, kobold ettins lack the kobold's craven streak, and adventurers who assume they are cowards have miscalculated, often with fatal results.

entries.name: Composite Form
entries: Kobold ettins are famously argumentative. As outcasts, their spite borne from an existence without privacy and a shared stomach's constant hunger. Known for complaining, bickering, and insulting each other, kobold ettins are heard before they are seen, and savvy heroes exploit this weakness. {@creature ettin||Ettins} and other giants avoid kobold ettins, but many kobold communities welcome their imposing brethren as laborers and protectors. Though kobold warrens are often cramped for the kobold ettin, they crave an audience for their grievances as much as food for their bellies and often tolerate the small spaces for the companionship.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The kogukhpak has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The kogukhpak's long jump is up to 30 feet and its high jump is up to 15 feet, with or without a running start.

trait.name: Sunlight Hypersensitivity
entries: The kogukhpak takes 20 radiant damage when it starts its turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, it has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kogukhpak makes one Bite attack and two Gore attacks, or it makes three Spit Fire attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 2d8 + 10}) bludgeoning damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) fire damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 18}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, the kogukhpak can automatically hit the target with its Bite, and the kogukhpak can't make Bite attacks against other targets.

action.name: Gore
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}37 ({@damage 5d10 + 10}) piercing damage.

action.name: Spit Fire
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 11} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}32 ({@damage 6d8 + 5}) fire damage.

action.name: Devastating Leap {@recharge 6}
entries: The kogukhpak leaps up to 30 feet and lands on its feet in a space it can see. Each creature in the space or within 5 feet of the kogukhpak when it lands must make a {@dc 20} Strength saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 72 ({@damage 16d8}) bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage, isn't knocked {@condition prone}, and, if it is in the kogukhpak's space, can choose to be pushed 5 feet back or to the side of the kogukhpak. A creature in the kogukhpak's space that chooses not to be pushed suffers the consequences of a failed saving throw.

action.name: Fire Breath {@recharge 6}
entries: The kogukhpak exhales fire in a 90-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw, taking 70 ({@damage 20d6}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
Tusks jut from the jaw of this massive albino toad, and small, spiky horns rise from above its demonic eyes.

Kogukhpak demons are massive toad-like creatures with mammoth tusks and a bottomless cauldron of untamed flame deep within their bellies. They are typically found in colder climes, which helps keep the raging fires within in check. They inhabit deep caverns to avoid the sun and visit the surface only at night to hunt.

entries.name: Enemy of the Light
entries: In the cold, far north, the nights are long and winter nearly interminable. This allows the kogukhpak to hunt and kill at its leisure, rarely having to fear its only true weakness: sunlight. During the summer, when daylight rarely ceases, the fiend remains underground.

entries.name: Voracious Killer
entries: The kogukhpak is a scourge to all it encounters. The fiend will attack and devour anyone or anything it comes across while hunting, to fuel the incessant fires within it. A koguhkpak eats everything, leaving little trace except blood and signs of struggle, with occasionally ashes or burnt grasses. Remnants of a settlement destroyed by a seemingly sourceless flame are a sure sign of a kogukhpak.

trait.name: Toxic Ichor
entries: While submerged in water, the lakescourge lotus's ichor taints the water within 10 feet of it. When a creature enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} while it remains in the area and for 1 minute after it leaves. A {@condition poisoned} creature that is no longer in the water can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If the purify food and drink spell is cast on a space within 5 feet of the lakescourge lotus or on the space it occupies, this trait ceases to function for 1 minute.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The lakescourge lotus doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Waterwalker
entries: The lakescourge lotus can walk across the surface of water as if it were solid ground. This trait otherwise works like the {@spell water walk} spell but isn't itself magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lakescourge lotus makes two Tainted Claw or Poisonous Water Jet attacks.

action.name: Tainted Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) slashing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. A {@condition poisoned} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Poisonous Water Jet
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 3d8 + 2}) poison damage.

action.name: Enter Reflection
entries: The lakescourge lotus touches a body of water large enough to hold it and becomes a reflection on the surface of the water. While in this form, the lotus has immunity to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks and resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from magical attacks. While a reflection, its speed is reduced to 5 feet, it can't make Tainted Claw attacks, and it can revert to its true form as a bonus action. If the lakescourge lotus takes damage that isn't bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing while it is a reflection, it is forced back into its true form.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The lantern giant casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Limited Amphibiousness
entries: The lantern giant can breathe air and water, but it needs to be submerged at least once every 4 hours to avoid {@quickref suffocating||2}.

trait.name: Hypnotic Luminescence
entries: The tendril on the lantern giant's head sheds bright light in a 60-foot radius and dim light for an additional 60 feet. When a creature that can see the light starts its turn within 60 feet of the giant, the creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Charisma saving throw or become {@condition charmed} for 24 hours, regarding the giant as a friendly acquaintance. If the giant or one of its allies harms the {@condition charmed} creature, this effect ends. If the creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the giant's Hypnotic Luminescence for the next 24 hours. At the start of its turn, the lantern giant chooses whether this light is active.

trait.name: Speak with Aquatic Creatures
entries: The lantern giant can communicate with Beasts and Monstrosities that have a swimming speed as if they shared a language.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lantern giant makes three Trident attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Trident
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 10 ft. or range 20/60 ft. one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d6 + 8}) piercing damage, or 21 ({@damage 3d8 + 8}) piercing damage if used with two hands to make a melee attack.

action.name: Crush of the Deep {@recharge 5}
entries: The lantern giant subverts the environment to summon the pressure of the deepest ocean depths in a 40-foot cube of water centered on a point it can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature in the cube must make a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw as the pressure increases, taking 44 ({@damage 8d10}) bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
A ghostly bobbing light illuminates a towering figure clad in the weathered ruins of a sunken ship's rigging.

Lantern giants are massive bipedal humanoids with rust-red skin that walk along the bottom-most depths of the ocean floor. A tendril hangs from the giant's forehead, tipped by a nodule that glows with a ghostly light.

entries.name: Helpful Hermits
entries: Lantern giants are one of the few creatures intimately familiar with the deepest reaches of the sea, and they are willing to act as guides or scouts to travelers.

entries.name: Treasure Seekers
entries: Few lantern giants remain, but each patrols the darkest depths of the ocean floor, searching ruins and wrecks for something to bolster their dying kingdom. Lantern giants are incredibly secretive, never revealing what they are seeking. Still, they are more than happy to align themselves with travelers eager to plumb the ocean depths—as long as the travelers don't ask too many questions.

monster.name: Leashed Lesion
languages: understands Void Speech but can't speak

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The leashed lesion makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) piercing damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4}) necrotic damage. The lesion and any creature {@condition grappled} by its Life Tether regain hp equal to the necrotic damage dealt.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) slashing damage.

action.name: Draining Burst {@recharge 5}
entries: The leashed lesion selects a point it can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature within 20 feet of that point must make a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw, taking 13 ({@damage 3d8}) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The lesion and any creature {@condition grappled} by its Life Tether each gain 13 ({@damage 3d8}) temporary hp.

bonus.name: Life Tether
entries: The leashed lesion attaches a symbiotic tether to a creature sitting in the recess in its back. A creature in the recess that isn't attached to the tether takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage at the end of the lesion's turn, and the lesion regains hp equal to the damage dealt. While the tether is attached, the creature is {@condition grappled} by the lesion. The lesion or the creature can detach the tether as a bonus action. The lesion can have its symbiotic tether attached to only one creature at a time.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The leavesrot ooze can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the leavesrot ooze remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary pile of leaves.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The leavesrot ooze doesn't require sleep.

trait.name: Rotting Leaves
entries: When a creature hits the leavesrot ooze with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it, mold spores and decomposing matter fly out from the wound. Each creature within 5 feet of the ooze must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) acid damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 13}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, the ooze can automatically hit the target with its Pseudopod, and the ooze can't make Pseudopod attacks against other targets.

action.name: Release Spores {@recharge 5}
entries: The leavesrot ooze releases spores from the mold coating its leaves. Each creature within 20 feet of the ooze must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) poison damage and is choking. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't choking. A choking creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
This pile of rotting leaves—in reds and yellows, oranges and browns—rustles as it scuttles along the ground in search of its next meal.

Leavesrot oozes spring to life when sentient mold grows on fallen leaves, causing them to coalesce and act in concert. Many believe they are the unlikely result of meddling by a wizard's apprentice, who grew tired of raking leaves outside the tower.

entries.name: Corrosive Heaps
entries: As piles of fallen leaves decompose, they coalesce, and as the mold grows, the ooze exudes acid potent enough to strip flesh from bone. Druids sometimes use these oozes to accelerate the decomposition of rotting corpses.

entries.name: Autumnal Ambuscade
entries: Leavesrot oozes lie in ambush on the forest floor, grabbing and devouring creatures that cross its path. Travelers have heard the cries of their companions who, after being wrestled to the ground with a withering squelch, are slowly eaten by these predatory mounds.

entries.name: Carnivorous Mounds
entries: No one is sure how the leavesrot ooze developed a taste for flesh, but they are sated, however briefly, after consuming seven creatures in a single month, temporarily entering a dormant state. Travelers in areas known to contain leavesrot oozes often bring along extra chickens, pigs, or other livestock to sate the creatures long enough to secure passage through the forest.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The leech demon can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Blood Sense
entries: The leech demon can pinpoint, by scent, the location of creatures that aren't Constructs or Undead within 30 feet of it.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The leech demon has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The leech demon makes one Bite attack and two Tentacle Bite attacks, or it makes two Bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Tentacle Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) piercing damage and 4 ({@damage 1d8}) necrotic damage, and the leech demon can attach its tentacle to the target. The demon has two tentacles, each of which can attach to only one target. While a tentacle is attached, the demon can't use that tentacle to make Tentacle Bite attacks, the target is {@condition restrained}, and the demon doesn't attack with the tentacle. Instead, at the start of each of the demon's turns, each creature with a tentacle attached to it loses 11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) hp due to blood loss, and the demon gains temporary hp equal to that amount. The demon can add temporary hp gained from this attack together and can add it to temporary hp gained from Release Swarm. The demon's temporary hp can't exceed half its hp maximum.
The leech demon can detach one or both tentacles as a bonus action. A creature, including the target, can use its action to detach the demon's tentacle by succeeding on a {@dc 17} Strength check.

action.name: Release Swarm {@recharge 5}
entries: The leech demon shakes loose dozens of leeches, creating a {@creature leech swarm|ToB2} (or use a {@creature swarm of poisonous snakes}). The swarm acts as an ally of the leech demon and obeys its spoken commands. The swarm remains for 1 minute, until the leech demon dies, or until the demon dismisses it as a bonus action. If the leech demon is within 5 feet of the swarm, it can use its action to consume the swarm, gaining temporary hp equal to the swarm's current hp. It can add temporary hp gained in this way with temporary hp gained from its Tentacle Bite attack. The demon's temporary hp can't exceed half its hp maximum. The demon can have only one swarm active at one time.
Hundreds of leeches cling to and crawl around this serpentine creature, which has a toothy maw filling most of its face. It raises two tentacles, revealing similar toothy maws lining each tentacle.

Leech demons prefer to spend their time on the Material Plane, where their unsettling appearance draws more of a reaction and where they can better inflict suffering. They especially enjoy infesting their victims' bodies with leeches, which burst forth from the corpses to wreck havoc on the victims' loved ones.

entries.name: Implements of Swampland Revenge
entries: Rumors proliferate about rituals to summon leech demons to right a grievous wrong. The demons gleefully wreak vengeance on their targets and try to ensure their summoners witness the gruesome deaths. The stories fail to disclose the demons demand a price from their callers, usually paid by the life of someone dear to them. Only the most desperate benefit from calling leech demons for retribution, as they often have no problem offering themselves up as payment when the demons complete their deeds.

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with silvered weapons

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The infernal tutor casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Devil's Sight
entries: Magical darkness doesn't impede the infernal tutor's {@sense darkvision}.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The infernal tutor has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Weaken Resolve
entries: The infernal tutor's speech is laced with subtle enchantments that make its words seem logical or profound, regardless of the actual words used. It has advantage on Charisma ({@skill Deception}) and Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks against Humanoids.

In addition, if the infernal tutor spends at least 1 minute conversing with a Humanoid, that creature has disadvantage on saving throws against the infernal tutor's Fiendish Tutelage and against enchantment spells the tutor casts.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The infernal tutor makes three Claw or Tutor's Baton attacks, or it makes one Claw attack and two Tutor's Baton attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Claw (True Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Tutor's Baton
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Fiendish Tutelage {@recharge 5}
entries: The infernal tutor speaks fiendish teachings. Each creature within 15 feet of the tutor and that can hear it must make a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw, taking 35 ({@damage 10d6}) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The infernal tutor magically transforms into a Small or Medium Humanoid, or back into its true, fiendish form. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

reaction.name: Strength of Character
entries: When the infernal tutor succeeds on an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw, the tutor responds with a scathing magical insult if the source of the effect is a creature within 60 feet of the tutor. That creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or take 7 ({@damage 2d6}) psychic damage and have disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against a spell cast by the infernal tutor.

trait.name: Expert Wrestler
entries: The leviathan hunter can grapple creatures that are two sizes larger than itself and can move at its full speed when dragging a creature it has {@condition grappled}. If the hunter grapples a Medium or smaller creature, the target has disadvantage on its escape attempts.
