In addition, the hunter has advantage on ability checks and saving throws made to escape a grapple or end the {@condition restrained} condition.

trait.name: Hardy Defense
entries: While the leviathan hunter is wearing no armor and wielding no shield, its AC includes its Constitution modifier.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The hunter can hold its breath for 30 minutes.

trait.name: Leviathan Hunter
entries: The leviathan hunter has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) and Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks to find and track Large or larger creatures with a swimming speed.

trait.name: Marine Weapons
entries: The leviathan hunter's weapon attacks are magical. When the hunter hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 2d8} cold damage or poison damage (included in the attack), the hunter's choice.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The leviathan hunter has advantage on an attack roll against a target if at least one of the hunter's allies is within 5 feet of the target and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the leviathan hunter has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The leviathan hunter makes three Slam attacks, or it makes one Harpoon attack and two Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) cold damage or poison damage (the hunter's choice), and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 17}). The hunter can grapple only one target at a time.

action.name: Harpoon
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) cold damage or poison damage (the hunter's choice), and the harpoon sticks in the target. While the harpoon is stuck, the target takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage at the start of each of its turns, the hunter can't make Harpoon attacks against other targets, and the target and hunter can't move further than 60 feet away from each other. A creature, including the target, can take its action to detach the harpoon by succeeding on a {@dc 17} Strength check. Alternatively, the cord connecting the leviathan hunter to the harpoon can be attacked and destroyed (AC 10; hp 25; immunity to bludgeoning, poison, and psychic damage), dislodging the harpoon into an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target and preventing the leviathan hunter from using Recall Harpoon.

action.name: Crush
entries: One creature {@condition grappled} by the hunter must make a {@dc 17} Strength saving throw, taking 33 ({@damage 8d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Recall Harpoon
entries: The leviathan hunter pulls on the cord connected to its harpoon, returning the harpoon to its empty hand. If the harpoon is stuck in a creature, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 17} Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 20 feet toward the hunter.

reaction.name: Grappled Redirect
entries: If the leviathan hunter is the target of an attack it can see while grappling a creature, it can hold the {@condition grappled} creature in the way, and the {@condition grappled} creature becomes the target of the attack instead.

trait.name: Sense Magic
entries: The ley wanderer senses magic within 120 feet of it at will. This trait otherwise works like the {@spell detect magic} spell but isn't itself magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ley wanderer makes three Slam or Psychic Lash attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) bludgeoning damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) psychic damage.

action.name: Psychic Lash
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d8 + 4}) psychic damage.

action.name: Dispelling Burst {@recharge 5}
entries: The ley wanderer emits a psychic burst that disrupts magic within 30 feet of it. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Intelligence saving throw, taking 27 ({@damage 6d8}) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. In addition, each spell of 3rd level or lower in the area immediately ends, and the ley wanderer gains 5 temporary hp for each spell ended in this way.

action.name: Teleport (3/Day)
entries: The ley wanderer magically teleports itself and up to six willing creatures holding hands with the ley wanderer, along with any equipment they are wearing or carrying, to a location the ley wanderer is familiar with, up to 100 miles away. If the destination is a location rich in magical energy, such as a ley line, the ley wanderer can teleport up to 300 miles away.

bonus.name: Drain Magic Item
entries: The ley wanderer drains the magic from an item it is holding. A magic item with charges loses {@dice 1d6} charges, an item with limited uses per day loses one daily use, and a single-use item, such as a potion or spell scroll is destroyed. All other magic items have their effects suppressed for 1 minute. The ley wanderer gains 5 temporary hp each time it drains a magic item. A drained item regains its magic after 24 hours.

reaction.name: Absorb Spell
entries: When a creature the ley wanderer can see within 30 feet of it casts a spell, the ley wanderer can absorb the spell's energy, countering it. This works like the {@spell counterspell} spell, except the ley wanderer must always make a spellcasting ability check, no matter the spell's level. Its ability check for this is +7. If it successfully counters the spell, the ley wanderer gains 5 temporary hp for each level of the spell.

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with cold iron weapons

trait.name: Draw Life Essence
entries: The life broker can spend 10 minutes coaxing the life essence out of a willing creature, taking only the agreed‑upon amount of time from the creature's maximum lifetime. The life essence appears as a rosy mist that rises from the mouth, nose, or skin of the creature and snakes into a carved crystal vial in the broker's cloak, where it takes on the form of a crimson liquid. A creature that drinks such a vial gains the life stored within it, provided the life broker gave the vial willingly. If the life broker draws all the remaining life from a creature, that creature dies, and it can be returned to life only by means of a {@spell wish} spell.

trait.name: Life Reading
entries: If the life broker spends 1 minute studying a mortal creature it can see within 30 feet of it, the broker can determine the remainder of that creature's natural life, to the second.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The life broker has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Necrotic Weapons
entries: The life broker's weapon attacks are magical. When the life broker hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 4d8} necrotic damage (included in the attack).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The life broker makes three Rapier attacks.

action.name: Rapier
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) piercing plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Life Feast {@recharge 5}
entries: The life broker pulls life from hostile creatures within 30 feet of it that aren't Constructs or Undead. Each such creature in the area must make a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw, taking 36 ({@damage 8d8}) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The life broker gains temporary hp equal to the single highest amount of necrotic damage dealt and has advantage on attack rolls until the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Quick-Footed
entries: The life broker takes the {@action Dash}, {@action Disengage}, or {@action Dodge} action.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the light eater remains motionless in dim light or darkness, it is indistinguishable from a normal rock.

trait.name: Light Absorption
entries: When the light eater starts its turn within 5 feet of a source of light, the light is reduced while the light eater remains within 100 feet of the light source. Bright light becomes dim light, and dim light becomes darkness. If the light eater reduces a light source's light to darkness, the light eater sheds multicolored bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet for 1 hour, and the light source is extinguished if it is nonmagical flame or dispelled if it was created by a spell of 2nd level or lower.

trait.name: Light Sense
entries: The light eater can pinpoint the location of any source of light within 100 feet of it and can sense the general direction of any source of light within 1 mile of it.

action.name: Tentacle
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) bludgeoning damage.

variant.name: Light Eater Familiars
entries: Some light eaters are willing to serve spellcasters as a familiar. Such light eaters have the following trait.

entries.name: Familiar
entries: The light eater can serve another creature as a familiar, forming a magic, telepathic bond with that willing companion. While the two are bonded, the companion can sense what the light eater senses as long as they are within 1 mile of each other. While the light eater is within 10 feet of its companion, the companion gains resistance to radiant damage. At any time and for any reason, the light eater can end its service as a familiar, ending the telepathic bond.

items.name: Familiar
entries: The light eater can serve another creature as a familiar, forming a magic, telepathic bond with that willing companion. While the two are bonded, the companion can sense what the light eater senses as long as they are within 1 mile of each other. While the light eater is within 10 feet of its companion, the companion gains resistance to radiant damage. At any time and for any reason, the light eater can end its service as a familiar, ending the telepathic bond.

trait.name: Brittle
entries: If the living soot takes cold damage, it partially freezes; its speed is reduced by 10 feet and its AC is reduced by 2 until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Choking Air Form
entries: The living soot can enter a hostile creature's space and stop there, and it can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing. In addition, when a creature starts its turn in the same space as the living soot, the creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or take 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage and be unable to breathe until it starts its turn outside the soot's space.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The living soot doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the living soot remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a tangled mass of blackened, dusty webbing.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The elemental makes two Slam attacks, or it makes one Slam attack and one Constrict attack.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Constrict
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, and the living soot can't Constrict another target.

action.name: Engulfing Sootstorm {@recharge 4}
entries: The living soot spins violently in place, spreading out tendrils of thick, poisonous ash. Each creature within 20 feet of it must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) poison damage and its speed is halved. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and its speed isn't reduced. The speed reduction lasts until a creature spends an action cleaning off the ash.

trait.name: Danger From Above
entries: If the lobe lemur jumps at least 10 feet straight toward a creature from a higher position than the target, such as leaping down from a tree, it has advantage on the next attack roll it makes against that creature.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The lobe lemur's long jump is up to 30 feet and its high jump is up to 15 feet, with or without a running start.

trait.name: Swamp Camouflage
entries: The lobe lemur has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in swampy terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lobe lemur makes three Claw Swipe attacks, or it makes one Face Clamp attack and two Claw Swipe attacks.

action.name: Claw Swipe
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Face Clamp
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) piercing damage, and the lobe lemur attaches to the target's head. If the lobe lemur is already attached to the target when it hits with this attack, the attack doesn't deal damage.

Instead, the target is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn. While attached to the target, the lobe lemur can attack no other creature except the target but has advantage on its attack rolls. The lemur's speed also becomes 0, it can't benefit from any bonus to its speed, and it moves with the target.

A creature, including the target, can take its action to detach the lemur by succeeding on a {@dc 14} Strength check. On its turn, the lemur can detach itself from the target by using 5 feet of movement.

trait.name: Devil's Sight
entries: Magical darkness doesn't impede the maelstrom devil's {@sense darkvision}.

trait.name: Influence Weather
entries: Nearby weather responds to the maelstrom devil's desires. At the start of each minute, the maelstrom devil can choose to change the precipitation and wind within 1 mile of it by one stage, up or down (no action required). This effect works like the changing weather conditions aspect of the {@spell control weather} spell, except the maelstrom devil can't change the temperature and the conditions change immediately.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The devil has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devil makes three Claws attacks and one Tempest Trident attack, or it makes three Lightning Ray attacks.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Tempest Trident
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d12 + 3}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) cold damage. A creature hit by this attack must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone} by a gust of wind channeled through the trident.

action.name: Lightning Ray
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 8} to hit, range 150 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) lightning damage.

action.name: Crown of Water (1/Day)
entries: The water on the maelstrom devil's head erupts in a geyser. Each creature within 10 feet of the devil must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw, taking 35 ({@damage 10d6}) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. For 1 minute, when a creature enters a space within 10 feet of the devil for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or take 10 ({@damage 3d6}) cold damage.
Razor-sharp claws tip the long arms of this coralline fiend, and a crown of flowing water adorns its head. It raises a rusted trident in fury, and the wind rises into a tumultuous gale around it.

Maelstrom devils are fiends who largely dwell on the Material Plane. Unconcerned with the ever-shifting politics of Hell, they lair within dangerous shoals, war-torn waters, and remote shipping lanes. These devils are brute-force manipulators, unleashing horrible storms and suborned monsters upon passing ships, then roping mortals into their schemes with promises of safe passage and personal profit.

entries.name: Tempestuous Extortionists
entries: Maelstrom devils can control the weather, conjuring horrific storms or becalming unruly seas. They use this to beset merchant vessels and ferries with sudden hurricanes and rough seas before offering to quell the storm if the captain will do as the devil asks. Compliant captains find that their ships sail safely, and they make record profits, as competitors find their own shipments running aground or delayed by unseasonable storms. Others, as well as any captains who try to go back on their deal with the devil, are killed.

entries.name: Labyrinthine Manipulators
entries: Maelstrom devils aren't concerned with the damnation of individual souls, instead preferring to use their network of blackmailed captains and subverted ships to spread misery on a larger scale. They ensure that food fails to reach starving cities, that vital medicine never reaches those who need it most, and that the families of all aboard the nearest vessel never see their loved ones again.

trait.name: Frozen Aura
entries: When a creature enters a space within 30 feet of the maha or starts its turn there, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw or have its speed reduced by 10 feet until it starts its turn outside the aura.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The maha has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Snow Camouflage
entries: The maha has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) made to hide in snowy or icy terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The maha makes three Claw attacks. If the maha hits one creature with two Claw attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw or chuckle until the end of its next turn. While chuckling, a creature can't speak coherently, can't cast spells with verbal components, and has disadvantage on attack rolls with weapons that use Strength or Dexterity.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) cold damage.

action.name: Deadly Laughter {@recharge 5}
entries: The maha chuckles, giggles, and chortles at nearby creatures. Each creature within 30 feet of the maha must make a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 42 ({@damage 12d6}) psychic damage, drops whatever it is holding, and laughs for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and doesn't drop whatever it is holding or laugh. While laughing, a creature is {@condition incapacitated}, can't speak coherently, and takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) psychic damage at the start of each of its turns. A laughing creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature dies while laughing, its face turns pale blue and displays a wide grin.
This emaciated creature with long, stringy black hair, milky white eyes, and pale blue skin squats in the snow. Its face is split in an almost too-wide grin.
Mahas are demons of the cold north, reveling in the snowy wastes but never far from civilization, which holds their preferred food source: humanoids.

entries.name: Laughing Nightmares
entries: Maha demons are always seen smiling, laughing, or giggling. They spread their malevolent hilarity wherever they go by tickling their victims with their claws, all the while tearing into flesh. The victims' bodies are said to retain that same grotesque and eternal smile on their cold and rigid faces.

entries.name: Sustained by Torment
entries: The ongoing torment mahas create sustains them. A maha will try to unsettle victims long before confronting them. By manipulating the environment and creating baffling and frightening conditions, the demon seeks to slowly build a feeling of terror. A maha often ambushes single travelers or picks off stragglers, separating them from their companions and building terror on multiple fronts.

entries.name: Not so Bright
entries: According to legends, maha demons are not terribly intelligent. Stories abound of travelers escaping mahas by tricking the demons into following false footsteps in the snow, attacking cloaked snow sculptures, or ambushing an empty campsite. Such deceptions often result in the loss of equipment or a reliable pack mule, but it is preferable to contending with the maha directly

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Malaabit fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Three Heads
entries: Malaabit has three heads. He has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks and on saving throws against being {@condition deafened}, {@condition stunned}, and knocked {@condition unconscious}.

trait.name: Reactive Precognition
entries: Malaabit gets two extra reactions that can be used only for Prescient Defense or Tactical Foresight.

trait.name: Unquenchable Voice
entries: Malaabit's voice can't be quieted or silenced by any means.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Malaabit makes three Slam or Psychic Blast attacks. If Malaabit hits one creature with two Slam attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 18} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}23 ({@damage 4d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Psychic Blast
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 10} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}22 ({@damage 5d6 + 5}) psychic damage.

action.name: Allegorical Apologue {@recharge 5}
entries: Malaabit recites a short moral story about animals, choosing one of the following options:

items.name: Oracular Fortune.
items.entry: Malaabit's fable fills the listeners' minds with the hope of future success. Each creature of Malaabit's choice within 20 feet of him and that can hear him gains 10 ({@damage 3d6}) temporary hp and has advantage on the next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw it makes before the start of Malaabit's next turn.

items.name: Sins of the Past.
items.entry: Malaabit's parable brings forth a torrent of bad memories. Each creature of Malaabit's choice within 20 feet of him and that can hear him must make a {@dc 18} Charisma saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 35 ({@damage 10d6}) psychic damage and has disadvantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws until the start of Malaabit's next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and doesn't have disadvantage on attack rolls, ability checks, or saving throws.

action.name: Travel the Planes (1/Day)
entries: Malaabit transports himself to a different plane of existence. This legendary action works like the {@spell plane shift} spell, except he can affect only himself and can't use it to banish an unwilling creature.

reaction.name: Prescient Defense
entries: When a creature scores a critical hit against Malaabit, the hit becomes a normal hit.

reaction.name: Tactical Foresight
entries: When Malaabit fails an attack roll, he can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

legendary.name: Foretell Favor
entries: Malaabit foretells fortune for one creature he can see within 30 feet of him. The target can roll a {@dice d6} and add the number rolled to the next attack roll or saving throw it makes. Once Malaabit has used this legendary action on a creature, he can't use it on that creature again until he has used it on another creature.

legendary.name: Move
entries: Malaabit moves up to his speed without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

legendary.name: Pronounce Doom
entries: Malaabit pronounces doom upon one creature he can see within 30 feet of him. The target must succeed on a {@dc 18} Charisma saving throw or roll a {@dice d6} and subtract the number rolled from the next attack roll or saving throw it makes. Once Malaabit has used this legendary action on a creature, he can't use it on that creature again until he has used it on another creature.

The glossy black fur coating the fiend's bullish body changes to a shock of crimson just above his hooves. Three heads top his muscular humanoid torso: a ram's head on his right, a bull's head in the center, and a male human head on his left.

Malaabit is the fiendish master of the past, present, and future, according to himself at least, and he is happy to share his knowledge—for a small price. Each of Malaabit's heads sees the truth of whatever he observes. His ram head sees the past, his bull head sees the present, and his human head sees the future. He allows news of his oracular abilities to spread far and wide, and as a result, many ambitious people seek him out, hoping to accomplish great deeds in their lifetime. Once a deal for his services is reached—and the requester has made the appropriate sacrifice—Malaabit keeps his end of the deal. And he never, ever lies about what he sees.

entries.name: Small Sacrifices
entries: Unlike many fiends, Malaabit isn't interested in anything as tawdry and ephemeral as mortal souls. Instead, he requires a blood sacrifice of his choosing before he will tell a person what the future holds for them, or how their past has come to inform their present. When he is asked for a reading, he informs the petitioner exactly who has to die for them to get what they want. The deaths Malaabit orders are never morally reprehensible to the specific petitioner. He asks holy warriors to slay murderers and despots; he asks blackguards to murder innocents. When they investigate the person they have been asked to slay, prophecy seekers find they are just as repugnant or virtuous as the fiend told them.

entries.name: Master of Nothing
entries: Malaabit desires nothing less than the utter destruction of reality. The executions he requests all serve his ultimate goal of bringing about the end of everything, one insignificant-seeming death at a time.

entries.name: Chimeric Appearance
entries: Those who encounter Malaabit sometimes assume he is a unique form of {@creature chimera} due to his tripartite appearance. Malaabit does not make such claims, nor does he represent himself as such a creature, but he secretly revels in the ability of mortals to deceive themselves.

entries.name: Malaabit in Southlands
entries: Malaabit allows mortals to view him as a herald of Thoth-Hermes, though he never claims the title directly. He makes his home in the Mbazha Mountains of the Southlands, though in a nomadic way: He frequently moves from one cave complex to another, to prevent too many petitioners from approaching him in too short a period of time.

entries.name: Malaabit's Lair
entries: Malaabit doesn't have a den on the Lower Planes, instead making his home exclusively on the Material Plane. He moves frequently to avoid being too easily locatable, but he favors the ruins of ancient places that were suffused in magic or deep cave complexes that hold arcane radiation. When encountered in his lair, Malaabit has a challenge rating of 14 (11,500 XP).

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The malmbjorn has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Metal Sense
entries: The malmbjorn can pinpoint, by scent, the location of any Small or larger ferrous metal, such as an iron deposit or steel armor, within 100 feet of it. It can sense the general direction of such metal within 1 mile.

trait.name: Tunneler
entries: The malmbjorn can burrow through solid rock at half its burrow speed and leaves a 15-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

action.name: Mulitattack
entries: The malmbjorn makes one Bite attack and two Adamantine Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}34 ({@damage 5d10 + 7}) piercing damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 18}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, and the malmbjorn can't Bite another target.

action.name: Adamantine Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}29 ({@damage 5d8 + 7}) slashing damage. This attack deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Metal Volley {@recharge 5}
entries: The malmbjorn shakes itself, launching shards of metal from its hide. Each creature within 20 feet of the malmbjorn must make a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw, taking 45 ({@damage 13d6}) slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Metal Eater
entries: The malmbjorn swallows one Medium or smaller ferrous metal object within 5 feet of it. If the object is being held or worn by a creature, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Strength saving throw or the malmbjorn swallows the object. If the creature holding or wearing the object is also {@condition grappled} by the malmbjorn, it has disadvantage on this saving throw. Nonmagical objects are digested and destroyed at the start of the malmbjorn's next turn. Magic objects remain intact in the malmbjorn's stomach for 8 hours then are destroyed. Artifacts are never destroyed in this way.

reaction.name: Ironhide
entries: When the malmbjorn is hit by a weapon made of metal, fur-like spikes of metal grow out of the malmbjorn's hide until the end of its next turn. While the spikes remain, the malmbjorn's AC increases 2, and any critical hit against it becomes a normal hit.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The ooze can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Eye-Watering Stench
entries: The manure ooze gives off an odor so pungent it can bring tears to the eyes. A creature that starts its turn within 20 feet of the ooze must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or have disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Flammable
entries: If a manure ooze takes fire damage, it bursts into flame until the end of its next turn. When it hits with a Pseudopod attack while on fire, the Pseudopod deals an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The manure ooze doesn't require sleep.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Digest Corpse
entries: The manure ooze dissolves a corpse in its space that is less than 1 week old. It regains {@dice 1d6} hp per size category of the creature it consumes. For example, it regains {@dice 1d6} hp when consuming a Tiny creature or {@dice 4d6} hp when consuming a Large creature. The corpse is then destroyed, and this action can't be used on it again.

reaction.name: Split
entries: When a manure ooze that is Medium or larger is subjected to slashing damage, it splits into two new oozes if it has at least 10 hp. Each new ooze has hp equal to half the original ooze's rounded down. New oozes are one size smaller than the original ooze.
Miniscule particles and larger unidentifiable chunks punctuate this sludgy, brown mass.

Sometimes putrid spirits in refuse, water, and fertilizer combine and lurch into a primordial form of life, especially in farmland where the soil itself has absorbed vast amounts of arcane energy, such as ancient battlegrounds or the sites of crumbled, mystical artifacts. Manure oozes consume and grow from that energy, and if left unchecked, they can increase their mass and volume to that of a large pond.

entries.name: Corrosive Heaps
entries: Manure oozes pose a considerable threat to farm hands, shepherds, and others who work in the countryside or farmlands. They can lie still for days, looking no different than any other pile of compost, until they kill and digest a farmer or grab unattended livestock. When the ooze runs out of food, it moves on to the next farm and begins its cycle again.

entries.name: Rich Remains
entries: Despite the danger they present, manure oozes can be surprisingly beneficial to a farm. As it moves, a manure ooze leaves a sticky, nutrient rich trail behind it. Plants sown in such soil grow faster and larger than plants grown with normal compost or fertilizer. Similarly, when a manure ooze is destroyed, it enriches the land as its decomposing form revitalizes the soil, resulting in healthy, lush gardens.

entries.name: Manure Oozes in Midgard
entries: Manure oozes are most commonly encountered in the lush farmlands of the Magocracy of Alain. A few families with some magical ability have taken on the dangerous but very lucrative occupation of breeding and raising manure oozes. Properties that house breeding facilities keep the nature of their business a secret as much to avoid depredation from their competitors as to avoid causing a panic amongst the common folk.

trait.name: Keen Sight
entries: The meerkat has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

trait.name: Snake Hunter
entries: The meerkat has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned}.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}1 piercing damage.

trait.name: Grassland Camouflage
entries: The megantereon has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in grassland terrain.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The megantereon has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Pounce
entries: If the megantereon moves at 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits with a Claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the megantereon can make one Bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Running Leap
entries: With a 10-foot running start, the megantereon can long jump up to 20 feet.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) piercing damage. If the target is a creature other than an Undead or Construct, it must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or lose 2 ({@damage 1d4}) hp at the start of each of its turns due to a bleeding wound. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Any creature can take an action to stanch the wound with a successful {@dc 12} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check. The wound also closes if the target receives magical healing.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The merchant captain casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The merchant captain uses Fast Talk. It then makes three Rapier or Quip attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Rapier
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Quip
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 7} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) psychic damage.

action.name: Fast Talk
entries: The merchant captain baffles a creature it can see within 30 feet of it with a barrage of jargon, quick speech, and big words. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw or have disadvantage on the next Wisdom saving throw it makes before the end of the merchant captain's next turn.

reaction.name: Inspiration (4/Day)
entries: When a creature within 30 feet of the merchant captain fails an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, the captain can force it to reroll the die. The target must use the new roll.

trait.name: Changing Form
entries: The midnight sun's form automatically changes based on the presence or absence of light in its space.

items.name: Day Form.
items.entry: While more than half the midnight sun is in darkness, it appears as a glowing orb of light, though it sheds no light. In this form, it deals radiant damage, has immunity to radiant damage, and has vulnerability to necrotic damage.

items.name: Night Form.
items.entry: While more than half the midnight sun is in bright or dim light, it appears as an orb of darkness. In this form, it deals necrotic damage, has immunity to necrotic damage, and has vulnerability to radiant damage.

items.name: Twilight Form.
items.entry: While half the midnight sun is in bright or dim light and the other half is in darkness, it appears as a split orb of light and darkness. In this form, it deals force damage.

trait.name: Energy Being
entries: The midnight sun can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing, and it can enter a hostile creature's space and stop there. The first time it enters a creature's space on a turn, that creature takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) damage of the type determined by the sun's current form.

trait.name: Reality Inversion
entries: Reality is inverted for creatures close to the midnight sun. When a creature starts its turn in the sun's space or within 5 feet of the sun, any circumstance, trait, or feature that would grant it advantage, instead grants it disadvantage, and vice versa, until the start of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The midnight sun makes three Energy Blast attacks.

action.name: Energy Blast
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) damage of the type determined by the sun's current form.

action.name: Energy Pulse {@recharge 5}
entries: The midnight sun emits a pulse of energy. Each creature within 15 feet of the midnight sun must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking 28 ({@damage 8d6}) damage of the type determined by the sun's current form on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Mindleech Aura
entries: When a {@condition charmed} creature enters a space within 15 feet of the mindshard on a turn or starts its turn within 15 feet of the mindshard, that creature takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) psychic damage.

trait.name: Translucent
entries: The mindshard is {@condition invisible} to creatures more than 60 feet away from it.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The mindshard makes two Refracted Light Beam attacks.

action.name: Refracted Light Beam
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) radiant damage.

action.name: Light Construction {@recharge 5}
entries: The mindshard bends light toward a point it can see within 60 feet of it, creating a colorful pattern on that point. Each creature within 20 feet of that point must make a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) psychic damage and is {@condition charmed} and {@condition incapacitated} for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition charmed} or {@condition incapacitated}. A {@condition charmed} and {@condition incapacitated} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Refract Mind (1/Day)
entries: The mindshard pulls a Humanoid with 0 hp into its body, refracting the creature into fragments of itself. The Humanoid dies and {@dice 2d4} cultists appear in unoccupied spaces within 15 feet of the mindshard. The cultists, which share the Humanoid's appearance and memories, act as allies of the mindshard and obey its telepathic commands. A Humanoid must have an Intelligence score of 5 or higher to be refracted in this manner.

reaction.name: Enthralling Defense
entries: When a creature the mindshard can see within 30 feet of it hits it with an attack, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 14} Charisma saving throw or be {@condition charmed} until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Devil's Sight
entries: Magical darkness doesn't impede the moldering devil's {@sense darkvision}.

trait.name: Field Hopper
entries: The moldering devil can fly up to 40 feet on its turn, but it must start and end its movement on a solid surface, such as a roof or the ground. If it is flying at the end of its turn, it falls to the ground and takes falling damage.

trait.name: Rotting Death
entries: When the moldering devil dies, all foodstuffs, water, and beverages within 100 feet of it are subjected to the devil's Touch of Rot trait.

trait.name: Touch of Rot
entries: Any foodstuff, water, or beverage, whether fresh or preserved, that comes into contact with the moldering devil immediately decays and becomes inedible or undrinkable. If a creature consumes such food or drink, it must make a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 24 hours. On a success, it takes half the damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 1 hour.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) necrotic damage.

Scabrous flesh and tufts of bristly hair sheath this devil from the tip of its piglike snout to the ends of its well-muscled legs. A pair of insectile wings lies flat against its back, and rows of needle teeth fill its gaping maw.

Moldering devils are a fiendish blight on the farmlands and grasslands. A region rarely suffers from the presence of just one of the pests, as they tend to arrive in an area in groups numbering a dozen or more.

entries.name: Devilish Hands
entries: Moldering devils are rarely the architects of their own mischief. They act at the command of a more powerful fiend, usually a {@creature spined devil} or {@creature bearded devil}, though occasionally a favored {@creature imp} is given command. Often their presence frightens a farming community into handing control over to an infernal overseer, who then exercises its influence over the populace to increase its personal power.

entries.name: Dealers of Despair
entries: A lone moldering devil can ruin a farmstead in a single season. A group of the fiends can ruin a village in the same amount of time, turning it into a ghost town. On a personal level, moldering devils enjoy exchanging the promise of their absence for the souls of the mortals they would otherwise victimize. They have a morbid sense of humor, and they relish pitting mortals against one another. A moldering devil might grant a mortal the opportunity to alleviate the mortal's suffering by providing the names and locations of rival farmers for the devil to target instead.

entries.name: Moldering Devils in Midgard
entries: Moldering devils hail from Bzeletalet, the Buzzing Hell of Insects, or less often from Fleggesun, the Fetid Hell of Cannibal Rot. In the mortal realms, they are often encountered alongside tosculi raiders and accompanied by fiendish wasps and locusts. Wealthy farming communities, such as those bordering the River Nuria, find the threat of moldering devils so great, they hire mercenaries to protect all nearby farmland the moment a single moldering devil is spotted.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While motionless, a monkey's bane vine is indistinguishable from a normal vine.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The monkey's bane vine makes two Tendril attacks.

action.name: Tendril
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 14}). Until this grapple ends, the creature is {@condition restrained} and takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns. The monkey's bane vine has two tendrils, each of which can grapple only one target.
Plump, orange fruit hangs from deep green vines wrapped around a great tree.
These deadly, semimobile carnivorous plants superficially resemble normal vines. Monkey's bane vines are found in forests and jungles, growing around the trunks of towering rainforest trees.

entries.name: Sweet Fruit
entries: In spring, the vines grow colorful blooms, and in late summer they produce tasty-looking orange fruit that attracts prey. The fruit has a sickly-sweet flavor that monkeys find irresistible; the nimble creatures are often willing to risk being grabbed by the plant's tendrils to snatch the tasty fruit.

entries.name: Food for the Roots
entries: When a creature gets too close, the vine strikes, grabbing hold with its tendrils and either ripping its prey apart or crushing the life out of it. The vine deposits the remains near its roots, where they fertilize the plant.

entries.name: Living Traps
entries: Local humanoids aware of the vine's carnivorous nature sometimes take advantage of the plant. They will grow a monkey's bane vine over a hidden treasure cache, the entrance to a tomb, or other locale as a defense against interlopers and thieves. The most reclusive and paranoid might even grow the vines near entrances to their homes as snares for would-be intruders. In areas where troops of monkeys become nuisances and infest cities and towns, these vines are sometimes deliberately cultivated to help control the monkey population.

entries.name: Risky Harvest
entries: While monkeys greatly desire the vine's fruit, it is too sweet for most humanoids. Still, some humanoids produce a liqueur from the juice of the fruit, and the resulting monkeybane brandy is popular among wealthy gnomes, goblins, orcs, and humans. Those willing to risk the danger of the vines can sell the results at a steep price.

trait.name: Lubricious Plumage
entries: The moon weaver's skin exudes an oil that permeates its plumage. The moon weaver can't be {@condition restrained} by magical or nonmagical webbing, and it ignores all movement restrictions caused by webbing.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Emboldening Song (2/Day)
entries: The moon weaver delivers a burst of beautiful song that motivates and emboldens one friendly creature the moon weaver can see within 60 feet of it. If the target can hear the song, it gains one Embolden die, a d6. Once within the next 10 minutes, the target can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes.

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with silvered weapons

trait.name: Deathly Origins
entries: The moonless hunter can be turned and can take damage from holy water as if it was Undead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The moonless hunter has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Nightmare Leap
entries: Once on its turn, the moonless hunter can use half its movement to step magically into the dreams of a sleeping creature within 5 feet of it. It emerges from the dreams of another sleeping creature within 1 mile of the first sleeper, appearing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the second sleeper. Each sleeper then must succeed on a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw or suffer one level of {@condition exhaustion}, as the resulting nightmares prevent restful sleep. A creature that fails this saving throw by 5 or more also suffers {@table long-term madness}.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The moonless hunter can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the moonless hunter has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The moonless hunter uses Briny Embrace, if it has a creature {@condition grappled}. It then makes two Claw attacks or one Bite attack and one Claw attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 14}). The moonless hunter has two claws, each of which can grapple only one target.

action.name: Briny Embrace
entries: The moonless hunter fills the lungs of one creature {@condition grappled} by it with seawater. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or begin {@quickref suffocating||2}. A {@quickref suffocating||2} but conscious creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. The effect also ends if the creature escapes the grapple.

action.name: Whispered Terrors {@recharge 5}
entries: The moonless hunter bombards the minds of up to three creatures it can see within 60 feet of it with nightmares. Each target must make a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 18 ({@damage 4d8}) psychic damage and is {@condition frightened} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition frightened}. If a creature fails the saving throw by 5 or more, it also suffers {@table short-term madness}.

trait.name: Alien Nature
entries: The moonweb doesn't require air or sleep.

trait.name: Transparent
entries: A moonweb has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks while motionless or in dim light.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The moonweb makes one Dissolving Bite attack and one Tendrils attack.

action.name: Dissolving Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) acid damage, and the moonweb regains hp equal to the acid damage dealt.

action.name: Tendrils
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition paralyzed} for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Etheric Pulse {@recharge 6}
entries: The moonweb releases a burst of ethereal energy. Each creature within 30 feet of the moonweb must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or become partially ethereal. A partially ethereal creature's attacks deal normal damage to the moonweb, even if the attacks are nonmagical, but all other creatures have resistance to the partially ethereal creature's nonmagical damage. In addition, the moonweb can pinpoint the location of the partially ethereal creature, and the moonweb has advantage on attack rolls against the creature. A partially ethereal creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Ethereal Jaunt
entries: The moonweb magically shifts from the Material Plane to the Ethereal Plane, or vice versa.

monster.name: Moppet
languages: understands one language known by its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The moppet doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the moppet remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal doll.

trait.name: Psychic Pool
entries: The moppet absorbs psychic energy from creatures in its waking nightmare, holding up to four Psychic Points at a time. As a bonus action while casting a spell within 5 feet of the moppet, the moppet's owner can expend these points to cast the spell without expending a spell slot. To do so, the owner must expend a number of Psychic Points equal to the spell's level.

action.name: Psychic Burst
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) psychic damage. If the moppet scores a critical hit, it gains one Psychic Point.

action.name: Waking Nightmare
entries: Each creature with an Intelligence of 3 or higher within 60 feet of the moppet must make a {@dc 13} Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, the creature enters a collective fugue state with all other creatures that failed the save. The creature is {@condition unconscious}, but it doesn't drop anything it is holding or fall {@condition prone}. All creatures in this state are faced with a hostile nightmare creature. The nightmare creature uses the statistics of a brown bear, except it deals psychic damage instead of piercing or slashing damage. The appearance of the nightmare creature is up to the GM but should reflect the mutual fears of the affected creatures.

A fight with the nightmare creature works as normal, except all the action takes place in the minds of the affected creatures. When an affected creature is reduced to 0 hp, the moppet gains one Psychic Point. If the nightmare creature, or the moppet, is reduced to 0 hp, all creatures in the fugue state awaken. Any spell slots or class features used in the nightmare combat are expended, but magic item charges or uses aren't expended.

If a creature in the fugue state takes damage from a source other than the nightmare creature, it can immediately repeat the saving throw with advantage, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the moppet's Waking Nightmare for the next 24 hours.

bonus.name: Nimble Moves
entries: The moppet takes the {@action Dash} or {@action Disengage} action.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The mortifera can breathe air and water.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the mortifera remains motionless and submerged in water, it is indistinguishable from a bed of lotus flowers.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The mortifera has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Poisonous Tendrils
entries: A creature that starts its turn {@condition grappled} by the mortifera must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. A {@condition poisoned} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The mortifera makes one Fanged Tentacles attack and two Slam attacks, or it makes three Slam attacks. It can replace two Slam attacks with a use of Chomp.

action.name: Fanged Tentacles
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) poison damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, and the mortifera can't use its Fanged Tentacles on another target.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d12 + 4}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Chomp
entries: One creature {@condition grappled} by the mortifera is pulled up to 5 feet toward the mortifera's central maw, which chomps down on the creature. The target must make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) piercing damage and 13 ({@damage 3d8}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Poison Spray {@recharge 5}
entries: The mortifera sprays poison from its central maw in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 27 ({@damage 6d8}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

resist.note: from attacks not made with cold iron or cedarwood weapons

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Mother Moth casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 17}):

trait.name: Cedar Weapons
entries: Mother Moth is vulnerable to weapons made from the wood of the cedar tree. When she is hit by a weapon made of cedarwood, Mother Moth takes damage as if the weapon was magical.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Mother Moth fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Mother Moth has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Rejuvenating Chrysalis
entries: If killed while within a forest on the Plane of Shadow, a chrysalis appears within the confines of the forest, and Mother Moth emerges from it after 48 hours, regaining all her hp and becoming active again. Only the complete destruction of her chrysalis (AC 10, hp 30) prevents this trait from functioning.

trait.name: Shimmering Defense
entries: Mother Moth's wings emit a sparkling dust, giving her a slightly blurred appearance. Her AC includes her Wisdom modifier.

trait.name: Silk Sovereignty
entries: Mother Moth can communicate with all silk‑producing beasts as if they shared a language. Silk‑producing beasts cannot willingly attack Mother Moth, though they can be forced to do so through magical means. In addition, Mother Moth ignores movement restrictions caused by webbing and can't be {@condition restrained} by webs or other silk-based material, such as a silk net.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Mother Moth makes three Hornworm Scepter attacks or two Moon Bolt attacks. If she hits one creature with two Hornworm Scepter attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw or lose its lowest-level available spell slot. Mother Moth then regains hp equal to four times the level of the lost spell slot.

action.name: Hornworm Scepter
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) radiant damage.

action.name: Moon Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 9} to hit, range 150 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d10}) radiant damage. If the target is a creature in a form other than its natural form, it takes an additional 5 ({@damage 1d10}) radiant damage and must succeed on a {@dc 17} Charisma saving throw or revert to its natural form. When the moon bolt hits a target, moonlight glows in that space, shedding dim light until the start of Mother Moth's next turn.

action.name: Hundreds Dusting {@recharge 5}
entries: Mother Moth flaps her wings, creating a cloud of blinding powder around her. Each creature within 20 feet of her must make a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 35 ({@damage 10d6}) poison damage and is {@condition blinded} for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition blinded}. A {@condition blinded} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

legendary.name: Cantrip
entries: Mother Moth casts a cantrip.

legendary.name: Moonlit Walk
entries: While in dim light, Mother Moth teleports to an unoccupied space she can see within 100 feet of her. The space she chooses must be in dim light.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Mother Moth uses Spellcasting.
The matriarch's gossamer gown and shawl of shimmery wings float as she strides through the moonlit garden. Wherever the sparkling dust from her wings fell, moths gathered.

Duchess Calpe Belinapter, known by most as Mother Moth, is a regent with subjects but no kingdom. Ousted by the devious political machinations that are commonplace in the courts of the fey, she is bereft of all but her natural holdings. Though her status is in decline, Mother Moth still contends with her fey brethren with clever application of nature magic, an unassuming spy network of beasts and fey, and an abundance of patience.

entries.name: Partisan Without a Parlor
entries: Mother Moth, despite having lost many of her noble virtues, still retains several of her titles. Her subjects know her as Queen-Mother of Moondust and Starlight, Lady Lacewing, and Her Mottled Majesty, and deferring to her in this way is sure to earn her favor most of the time. She communicates to her followers and rivals via delivered letter and is well known for her mercurial and varied tones of address.

entries.name: Monarch of Metamorphosis and Silk
entries: Those who suffer lycanthropy in its many forms often seek out Mother Moth for her wisdom, compassion, and, sometimes, cure. Mother Moth's command and regal presence over moths, spiders, and other silk-producing creatures is so absolute that many often wonder if she is in fact an animal lord and not a fey lady. Any who present such questions before her or even within her realm provoke a swift and decisive rebuke.

entries.name: Mother Moth in the Shadow Realm
entries: According to the shadow fey, Mother Moth is an outcast who has no claim of land or territory, having lost such entitlements long ago. She uses the shadow roads to travel between the densest forests the way a moth flutters amongst flowers. It is rumored that Mother Moth was once the mother of the Moonlit King. She has never confirmed nor denied these rumors, and the Moonlit King is busy building his new Court on the Plane of Shadow. Many believe that she now cavorts with Baba Yaga, tempering the Grandmother's wilder urges. The gnomes of the Wormwood have a special relationship with Mother Moth, often leaving offerings in webs and burst cocoons to seek her favor in hope of intervention with Baba Yaga's ire.

entries.name: The Mother's Lair
entries: Mother Moth resides within the Silken Sanctuary, a mixed grove of young and elder trees cocooned in thick silk in the Plane of Shadow. The sanctuary is a small and modestly furnished place that travels as the Mother Moth sees fit, never venturing beyond the forest borders in which she currently resides. Beasts and fey with more neutral alignments are drawn to the lair, feeling a compulsion to protect it. When encountered in her lair, Mother Moth has a challenge rating of 12 (8,400 XP).

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The mudmutt can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Hop By
entries: The mudmutt doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks} when it jumps out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The mudmutt has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the mudmutt's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The mudmutt's long jump is up to 20 feet and its high jump is up to 10 feet, with or without a running start.

trait.name: Swamp Camouflage
entries: The mudmutt has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in swampy terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The mudmutt makes one Bite attack and one Kick attack. It can replace one Bite attack with one Sticky Tongue attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Kick
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Sticky Tongue
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 20 ft., one creature. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 15 feet toward to the mudmutt.

action.name: Sonic Croak {@recharge 5}
entries: The mudmutt unleashes an earpiercing croak in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 18 ({@damage 4d8}) thunder damage and is {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition stunned}. Creatures submerged in water have disadvantage on the saving throw and take 27 ({@damage 6d8}) thunder damage instead of 18 ({@damage 4d8}).

trait.name: Central Stalk Weakness
entries: The mulcher's central stalk lies hidden within the mulcher's fungal mass. When exposed, the central stalk can be attacked and destroyed (AC 13; hp 50; vulnerability to acid, cold, and fire damage). If the central stalk is destroyed, the mulcher has disadvantage on attack rolls and can't use Expose Stalk until it regrows a new central stalk at the end of its next long rest.

trait.name: Disturbed Soil (Exposed Stalk Only)
entries: The ground within 20 feet of the mulcher is difficult terrain.

trait.name: Mulcher Pit
entries: If the mulcher burrows at least 20 feet straight toward a creature, it can dig a 10-foot diameter, 20-foot-deep pit beneath the creature. Each Large or smaller creature in the pit's area must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or fall into the mycelium-lined pit and land {@condition prone}, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage from the spiked mycelium in addition to any falling damage. The mulcher can make one Mycelium Spike attack against a {@condition prone} creature in the pit as a bonus action.

trait.name: Stalk Regeneration (Exposed Stalk Only)
entries: The mulcher gains 15 hp at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 hp.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The mulcher makes two Mycelium Spike attacks.

action.name: Mycelium Spike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}) if the mulcher doesn't have two other creatures {@condition grappled}.

action.name: Excavate
entries: While underground, the mulcher creates a 20-foot square cover on the ground over itself and lurks just below the surface. The cover is identical to the ground around it. When a creature enters the cover's space, the mulcher can make one Mycelium Spike attack against the creature as a reaction, destroying the cover. The target has disadvantage on the saving throw to avoid being {@condition grappled}.

bonus.name: Expose Stalk
entries: The mulcher exposes its central stalk above the ground until the end of its next turn or until it ends the effect as a bonus action. While the central stalk is exposed, the mulcher can't burrow.

bonus.name: Rapid Burrow
entries: The mulcher burrows up to 60 feet. {@action Opportunity attack||Opportunity attacks} against it are made with disadvantage when the mulcher burrows out of an enemy's reach in this way. Creatures {@condition grappled} by it are freed before it burrows.

reaction.name: Emergent Stalk
entries: When the mulcher is reduced to below half its hp maximum or when a creature scores a critical hit against it, the mulcher immediately uses Expose Stalk.
The large orange flower reveals a tall, blue stalk. The petals clamp on a swarm of insects, showing their fleshy, tooth-like nature.
A voracious fungus, the mulcher hides within topsoil, waiting to trap oblivious creatures.

entries.name: Hidden Danger
entries: This massive fungus is as sweeping and bulky as a {@creature dragon turtle}. It uses the ground as cover, where it can ambush prey that travels above it. Alternatively, it can trigger pit traps beneath its prey, impaling the falling creatures on its spike-like mycelium. Once prey has been impaled, the fungus drinks in the victim's blood as greedily as groundwater.

entries.name: Weak Spot
entries: When injured or when it fails to capture sufficient food, the mulcher pushes its fragile central stalk to the surface, releasing insect-attracting pheromones to gain some quick nutrients from the meager prey.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The muraenid has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the muraenid's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Speak with Aquatic Creatures
entries: The muraenid can communicate with Beasts and Monstrosities that have a swimming speed as if they shared a language.

trait.name: Water Breathing
entries: The muraenid can breathe only underwater.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d10 + 3}) piercing damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 13}). Until this grapple ends, the muraenid can Bite only the {@condition grappled} creature and has advantage on attack rolls to do so.

action.name: Telekinetic Grip
entries: One creature the muraenid can see within 60 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be moved up to 30 feet in a direction of the muraenid's choice and be {@condition restrained} until the start of the muraenid's next turn.

If the muraenid targets an object weighing 300 pounds or less that isn't being worn or carried, the object is moved up to 30 feet in a direction of the muraenid's choice. The muraenid can also use this action to exert fine control on objects, such as manipulating a simple tool or opening a door or a container.

action.name: Lord of the Fishes (1/Day)
entries: One Beast with a swimming speed that the muraenid can see within 30 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 12} Wisdom saving throw or be magically {@condition charmed} by the muraenid for 1 day, or until the muraenid dies or is more than 1 mile from the target. The {@condition charmed} target obeys the muraenid's verbal or telepathic commands, can't take reactions, and can telepathically communicate with the muraenid over any distance, provided the two are on the same plane of existence. If the target suffers any harm, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.

monster.name: Mush Marcher
languages: understands Common, plus one language known by its grower, but can't speak

trait.name: Partial Echolocation
entries: The mush marcher's {@sense blindsight} is reduced to 10 feet while {@condition deafened}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The mush marcher makes two Spore-Laced Claw attacks or one Mycelial Harpoon attack and one Spore-Laced Claw attack.

action.name: Mycelial Harpoon
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 15/30 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) piercing damage, and the harpoon sticks in the target if it is a Medium or smaller creature. While the harpoon is stuck, the target takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) piercing damage at the start of each of its turns, the mush marcher can't make Mycelial Harpoon attacks against other targets, and the target and mush marcher can't move further than 30 feet away from each other. A creature, including the target, can take its action to detach the harpoon by succeeding on a {@dc 13} Strength check. Alternatively, the mycelial thread connecting the mush marcher to the harpoon can be attacked and destroyed (AC 12; hp 10; vulnerability to thunder damage; immunity to bludgeoning, poison, and psychic damage), dislodging the harpoon into an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target and preventing the mush marcher from using Recall Harpoon.

action.name: Spore-Laced Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) poison damage.

action.name: Slowing Spores {@recharge 5}
entries: The mush marcher releases spores from its cap. Each creature within 20 feet of the mush marcher must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 18 ({@damage 4d8}) poison damage and is {@condition poisoned}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition poisoned}. While {@condition poisoned} in this way, a creature's speed is halved. A {@condition poisoned} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Recall Harpoon
entries: The mush marcher pulls on the mycelial threads connecting it to its harpoon, returning the harpoon to its empty hand. If the harpoon is stuck in a creature, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 15 feet toward the mush marcher.
A hulking red and white mushroom stomps forward. As it patrols, the gills beneath its cap taste the air for any sign of movement.

When a ritual is performed on a patch of moondrop agaricus on the night of a full moon, the fruiting bodies of these rare mushrooms begin a slow transformation. By the next full moon, between one and three of these mushrooms sprout to a great size, uprooting themselves with three thick legs and patrolling the nearby area, awaiting orders.

entries.name: Loyal Minions
entries: Mush marchers are friendly and loyal to their grower, following their orders almost instinctively. If its grower is especially neglectful or cruel, a mush marcher abandons them, setting out to find a new purpose in life, becoming hostile to its grower only as a last resort. While obscure, the ritual to create mush marchers overlaps many disciplines, and {@class druid||druids}, alchemists, and {@class wizard||wizards} might stumble across the ritual needed to create them. Thus, mush marchers can be found in druidic groves, wizard towers, priestly caverns, or hidden laboratories in cities and villages.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Musk (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: The musk deer emits a pungent musk around itself for 1 minute. While the musk is active, a creature that starts its turn within 10 feet of the deer must succeed on a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. A {@condition poisoned} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

For 1 hour after a creature fails this saving throw, other creatures have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) and Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks to find or track the creature, unless the creature spends at least 10 minutes washing off the musk.

bonus.name: Sprinter
entries: The musk deer takes the {@action Dash} action.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The myrmex doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The myrmex has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Limited Telepathy
entries: The myrmex can magically transmit simple messages and images to any creature within 120 feet of it that can understand a language. This form of telepathy doesn't allow the receiving creature to telepathically respond.

trait.name: Stone Walk
entries: Difficult terrain composed of earth or stone doesn't cost the myrmex extra movement.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The myrmex makes three Bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Earth Shift
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 60 ft., one target in contact with the ground. {@h}The target is pushed up to 15 feet in a direction of the myrmex's choice, and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Wall of Earth
entries: The myrmex can make a wall of earth spring out of earth or rock on a point the myrmex can sense within 30 feet of it. This action works like the wall of stone spell, except the myrmex can create only one 10-foot-by-10-foot panel and the panel has AC 13 and 15 hp.

bonus.name: Earth Manipulation
entries: The myrmex can manipulate and move earth within 30 feet of it that fits within a 5-foot cube. This manipulation is limited only by the myrmex's imagination, but it often includes creating caricatures of creatures to tell stories of travels or etching symbols to denote dangerous caverns or similar markers for those in the colony. The myrmex can also choose to make the ground within 10 feet of it difficult terrain or to make difficult terrain normal, provided the ground is made of earth or stone. Changes caused by Earth Manipulation are permanent.

monster.name: Myrmex Speaker
languages: understands Common, Terran, and Undercommon but can't speak

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The myrmex doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The myrmex has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Stone Walk
entries: Difficult terrain composed of earth or stone doesn't cost the myrmex extra movement.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The myrmex speaker makes three Bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Earth Shift
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 7} to hit, range 60 ft., one target in contact with the ground. {@h}The target is pushed up to 15 feet in a direction of the myrmex's choice, and its speed is halved until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Static Barrage {@recharge 5}
entries: The myrmex speaker blasts foes with painful psychic static. Each creature of the myrmex's choice within 30 feet of it must make a {@dc 15} Intelligence saving throw, taking 18 ({@damage 4d8}) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Wall of Earth
entries: The myrmex can make a wall of earth spring out of earth or rock on a point the myrmex can sense within 30 feet of it. This action works like the wall of stone spell, except the myrmex can create only one 10-foot-by-10-foot panel and the panel has AC 13 and 15 hp.

bonus.name: Earth Manipulation
entries: The myrmex can make a wall of earth spring out of earth or rock on a point the myrmex can sense within 30 feet of it. This action works like the wall of stone spell, except the myrmex can create only one 10-foot-by-10-foot panel and the panel has AC 13 and 15 hp.

vulnerable.note: from magic weapons wielded by good creatures

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The myrmidon casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 12}):

trait.name: Arcane Weapons
entries: The myrmidon's weapon attacks are magical. When the myrmidon hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 2d4} force damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Dedicated Warrior
entries: The myrmidon has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} and {@condition frightened}.

trait.name: Limited Magic Immunity
entries: The myrmidon can't be affected or detected by spells of 1st level or lower unless it wishes to be. It has advantage on saving throws against all other spells and magical effects.

trait.name: Tiger Tag Team
entries: The myrmidon has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one friendly rakshasa is within 5 feet of the creature and that rakshasa isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The myrmidon rakshasa makes two Forked Scimitar or Arcane Bolt attacks.

action.name: Forked Scimitar
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) slashing damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4}) force damage. Instead of dealing damage, the myrmidon can disarm a target wielding a weapon. The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Strength saving throw or its weapon lands in a random space within 10 feet of the target.

action.name: Arcane Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 4d4 + 2}) force damage.

reaction.name: Allied Parry
entries: When a creature the myrmidon can see attacks a creature within 5 feet of it, the myrmidon can impose disadvantage on the attack roll. To do so, the myrmidon must be wielding a shield.

trait.name: False Appearance
