entries: While the nariphon remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary tree.

trait.name: Vegetative Clone
entries: A vegetative clone resembles the creature hit by the nariphon's Thorn attack. Each clone uses the statistics of an awakened tree, except it has the target's size, speed, and any special senses, such as {@sense darkvision}. Clones are extensions of the nariphon, and it can see and hear what a clone sees and hears as if it was in the clone's space. The nariphon can switch from using its senses to using a clone's or back again as a bonus action. The nariphon can have no more than six vegetative clones under its control at one time. Each clone remains until killed or until the nariphon dismisses it (no action required).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The nariphon makes four Roots or Thorns attacks.

action.name: Roots
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 7}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 18}). Until the grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, and it takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) poison damage at the start of each of its turns. The nariphon has four roots, each of which can grapple only one target.

action.name: Thorns
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 12} to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d6 + 7}) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 18} Wisdom saving throw or the thorn falls to the ground and instantly grows into a vegetative clone (see the Vegetative Clone trait) under the nariphon's control.

bonus.name: Bury
entries: One creature {@condition grappled} by the nariphon is knocked {@condition prone}, dragged into the ground, and buried just below the surface, ending the grapple. The buried creature is {@condition restrained} and unable to breathe or stand up. A creature, including the buried creature, can take its action to free the buried creature by succeeding on a {@dc 18} Strength check.

trait.name: Elemental Traveler
entries: The nautiloid doesn't require air, food, drink, sleep, or ambient pressure.

trait.name: Enchanted Shell
entries: A dome of magic covers the nautiloid, just large enough to contain its shell. The nautiloid can control the ambient pressure, temperature, water levels, and breathable air levels (for air-breathing passengers) inside, allowing creatures and objects within it to exist comfortably in spite of conditions outside the nautiloid. Creatures and objects within the shell have total cover against attacks and other effects outside the nautiloid. Creatures inside the shell can exit whenever they want, but nothing can pass into the shell's dome unless the nautiloid allows it. The area inside the dome is a magnificent palace carved into the nautiloid's shell, complete with open 'air' balconies used as entrances or exits, and numerous covered chambers that can comfortably hold up to 50 passengers. The palace is 60 feet long, 30 feet wide, and 80 feet tall. When the nautiloid dies, any creatures inside the dome are expelled into unoccupied spaces near the closest exit.

trait.name: Limited Telepathy
entries: The nautiloid can magically communicate simple ideas, emotions, and images telepathically with any creature inside the magical dome that surrounds it shell. Similarly, it can hear and understand any creature inside the dome, regardless of the language it speaks.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The nautiloid makes two Beak attacks and one Tentacles attack.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 3d10 + 5}) piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) poison damage.

action.name: Tentacles
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d12 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 18}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, the nautiloid can automatically hit the target with its Tentacles, and the nautiloid can't make Tentacles attacks against other targets.

action.name: Jet Propulsion {@recharge 5}
entries: The nautiloid releases a magical burst of pressure that propels it backwards in a line that is 90 feet long and 10 feet wide. Each creature within 15 feet of the space the nautiloid left and each creature in that line must make a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 45 ({@damage 10d8}) force damage and is pushed up to 20 feet away from the nautiloid and knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage, but isn't pushed or knocked {@condition prone}.

reaction.name: Withdraw
entries: When a creature the nautiloid can see targets it with an attack, the nautiloid can pull its body into its shell, gaining a +5 bonus to AC until the start of its next turn.

monster.name: Necrotech Reaver
languages: understands Common and Darakhul but can't speak

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The necrotech reaver deals double damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The necrotech reaver doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Unstable Footing
entries: The necrotech reaver has disadvantage on saving throws against being knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The necrotech reaver makes two Chain Lash attacks.

action.name: Chain Lash
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d6 + 4}) slashing damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}). The reaver has four chains, each of which can grapple only one target.

action.name: Bladed Sweep {@recharge 5}
entries: The necrotech reaver swings its chains in a wide arc. Each creature within 15 feet of the reaver must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) slashing damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}. A creature that fails the saving throw by 5 or more is pushed up to 15 feet away from the reaver and knocked {@condition prone}.

monster.name: Necrotech Thunderer
languages: understands Common and Darakhul but can't speak

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The necrotech thunderer deals double damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Turn Resistance
entries: The necrotech thunderer has advantage on saving throws against any effect that turns undead.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The necrotech thunderer doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The necrotech thunderer makes two Trample attacks.

action.name: Trample
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 3d10 + 5}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Ballista Shot
entries: The necrotech thunderer chooses up to two points it can see within 120 feet of it. The thunderer can't choose a point within 10 feet of it. Each creature within 5 feet of a point must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) necrotic damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one. If a creature is within 5 feet of both points, it has disadvantage on the saving throw, but it takes damage from only one effect, not both.

action.name: Concentrated Shot {@recharge 5}
entries: The necrotech thunderer picks a point it can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature within 20 feet of that point must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 35 ({@damage 10d6}) necrotic damage and suffers one level of {@condition exhaustion}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and doesn't suffer a level of {@condition exhaustion}.

trait.name: Commanding Bulk
entries: While the raja is enlarged, each friendly creature within 30 feet of the raja can't be {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

trait.name: Evasion
entries: If the raja is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a saving throw to take only half damage, the raja instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails. The raja can't use this trait while enlarged.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The raja has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects. The raja can't use this trait while enlarged.

trait.name: Reduce
entries: If the raja starts its turn enlarged, it can choose to end the effect and return to its normal size (no action required).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The raja uses its War Bellow. It then makes three Tulwar attacks or four Dart attacks.

action.name: Tulwar
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d8 + 5}) slashing damage, or 14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) slashing damage if enlarged.

action.name: Dart
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 8} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d4 + 5}) piercing damage, or 10 ({@damage 2d4 + 5}) piercing damage if enlarged.

action.name: War Bellow
entries: The raja releases a loud, challenging bellow. Each hostile creature within 60 feet of the raja that can hear the bellow must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw or become {@condition frightened}. A {@condition frightened} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the raja's War Bellow for the next 24 hours.

bonus.name: Enlarge
entries: The raja magically increases in size, along with anything it is wearing or carrying. While enlarged, the raja is Large, doubles its damage dice on weapon attacks (included in the attacks), and makes Strength checks and Strength saving throws with advantage. While enlarged, it also can no longer use the Evasion or Magic Resistance traits. If the raja lacks the room to become Large, this action fails.

trait.name: Icy Wrath
entries: The sorcerer's weapons are imbued with icy magic. When the sorcerer hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 4 ({@damage 1d8}) cold damage (included in the attack). While enlarged, the sorcerer loses this bonus damage, but its AC increases to 16.

trait.name: Reduce
entries: If the sorcerer starts its turn enlarged, it can choose to end the effect and return to its normal size (no action required).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The sorcerer makes three Scepter attacks or two Ice Shard attacks.

action.name: Scepter
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d6 + 1}) bludgeoning damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) cold damage if the sorcerer is not enlarged, or 8 ({@damage 2d6 + 1}) bludgeoning damage if the sorcerer is enlarged.

action.name: Ice Shard
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 6} to hit, range 60/240 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d10}) piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) cold damage.

action.name: Summon Iceberg {@recharge 5}
entries: The sorcerer chooses a point it can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature within a 20 feet of that point must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 11 ({@damage 2d10}) bludgeoning damage and 7 ({@damage 2d6}) cold damage and is {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition restrained}. The sorcerer can't use this action while enlarged.

bonus.name: Enlarge
entries: The sorcerer magically increases in size, along with anything it is wearing or carrying. While enlarged, the sorcerer is Large, doubles its damage dice on weapon attacks (included in the attacks), and makes Strength checks and Strength saving throws with advantage. While enlarged, it also can no longer use the Summon Iceberg action. If the sorcerer lacks the room to become Large, this action fails.

trait.name: Reduce
entries: If the warrior starts its turn enlarged, it can choose to end the effect and return to its normal size (no action required).

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The warrior's long jump is up to 20 feet and its high jump is up to 10 feet, with or without a running start. The warrior can't use this trait while enlarged.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The warrior makes two Mace or Longbow attacks.

action.name: Mace
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) bludgeoning damage, or 9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) bludgeoning damage while enlarged.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) piercing damage, or 12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) piercing damage while enlarged.

bonus.name: Enlarge
entries: The warrior magically increases in size, along with anything it is wearing or carrying. While enlarged, the warrior is Large, doubles its damage dice on weapon attacks (included in the attacks), and makes Strength checks and Strength saving throws with advantage. While enlarged, it also can no longer use the Standing Leap trait. If the warrior lacks the room to become Large, this action fails.

trait.name: Magic Evasion
entries: If the nullicorn is subjected to a spell or magical effect that allows it to make a saving throw to take only half damage, the nullicorn instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

trait.name: Limited Magic Immunity
entries: At the end of the nullicorn's turn, any spell or magical effect with a duration that is affecting the nullicorn, such as hold monster or mage armor, immediately ends.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The nullicorn has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Nullifying Blight
entries: A creature infected with this disease manifests symptoms {@dice 1d4} hours after infection, which include fatigue, light‑headedness, and blurry vision. When an infected creature casts a spell or activates a magic item, an itchy rash appears and spreads as more magic is used. Until this disease is cured, when the infected creature starts its turn wielding or wearing a magic item and each time it casts a spell or activates a magic item, it loses 5 ({@damage 2d4}) hp.

At the end of each long rest, the infected creature must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the hp the creature loses each time it uses magic increases by 2 ({@damage 1d4}). A creature that succeeds on three saving throws recovers from the disease.

trait.name: Sense Magic
entries: The nullicorn senses magic within 120 feet of it at will. This trait otherwise works like the {@spell detect magic} spell but isn't itself magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The nullicorn makes two Hooves attacks and one Void Horn attack.

action.name: Hooves
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Void Horn
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) force damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or contract the nullifying blight disease (see the Nullifying Blight trait).

action.name: Spread Blight {@recharge 5}
entries: The nullicorn's Void horn flashes and emits a brief ripple of darkness. Each creature within 20 feet of the nullicorn must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 18 ({@damage 4d8}) necrotic damage and contracts the nullifying blight disease (see the Nullifying Blight trait). On a success, a creature takes half the da half the damage and doesn't contract the disease.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the oaken sentinel remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary oak tree.

trait.name: Grasping Branches
entries: The oaken sentinel can have up to six Grasping Branches at a time. Each Grasping Branch can be attacked (AC 16; 25 hp; vulnerability to lightning damage; immunity to poison and psychic damage). Destroying a Grasping Branch deals no damage to the oaken sentinel, which can extend a replacement branch on its next turn. A Grasping Branch can also be broken if a creature takes an action and succeeds on a {@dc 16} Strength check against it.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The oaken sentinel makes three Grasping Branch or Rock attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Fling.

action.name: Grasping Branch
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 50 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 3d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 16}). Until the grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained} and takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns, and the sentinel can't use the same Grasping Branch on another target.

action.name: Rock
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 10} to hit, range 60/240 ft., one target. {@h}22 ({@damage 3d10 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Fling
entries: One Medium or smaller creature {@condition grappled} by the oaken sentinel is thrown up to 60 feet in a random direction and knocked {@condition prone}. If a thrown target strikes a solid surface, the target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it was thrown. If the target is thrown at another creature, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw or take the same damage and be knocked {@condition prone}.

monster.name: Oathrot Wraith

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with silvered weapons

languages: the languages it knew in life

trait.name: Aura of Oathbreaking
entries: Any creature that begins its turn within 30 feet of the oathrot wraith must succeed on a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw or become cursed, losing its resolve in important oaths it has taken. While cursed, the creature has disadvantage on Constitution saving throws to maintain concentration and can't add its proficiency bonus to ability checks and saving throws in which it is proficient. On a successful saving throw, the creature is immune to the wraith's Aura of Oathbreaking for 24 hours.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The wraith can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Oathseeker
entries: The oathrot wraith can pinpoint the location of any cleric, paladin, celestial, or other divinely connected creature within 60 feet of it.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the wraith has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Life Drain
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}20 ({@damage 4d8 + 2}) necrotic damage. The target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or its hp maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the damage taken. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its hp maximum to 0.
The wraith drifts forward with a spectral tome attached to its waist by tendrils of shadow.

Denied eternal rest, oathrot wraiths are the tormented spirits of those who broke solemn vows, causing tragedy by doing so. Unrepentant, they now roam the world constantly reminded of their weakness and failure.

entries.name: Chained to their Oaths
entries: Oathrot wraiths stand out from other {@creature wraith||wraiths} by the texts that are tethered to them. These tomes bear the oaths they once swore and later betrayed. If a {@creature wraith} chooses to read the text and reaffirm its oaths, its curse is ended and its soul is freed from undeath. However, most oathrot wraiths are too consumed by self-loathing, pride, or anger to read the texts, seeing the books as constant reminders of their failure.

entries.name: Drawn by Loyalty
entries: Oathrot wraiths can sense the aura of those who live by their word, especially those who draw power from an oath, such as {@class cleric||clerics} and {@class paladin||paladins}. Their presence fills the oathrot wraith with pain and rage, and they quickly move to destroy such reminders of their failures.

entries.name: Beacons of Doubt
entries: Oathrot wraiths are not only punishment for oathbreakers, but also trials for those who live by their oaths. Their very presence tests such people, as the guilt, doubt, and weakness the wraiths bear preys on the minds of those nearby. {@class cleric||Clerics} and {@class paladin||paladins} that survive an encounter with an oathrot wraith consider it a reminder that they must be vigilant in their loyalty, lest they too fall from grace.

monster.name: Obeleric
languages: understands Common and Void Speech but can't speak

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the obeleric remains motionless and in its defensive form, it is indistinguishable from a normal boulder.

trait.name: Ricochet Charge (Defensive Form Only)
entries: If the obeleric moves at least 15 feet straight toward a target, and then hits the target with a Boulder Bash attack on the same turn, the target has disadvantage on the saving throw against being knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the obeleric can move up to 15 feet straight toward a second target without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks} from the first target, and make one Boulder Bash attack against the second target as a bonus action, if the second target is within its reach.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The obeleric makes two Tentacle Slam or Acid Spit attacks.

action.name: Boulder Bash (Defensive Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d10 + 3}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Tentacle Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) acid damage.

action.name: Spit Acid
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) acid damage. A creature hit by this attack takes an additional 3 ({@damage 1d6}) acid damage at the start of its next turn.

bonus.name: Defensive Roll
entries: The obeleric can roll itself into a boulder-like defensive form or back into its tentacled true form. While in defensive form, it has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks, can't make Tentacle Slam or Spit Acid attacks, and can't use Reflux.

reaction.name: Reflux
entries: When the obeleric takes bludgeoning damage, it can vomit acid at one creature within 5 feet of it. That creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take 3 ({@damage 1d6}) acid damage.

trait.name: Lava Walker
entries: The obsidian ophidian can move across the surface of lava as if it were harmless, solid ground. In addition, it can swim through lava at its walking speed.

trait.name: One with Lava
entries: The obsidian ophidian can't be surprised by creatures within 5 feet of lava, and it can sense any creature touching the same pool of lava the ophidian is touching.

trait.name: Sharp Fragments
entries: When the obsidian ophidian takes bludgeoning damage, each creature within 5 feet of the ophidian takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) piercing damage, as shards of obsidian fly out from the elemental's body.

trait.name: Volcanic Rejuvenation
entries: The obsidian ophidian regains 10 hp at the start of its turn if at least part of its body is in contact with lava. If the obsidian ophidian takes cold damage, this trait doesn't function at the start of the obsidian ophidian's next turn. The obsidian ophidian dies only if it starts its turn with 0 hp and doesn't regenerate.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The obsidian ophidian makes two Bite attacks or one Bite attack and one Constrict attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) piercing damage. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or take 5 ({@damage 2d4}) piercing damage at the start of each of its turns, as shards of obsidian lodge in the wound. Any creature can take an action to remove the shards with a successful {@dc 12} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check. The shards shatter and crumble to dust if the target receives magical healing.

action.name: Constrict
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}) if it is a Large or smaller creature. Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained} and takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) slashing damage at the start of each of its turns, and the ophidian can't use Constrict on another target.

action.name: Lava Splash {@recharge 4}
entries: The obsidian ophidian writhes and flails, splashing lava at everything near it. Each creature within 20 feet of the obsidian ophidian must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 21 ({@damage 6d6}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. To use this action, the obsidian ophidian must be touching lava or within 5 feet of lava.

monster.name: Offal Walker
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Gut Lassos
entries: The offal walker can have up to three gut lassos at a time. Each gut lasso can be attacked (AC 13; 5 hp; immunity to poison damage). Destroying a lasso deals no damage to the offal walker, which can extrude a replacement gut lasso on its next turn. A gut lasso can also be broken if a creature takes an action and succeeds on a {@dc 13} Strength check against it.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The offal walker doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The offal walker makes one Lasso attack for each gut lasso it has then uses Reel.

action.name: Lasso
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 13}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained} and the offal walker can't use the same gut lasso on another target.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} +3 reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d6 + 1}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Reel
entries: The offal walker pulls one creature {@condition grappled} by it up to 25 feet straight toward it.

monster.name: Old Salt
languages: the languages it knew in life

trait.name: Experienced Sailor
entries: The old salt has advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to climb while on a sailing ship and on Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) checks to maintain its footing while on a sailing ship.

trait.name: Seaside Rejuvenation
entries: If at least one of the old salt's accusers is still alive or if the old salt's name hasn't been cleared, a destroyed old salt gains a new body in 13 days, regaining all its hp and becoming active again. The new body appears on the beach or dock nearest one of its living accusers.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The old salt doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The old salt makes two Desiccating Slam attacks.

action.name: Desiccating Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) necrotic damage. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or its hp maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its hp maximum to 0.

action.name: Wave of Seawater {@recharge 5}
entries: The old salt throws its hand forward, sending out a wave of seawater in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) bludgeoning damage and begins choking as its lungs fill with seawater. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't choking. A creature that can breathe water doesn't choke from failing this saving throw. A choking creature can make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, coughing up the seawater and ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Cunning Action
entries: The old salt can take the {@action Dash}, {@action Disengage}, or {@action Hide} action.

monster.name: Origami Golem

immune.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with adamantine weapons

languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The origami golem doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: False Appearance (Unfolded Form Only)
entries: While the golem remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary paper screen, tapestry, or similar flat paper or fabric artwork.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form, except Fold.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The golem has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The golem's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Standing Leap (Frog Form Only)
entries: The golem's long jump is up to 30 feet and its high jump is up to 15 feet, with or without a running start.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The origami golem makes three Lacerating Strike or Wing Buffet attacks.

action.name: Lacerating Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d8 + 3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Wing Buffet (Dragon or Swan Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Hazardous Hop (Frog Form Only)
entries: If the golem jumps at least 15 feet as part of its movement, it can then use this action to land on its feet in an unoccupied space it can see. Each creature within 10 of the golem when it lands must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 33 ({@damage 6d10}) bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, it takes half the damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Shredded Breath (Dragon Form Only) {@recharge 5}
entries: The golem exhales a spray of paper fragments in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 31 ({@damage 9d6}) slashing damage and is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition blinded}.

action.name: Trumpeting Blast (Swan Form Only) {@recharge 5}
entries: The golem emits a trumpeting blast in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 31 ({@damage 7d8}) thunder damage and is {@condition deafened} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition deafened}.

bonus.name: Fold
entries: The golem can fold and reshape its papery body to resemble a Large dragon, a Large swan, or a Medium frog. It can use this bonus action again to unfold into a Large, flat piece of decorative fabric or paper. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. It reverts to its unfolded form if it dies.
A vellum tapestry tears away from its wall mounts, then folds itself into the form of a paper dragon the size of a horse.

Origami golems are exquisitely crafted from fine textiles, such as paper, vellum, papyrus, or starched silks. Origami golems serve well as hidden sentries, appearing as expensive, but innocuous screens, wall hangings, artwork, or floor-coverings until they attack.

entries.name: Murderous Masterworks
entries: Some creators have a more sinister role in mind for the origami golem. These creatures use the golems as assassins, sneaking the creatures into areas where the intended victim will be, or delivering it as a gift. The golems then attack when opportunity allows.

monster.name: Painted Phantasm
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The painted phantasm doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The painted phantasm is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The painted phantasm has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The painted phantasm makes two Paint Slash attacks or one Capture Image attack and one Paint Slash attack.

action.name: Capture Image
entries: {@atk ms} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d10}) force damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw or be captured as an image on a flat surface of the phantasm's choice within 60 feet of it. While an image, the target appears on the surface as a painting, drawing, or similar caricature of itself, and it is {@condition incapacitated} and {@condition restrained}. The target can see and hear outside of the surface, but it can't target those outside the surface with spells or cast spells or use features that allow it to leave the surface. If the surface where the target is captured takes damage, the target takes half the damage. If the surface is reduced to 0 hp, the target is freed. A successful {@spell dispel magic} ({@dc 15}) cast on the surface also frees the captured creature.

If the painted phantasm takes 20 damage or more on a single turn, the phantasm must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw at the end of that turn or lose its focus, releasing all captured creatures.

action.name: Paint Slash
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) slashing plus 16 ({@damage 3d10}) poison damage.

action.name: Painting Sensor
entries: While touching one artwork, the painted phantasm can see and hear through another artwork it has previously touched as if it was in that artwork's space, provided the artwork is within 1 mile of it. "Artwork" can mean any painting, drawing, or other design on a flat surface, including a pattern painted on a wall or in a book.

bonus.name: Painting Portal
entries: The painted phantasm uses 10 feet of its movement to step magically into one artwork within its reach and emerge from a second artwork within 100 feet of the first artwork, appearing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the second artwork. If both artworks are Large or larger, the phantasm can bring up to three willing creatures with it as part of this movement.

monster.name: Peat Mummy
languages: the languages it knew in life

trait.name: Bog Melt
entries: A creature afflicted with the peat mummy's bog melt curse can't regain hp, and its hp maximum is reduced by 7 ({@damage 2d6}) for every 24 hours that elapse. In addition, when the cursed creature starts its turn within 30 feet of a creature it can see, it has a {@chance 50} chance of going into a rage until the end of its turn. While in a rage, the cursed creature must attack the nearest creature. If no creature is near enough to move to and attack, the cursed creature stalks off in a random direction, seeking a target for its rage. The curse lasts until it is lifted by a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. If the curse reduces the creature's hp maximum to 0, the creature dies, and its body dissolves into a viscous puddle of goo.

Carnivorous creatures with an Intelligence of 3 or lower that can see or smell the goo must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or drink the goo. After 1 minute, the creature's Bite, Claw, or Slam attack becomes infused with the curse, and any creature hit by that attack must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be cursed with bog melt.

trait.name: Noxious Slurry
entries: A creature that hits the peat mummy with a melee weapon attack that deals slashing damage while within 5 feet of the mummy must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn as noxious fumes and liquefied flesh spray from the wound.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The peat mummy doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The peat mummy makes two Rabid Bite attacks. If the mummy hits one creature with both attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be cursed with bog melt (see the Bog Melt trait).

action.name: Rabid Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) necrotic damage.
Wearing tattered and muddy clothing, the creature looks like a common villager who ran into trouble in the swamp, though its leathery skin and dead eyes betray its undead state.

Peat mummies are the undead remains of innocents who were murdered by drowning in a swamp. The mud of the bog preserves the body while the unquiet spirit festers within it. The resulting mummy is a rage-fueled shell that seeks vengeance against the people it blames for its condition. Since becoming a peat mummy can take decades, the unfortunates who suffer the creature's wrath are often unaware of its existence.

entries.name: Murderers of Murderers
entries: If the parties responsible for the peat mummy's condition are still alive at its return, the undead horror vents its fury on them. Peat mummies are completely single-minded. They feel no remorse about collateral damage, and they always take the most direct route to their prey, even if it brings them into conflict with others. If a peat mummy can't find its killer, it goes on a killing spree that ceases at night, when it returns to its muddy grave, only to begin anew the following day. Even the most talented necromancers can't reason with an enraged peat mummy on the killing path.

trait.name: Echolocation
entries: The pelagic blush worm can't use its {@sense blindsight} while {@condition deafened}.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The pelagic blush worm deals double damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Water Breathing
entries: The pelagic blush worm can breathe only underwater.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The worm makes one Bite attack and one Tail Fin attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 3d8 + 6}) piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw or be swallowed by the worm. A swallowed creature is {@condition blinded} and {@condition restrained}, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the worm, and it takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) acid damage at the start of each of the worm's turns.

If the worm takes 25 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the worm must succeed on a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, which fall {@condition prone} in a space within 10 feet of the worm. If the worm dies, a swallowed creature is no longer {@condition restrained} by it and can escape from the corpse by using 15 feet of movement, exiting {@condition prone}.

action.name: Tail Fin
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d6 + 6}) slashing damage plus 16 ({@damage 3d6 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Red Acid Spume {@recharge 5}
entries: The worm exhales a gout of crimson stomach acid in a 30-foot cone, if the worm is underwater, or a 50-foot line that is 5 feet wide, if the worm is above water. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 31 ({@damage 9d6}) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The pelagic deva can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Angelic Weapons
entries: The pelagic deva's weapon attacks are magical. When the deva hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 4d8} radiant damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: The pelagic deva doesn't require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Living Coral Armor
entries: The pelagic deva's armor is made of living coral. If the deva's armor is damaged, such as from a black pudding's Pseudopod attack, the armor fully repairs itself within 1 minute, provided it wasn't destroyed.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The pelagic deva has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The pelagic deva makes three Trident attacks.

action.name: Trident
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) piercing damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) radiant damage. If the pelagic deva makes a ranged attack with its trident, the trident returns to its hands at the start of its next turn.

action.name: Transforming Touch (5/Day)
entries: The pelagic deva can magically polymorph a willing creature into a giant octopus, hunter shark, or plesiosaurus. The transformation lasts 8 hours, until the target uses a bonus action to transform back into its true form, or until the target dies. Any equipment the target is wearing or carrying is absorbed into its new form.

In the new form, the target retains its alignment and Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores as well as its ability to speak. Its other statistics are replaced by those of its new form, and it gains any capabilities that the new form has but it lacks.

bonus.name: Anoxic Aura (1/Day)
entries: The pelagic deva removes oxygen from the nearby water for 1 minute. Each creature that requires oxygen to live (including air-breathing creatures under the effects of the {@spell water breathing} spell) and that starts its turn within 20 feet of the pelagic deva must succeed on a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw or begin {@quickref suffocating||2}. The deva never suffers the effects of this aura and can choose any number of creatures in the area to ignore its effects.

trait.name: Elemental Demise
entries: If the peri dies, its body disintegrates into a warm breeze, leaving behind only the equipment the peri was wearing or carrying.

action.name: Wind Slash
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) slashing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) lightning damage or thunder damage (the peri's choice).

action.name: Invisibility
entries: The peri magically turns {@condition invisible} until it attacks or uses Storm Wave, or until its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the peri wears or carries is {@condition invisible} with it.

action.name: Storm Wave (1/Day)
entries: The peri throws its arms forward, releasing a blast of stormy wind in a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) lightning damage and 7 ({@damage 2d6}) thunder damage and is pushed up to 10 feet away from the peri. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't pushed.

trait.name: Earth Glide
entries: The permafrost elemental can burrow through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone. While doing so, the permafrost elemental doesn't disturb the material it moves through.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The permafrost elemental doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Frigid Footprints
entries: The ground within 10 feet of the permafrost elemental freezes over and is difficult terrain.

trait.name: Plague Bearer
entries: If the permafrost elemental takes more than 15 fire damage on a single turn, each creature within 10 feet of the elemental must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or becomes infected with the {@i primordial plague} disease. Alternatively, the creature becomes infected with {@disease sewer plague} or {@disease cackle fever} (the elemental's choice) instead.

{@i Primordial plague} takes 1 minute to manifest in an infected creature. After 1 minute, the creature is {@condition poisoned} until the disease is cured. Every 24 hours that elapse, the creature must repeat the saving throw, reducing its hp maximum by 5 ({@damage 1d10}) on a failure. The disease is cured on a success. This reduction lasts until the disease is cured. The creature dies if the disease reduces its hp maximum to 0.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The elemental makes two Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) cold damage.

action.name: Plague-Ridden Pound {@recharge 5}
entries: The permafrost elemental brings both of its fists down, striking the ground and sending shards of ice from its body flying at nearby creatures. Each creature on the ground within 20 feet of the elemental must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) bludgeoning damage and 10 ({@damage 3d6}) cold damage, is knocked {@condition prone}, and becomes infected with {@i primordial plague} (see the Plague Bearer trait). On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone} or infected.
Cracks in the earth form a furrowed brow, deep dimples, and wide mouth in this ice-coated earth elemental.

Hailing from the border between the elemental planes of earth and water, the permafrost elemental is composed of large intersecting pieces of frozen earth coated with thick, white ice. The permafrost elemental moves slowly and purposefully with the knowledge of eons concealed within its rocky form.

entries.name: Primordial Traps
entries: Many diseases from ancient times have been frozen in the permafrost soil. Those bold enough to burn the permafrost elemental—or foolish enough to let its corpse thaw—soon find themselves fighting maladies dating back to when the gods were young.

entries.name: Permanent Hoarfrost
entries: The permafrost elemental exudes an ancient cold that hangs heavy in the air and coats nearby ground with a slick of ice.

trait.name: Celestial Weapons
entries: The pescavitus's weapon attacks are magical. When the pescavitus hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 2d4} radiant damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: The pescavitus doesn't require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The pescavitus has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Piscine Curse
entries: When a creature reduces the pescavitus to 0 hp, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw or be cursed for 5 days. A cursed creature can be detected as a fiend with spells such as detect evil and good. In addition, for the duration, the cursed creature gains only half the benefit of magic that restores hp and gains no benefits from finishing a long rest. A creature that consumes any amount of the flesh of a pescavitus is automatically cursed. The curse can be lifted by a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic.

trait.name: Water Breathing
entries: The pescavitus can breathe only underwater.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4}) radiant damage.

action.name: Air Bubble (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: The pescavitus creates an air bubble around the heads of any number of creatures that are touching it. A creature that benefits from an air bubble can breathe underwater and gains a swimming speed of 20 feet, if it doesn't already have a swimming speed. The air bubbles hold enough air for 24 hours of breathing, divided by the number of breathing creatures that received an air bubble.

action.name: Healing Touch (3/Day)
entries: The pescavitus touches another creature. The target magically regains 5 ({@damage 1d8 + 1}) hp and is freed from any disease, poison, blindness, or deafness.

trait.name: Disease Sense
entries: The pesta hag can pinpoint the location of {@condition poisoned} creatures or creatures suffering from a disease within 60 feet of her and can sense the general direction of such creatures within 1 mile of her.

trait.name: Pestilence Aura
entries: At the start of each of the pesta hag's turns, each creature within 10 feet of her takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) poison damage. If a creature remains within the aura for more than 1 minute, it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or contract a disease of the hag's choice. The disease's saving throw DC is 13, regardless of the disease the hag chooses.

trait.name: Plague Carrier
entries: The pesta hag is immune to diseases.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The pesta hag makes two Pestilence Rake or Poison Bolt attacks.

action.name: Pestilence Rake
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) piercing damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4}) necrotic damage. The target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or its hp maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken, and it contracts sewer plague, sight rot, or another disease of the pesta hag's choice. The disease's saving throw DC is 13, regardless of the disease the hag chooses.

action.name: Poison Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) poison damage.

action.name: Curative Touch (3/Day)
entries: The pesta hag touches another creature. The target magically regains 10 ({@damage 2d8 + 1}) hp and is freed from any disease or poison afflicting it.

action.name: Summon Plague Rats (1/Day)
entries: The pesta hag magically calls {@dice 1d3} swarms of rats. The called rats arrive in {@dice 1d4} rounds, acting as allies of the hag and obeying her spoken commands. The swarms carry a terrible disease. If a creature takes damage from the swarm's Bites, it must succeed on a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw or contract the disease. Until the disease is cured, the creature can't regain hp except by magical means, and the target's hp maximum decreases by 3 ({@damage 1d6}) every 24 hours. If the creature's hp maximum drops to 0 as a result of this disease, it dies. The rats remain for 1 hour, until the hag dies, or the hag dismisses them as a bonus action.
This hideous crone is wrapped in tattered, black garments and carries a large, wooden rake. Her pale skin is blackened on her nose and fingertips, and her exposed flesh shows open, weeping sores.

Pesta hags, also known as plague hags, are physical manifestations of disease. As a pesta hag wanders, disease spreads in her wake, especially where folk antagonize her. A pesta hag will cure diseases from petitioners that come bearing gifts or even on a whim. The gifts demanded vary widely, depending on the hag's mood. She may demand the grandest horse in a lord's stables, for example, but accept a fresh pear or a scarf full of berries from a poor leper. Or she may turn away both without explanation.

entries.name: Ghost Ships
entries: Occasionally, a pesta hag takes passage on a vessel. Inevitably, the crew falls victim to disease. If the entire crew succumbs, the ghost ship continues on its course, often with the body of a determined captain slumped over the wheel to keep the vessel on course. When it docks, the pesta hag slips ashore amid the confusion, leaving the ship and its grisly cargo behind.

trait.name: Fiery Feathers
entries: The phoenixborn sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet.

trait.name: Rebirth (1/Day)
entries: If the phoenixborn is reduced to 0 hp, it erupts in a burst of flame. Each creature within 10 feet of it takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage, and the phoenixborn regains hp equal to the total damage taken.

action.name: Talon
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Hurl Flame
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) fire damage.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The phoenixborn sorcerer casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}):

trait.name: Fiery Feathers
entries: The phoenixborn sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet.

trait.name: Rebirth (1/Day)
entries: If the phoenixborn sorcerer is reduced to 0 hp, it erupts in a burst of flame. Each creature within 10 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage, and the phoenixborn sorcerer regains hp equal to the total damage taken.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The phoenixborn sorcerer makes two Talon or Hurl Flame attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Talon
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) slashing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Hurl Flame
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) fire damage.

action.name: Fire Jet {@recharge 6}
entries: The phoenixborn sorcerer shoots a jet of fire in a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Forest of Pikes
entries: If a pike guard is within 5 feet of at least one pike guard or pike guard captain, it has half cover against ranged attacks.

action.name: Pike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d10 + 2}) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Brace Pike
entries: When a creature enters the pike guard's reach, the pike guard can brace its pike. If it does so, it has advantage on its next attack roll against that creature.

trait.name: Forest of Pikes
entries: If a pike guard is within 5 feet of at least one pike guard or pike guard captain, it has half cover against ranged attacks.

trait.name: Pike Mastery
entries: {@footnote As the Pike Guard.|This doesn't exist in the book.}

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The pike guard captain uses Not One Step Back, if it can. It then makes two Pike attacks.

action.name: Pike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d10 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Not One Step Back! {@recharge 5}
entries: The captain bellows an order, inspiring its subordinates to glory. Each creature of the captain's choice within 10 feet of it becomes immune to the {@condition charmed} and {@condition frightened} conditions for 1 minute. In addition, the captain grants one such creature the Bring It Down reaction for 1 minute, allowing the target to make an {@action opportunity attack} if a pike guard or the captain deals damage to a creature in the target's reach. The captain can share Bring It Down with only one creature at a time. If the captain shares Bring It Down with another, the effect on the previous target ends. These effects ends early if the captain is {@condition incapacitated}.

reaction.name: Brace Pike
entries: When a creature enters the captain's reach, the captain can brace its pike. If it does so, it has advantage on its next attack roll against that creature.

reaction.name: Bring It Down
entries: When a creature within the captain's reach takes damage from a pike guard or pike guard captain, the captain can make one {@action opportunity attack} against that creature.

trait.name: Limited Telepathy
entries: The plague harpy can magically transmit simple messages and images to any creature within 90 feet of it that can understand a language. This form of telepathy doesn't allow the receiving creature to telepathically respond.

trait.name: Virulence
entries: A creature infected with harpy's plague becomes contagious 24 hours after contracting the disease. When a creature starts its turn within 5 feet of the contagious target, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or also contract the harpy's plague disease.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The harpy uses Dirge. It then makes one Bite attack and two Talons attacks, or it makes three Sorrowful Caw attacks. If the harpy hits one Medium or smaller creature with two Talons attacks, the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}). Until this grapple ends, the harpy can automatically hit the target with its Talons, and the harpy can't make Talons attacks against other targets.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or contract the harpy's plague disease. If the target is disheartened and contracts harpy's plague, its hp maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken. Until the disease is cured, the target can't regain hp except by magical means, and the target's hp maximum decreases by 10 ({@damage 3d6}) every 24 hours. If the target's hp maximum drops to 0 as a result of this disease, the target dies.

action.name: Talons
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d10 + 3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Sorrowful Caw
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 6} to hit, range 90 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d6 + 3}) psychic damage.

action.name: Dirge
entries: The harpy telepathically sings a mournful hymn and projects images of sickly and dying loved ones in the mind of one creature it can see within 90 feet of it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be disheartened for 1 minute. While disheartened, a creature has disadvantage on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned} or contracting a disease. The disheartened creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
The creature combines the body of a woman with the head, wings, and talons of a giant vulture. It perches silently, head cocked to the side, studying its surroundings.
The plague harpy is a carrion eater and an aggressive plague carrier. They are found anywhere bodies accumulate, especially those that are diseased.

entries.name: Patiently Awaiting Death
entries: A plague harpy likes its meals dead, and preferably rotten and diseased. The harpies are perfectly content to infect individuals, leaving the creatures otherwise unmolested, and allow them to return to their loved ones. Plague harpies sow the seeds of disease today in the hopes of reaping greater rewards tomorrow.

entries.name: Pain of the Dying
entries: The dirge sung by plague harpies sounds different to each person that hears it and is accompanied by telepathic images of dying friends and family. The harpies hope to instill a state of sorrow and grief with the dirge, making their victims more susceptible to illness.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The planes hunter has advantage on attack rolls against a target if at least one of the hunter's allies is within 5 feet of the target and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Planar Attunement
entries: At the start of each of its turns, the planes hunter chooses one of the following damage types: acid, cold, fire, lightning, necrotic, radiant, or thunder. It has resistance to the chosen damage type until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Planar Weapons
entries: The planes hunter's weapon attacks are magical. When it hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 3d8} damage of the type chosen with Planar Attunement (included in the attack).

trait.name: Planes Hunter
entries: The planes hunter has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) and Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks to find and track Celestials, Fiends, and Elementals.

trait.name: Snaring Blade
entries: If the planes hunter scores a critical hit against a creature, that creature can't use any method of extradimensional movement, including teleportation or travel to a different plane of existence, for 1 minute. The creature can make a {@dc 16} Charisma saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the planes hunter has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The planes hunter makes three Longsword attacks.

action.name: Longsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) slashing damage, or 9 ({@damage 1d10 + 4}) slashing damage if used with two hands, plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) acid, cold, fire, lightning, necrotic, radiant, or thunder damage (as determined by Planar Attunement).

action.name: Warping Whirlwind {@recharge 5}
entries: The planes hunter bends reality around it, slicing nearby creatures with sword strikes from across the planes. Each creature within 10 feet of the planes hunter must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 18 ({@damage 4d8}) slashing damage and 18 ({@damage 4d8}) acid, cold, fire, lightning, necrotic, radiant, or thunder damage (as determined by Planar Attunement) on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Planar Traveler (1/Day)
entries: The planes hunter can transport itself to a different plane of existence. This works like the {@spell plane shift} spell, except the hunter can only affect itself and can't use this action to banish an unwilling creature to another plane.

bonus.name: Planar Step
entries: The planes hunter teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. Glowing swirls of elemental energy appear at the origin and destination when it uses this bonus action.

reaction.name: Parry
entries: The planes hunter adds 4 to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it. To do so, the hunter must see the attacker and be wielding a melee weapon.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The porcillina has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The porcillina has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the porcellina's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Poisonous Flesh
entries: A creature that touches the porcellina or consumes the meat of the porcellina must succeed on a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} for 1 hour.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 11} Dexterity saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. The DC increases by 1 for each other porcellina that hit the target since the end of this porcellina's previous turn, to a maximum of {@dc 15}.

reaction.name: Squeal
entries: When a porcellina is hit with an attack, it can let out a blood-curdling scream. Each creature within 10 feet of the porcellina must succeed on a {@dc 11} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} until the end of its next turn.

monster.name: Primordial Matriarch

trait.name: Elemental Attacks
entries: The primordial matriarch's weapon attacks are magical. When she hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 5d6} damage of the type determined by Roiling Elements.

trait.name: Elemental Aura
entries: At the start of each of the primordial matriarch's turns, each creature within 5 feet of her takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) damage of the type determined by Roiling Elements. A creature that touches the primordial matriarch or hits her with a melee attack while within 10 feet of her takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) damage of the type determined by Roiling Elements. A nonmagical weapon that hits the matriarch is destroyed after dealing damage.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The primordial matriarch doesn't require air, food, drink or sleep.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the primordial matriarch fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The primordial matriarch has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Roiling Elements
entries: At the start of each of her turns, the primordial matriarch chooses one of the following damage types: acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder. The matriarch has immunity to that damage type until the start of her next turn.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The primordial matriarch deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The elemental matriarch uses Storm Blast. She then makes two Elemental Lash attacks.

action.name: Elemental Lash
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 17} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}32 ({@damage 4d10 + 10}) bludgeoning damage plus 17 ({@damage 5d6}) damage of the type determined by Roiling Elements.

action.name: Storm Blast
entries: The primordial matriarch emits elemental energy in a 90-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) damage of the type determined by Roiling Elements and suffers one of the following effects, depending on the damage type. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and doesn't suffer an additional effect, unless specified below.

items.name: Acid Blast
items.entry: On a failed save, the creature is covered in acid. At the start of each of its turns, it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) acid damage until it or another creature takes an action to scrape or wash off the acid.

items.name: Cold Blast.
items.entry: On a failed save, the creature is {@condition restrained} by ice until the end of its next turn, or until it or another creature takes an action to break it free. On a successful save, the creature's speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Fire Blast.
items.entry: On a failed save, the creature catches fire. Until it or another creature takes an action to douse the fire, the creature takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

items.name: Lightning Blast.
items.entry: On a failed save, the creature is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the creature can't take reactions until the start of its next turn.

items.name: Thunder Blast.
items.entry: On a failed save, the creature is {@condition deafened} for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, a {@condition deafened} creature can repeat the saving throw, ending the deafness on itself on a success. On a successful save, the creature is {@condition deafened} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Create Elementals
entries: The primordial matriarch magically creates up to {@dice 3d6} {@filter mephits|bestiary|search=mephit}, 2 {@creature air elemental||air}, {@creature earth elemental||earth}, {@creature fire elemental||fire}, or {@creature water elemental||water elementals}, 2 {@creature ice elemental|ToB2|ice elementals}, or 1 {@creature rockslide elemental|ToB3}. The Elementals arrive at the start of her next turn, acting as allies of the matriarch and obeying her spoken commands. The Elementals remain for 1 hour, until the matriarch dies, or until the matriarch dismisses them as a bonus action. The primordial matriarch can have any number of Elementals under her control at one time, provided the combined total CR of the Elementals is no higher than 10.

reaction.name: Elemental Adaptation
entries: When the matriarch would take acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage, she can change the element selected with Roiling Elements to that type of damage, gaining immunity to that damage type, including the triggering damage.

legendaryHeader: The primordial matriarch can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The matriarch regains spent legendary actions at the start of her turn.

legendary.name: Elemental Change
entries: The primordial matriarch changes the damage immunity granted by Roiling Elements.

legendary.name: Fly
entries: The primordial matriarch flies up to half her flying speed without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

legendary.name: Create Elemental (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The primordial matriarch uses Create Elementals.

legendary.name: Return to Mother (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: The primordial matriarch absorbs an Elemental within 10 feet of her, gaining a temporary hp equal to 10 times the CR of the Elemental absorbed.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The primordial surge doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Life From Death
entries: When the surge dies, it explodes in a burst of raw creation. The area in a 50-foot radius centered on the primordial surge is blanketed in lush flora native to the region, providing enough food to feed a dozen Large or smaller creatures for 6 ({@damage 1d12}) days. Creatures in the area that have been dead no longer than 1 day are returned to life with 4 ({@damage 1d8}) hp.

trait.name: Primordial Form
entries: The primordial surge can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing. A creature that touches the primordial surge or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage (the primordial surge's choice). In addition, the primordial surge can enter a hostile creature's space and stop there. The first time it enters a creature's space on a turn, that creature takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage (the surge's choice). A creature that starts its turn in the primordial surge's space must succeed on a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} and {@condition deafened} until it starts its turn outside the surge's space.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The primordial surge makes three Elemental Strike attacks.

action.name: Elemental Strike
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 15 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. {@h}28 ({@damage 4d10 + 6}) acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage (the primordial surge's choice).

action.name: Primordial Storm {@recharge 6}
entries: A rain of elemental energies falls around the primordial surge, producing one of the following effects:

items.name: Restorative Rain.
items.entry: Each creature within 30 feet of the surge that isn't a Construct or Undead regains 27 ({@damage 5d10}) hp. The area becomes difficult terrain as nonmagical plants in the area become thick and overgrown.

items.name: Ruinous Rain.
items.entry: Each creature within 30 feet of the surge must make a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 27 ({@damage 5d10}) acid damage and 27 ({@damage 5d10}) fire damage and is coated in burning acid. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't coated in acid. A creature coated in acid takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) acid damage and 5 ({@damage 1d10}) fire damage at the start of each of its turns until it or another creature takes an action to scrape or wash off the acid.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The prismatic dragon casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 12}):

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d10 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Light Beam {@recharge 5}
entries: The prismatic dragon emits a beam of white light in a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw, taking 18 ({@damage 4d8}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting (Psionics)
headerEntries: The pyschophant casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 16}):

trait.name: Impenetrable Ego
entries: While the psychophant is conscious and wearing no armor and wielding no shield, it adds its Charisma modifier to its AC (included above) and saving throws. In addition, it has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The psychophant makes three Psychic Strike attacks. If the psychophant hits one creature with two Psychic Strike attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Intelligence saving throw or be {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Psychic Strike
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d6 + 4}) psychic damage.

action.name: Brain Storm {@recharge 6}
entries: The psychophant lashes out with a brutal manifestation of psychic force, wracking its foes' psyches. Each creature within 30 feet of the psychophant must make a {@dc 16} Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 31 ({@damage 9d6}) psychic damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute as it suffers a debilitating headache. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition poisoned}. A {@condition poisoned} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Bend the Spoon
entries: The psychophant chooses one nonmagical weapon it can see within 30 feet of it and magically warps the weapon. If the weapon is being worn or carried, the creature wearing or carrying it can prevent the warping by succeeding on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw. A warped weapon deals only half its normal damage on a hit, and a creature wielding a warped weapon has disadvantage on attack rolls with it. A creature can repair a warped weapon with the {@spell mending} spell or by spending a short rest repairing the weapon with the appropriate tools.

reaction.name: Appeal to the Fervent
entries: When a creature the psychophant can see targets it with an attack, the psychophant calls for aid from its followers, switching places with a friendly creature within 5 feet of it. The friendly creature becomes the target of the attack instead.

Whether by profane pacts, experiments gone awry, or awoken inner potential, psychophants have expanded their minds far beyond the mundane. Their commanding presences and indomitable personalities coupled with great psychic prowess can influence and control others, completely indifferent to the effect on those individuals. Their magnetic personalities draw followers to their sides, and many psychophants become leaders of cults, thieves' guilds, or other illicit organizations, while others dabble in politics. In battle, they compel others to their defense while they launch mental onslaughts against their foes.

trait.name: Death's Accomplice
entries: When the psychopomp deals radiant damage, it can choose to deal necrotic damage instead.

trait.name: Fiendish Countenance
entries: When traveling the planes of existence demons and devils are native to, such as Hell or the Abyss, the psychopomp appears to be a Fiend of a type native to that plane. Until it reveals its true nature (no action required) or uses its Divine Dictum, Spirit Usher, or Unmake Contract features, it is undetectable as a Celestial.

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: The psychopomp doesn't require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The psychopomp has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The psychopomp makes two Lantern Flail attacks.

action.name: Lantern Flail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) radiant damage. A fiend or undead hit by this attack takes an extra 10 ({@damage 3d6}) radiant damage.

action.name: Divine Dictum {@recharge 5}
entries: The psychopomp unleashes a small portion of its creator's influence. Each creature of the psychopomp's choice that it can see within 30 feet of it must make a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw, taking 22 ({@damage 5d8}) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Each {@condition charmed}, {@condition frightened}, or possessed creature of the psychopomp's choice within 60 feet of it can choose to end the condition.

action.name: Unmake Contract (1/Day)
entries: The psychopomp can project the pure power and majesty of its patron deity. A creature the psychopomp can see or hear within 60 feet of it is freed of all liens upon its soul.

bonus.name: Spirit Usher (3/Day)
entries: The psychopomp wards a creature with divine power for 1 hour. While warded, Celestials, Fiends, and Undead have disadvantage on attack rolls against the creature, and the creature can't be {@condition charmed}, {@condition frightened}, or possessed by them. In addition, the warded creature gains 11 temporary hit points and, if slain, can't be raised as an Undead for 1 year.

trait.name: Blessings of the Sea Gods
entries: The puffinfolk has advantage on saving throws while flying over or swimming in ocean waters.

trait.name: Hardy Ocean Explorers
entries: The puffinfolk is considered a Medium creature when determining its carrying capacity. In addition, it can hold its breath for 30 minutes.

trait.name: Oceanic Recall
entries: The puffinfolk can perfectly recall any path it has traveled above or within an ocean.

action.name: Peck
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Quick and Nimble
entries: When a creature the puffinfolk can see targets it with a melee or ranged weapon attack, the puffinfolk darts out of the way, and the attacker has disadvantage on the attack roll.

vulnerable.note: from magic weapons wielded by good creatures

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The pustakam casts one of the following spells, requiring no material or somatic components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: False Appearance (Object Form Only)
entries: While the pustakam remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary object.

trait.name: Limited Magic Immunity
entries: The pustakam can't be affected or detected by spells of 2nd level or lower unless it wishes to be. It has advantage on saving throws against all other spells and magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The pustakam makes two Claw attacks or uses False Promises twice. It can replace one use of False Promises with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Claw (Fiend Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d4 + 2}) slashing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) psychic damage.

action.name: False Promises
entries: The pustakam whispers promises of power and riches in the mind of one creature it can see within 60 feet of it. The target must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 18 ({@damage 4d8}) psychic damage and is {@condition charmed} for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition charmed}.
