While {@condition charmed}, a creature has disadvantage on saving throws against the pustakam's enchantment spells. A {@condition charmed} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The pustakam transforms into a Tiny object or back into its true form, which is a fiend. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

monster.name: Pyrite Pile
languages: understands Dwarvish and Terran but can't speak

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the pyrite pile remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal pile of gold nuggets.

trait.name: Gold Fever
entries: When a Humanoid or xorn that can see the pyrite pile starts its turn within 60 feet of the pyrite pile, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} until the end of its next turn. A {@condition charmed} creature must take the {@action Dash} action and move toward the pyrite pile by the safest available route on its next turn, trying to get within 5 feet of the Elemental.

trait.name: Metal Sense
entries: The pyrite pile can pinpoint, by scent, the location of precious metals within 60 feet of it and can sense the general direction of Small or larger deposits of such metals within 1 mile.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The pyrite pile makes two Slam attacks. If the pyrite pile hits one Medium or smaller creature with both attacks, the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 16}). Until this grapple ends, the target takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns. The pyrite pile can have only one creature {@condition grappled} at a time.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 3d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Hurl Nugget
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 8} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Eat Gold
entries: The pyrite pile absorbs 52 ({@damage 8d12}) gp worth of nonmagical items and coins made of precious metals, ignoring copper, worn or carried by one creature {@condition grappled} by it, and the elemental regains hp equal to half that amount. Absorbed metal is destroyed.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the pyrrhic podthrower remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a clump of crabgrass.

trait.name: Flaming Seedpod Regrowth
entries: The pyrrhic podthrower has four Flaming Seedpods. Used Flaming Seedpods may regrow each turn. If the pyrrhic podthrower starts its turn with less than four Flaming Seedpods, roll a {@dice d6}. On a roll of 5 or 6, it regrows {@dice 1d4} Flaming Seedpods, to a maximum of 4.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The pyrrhic podthrower regains 10 hp at the start of its turn. If the podthrower takes necrotic damage or if it is {@condition grappled} and removed from the ground, this trait doesn't function at the start of its next turn. The podthrower dies only if it starts its turn with 0 hp and doesn't regenerate.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The pyrrhic podthrower makes three Grass Blade attacks, or it uses Flaming Seedpod twice.

action.name: Grass Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Flaming Seedpod
entries: The podthrower lobs a flaming seedpod at a point it can see within 60 feet of it. The seedpod explodes on impact and is destroyed. Each creature within 10 feet of that point must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 12 ({@damage 5d4}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

reaction.name: Unstable Bulb
entries: When the pyrrhic podthrower is {@condition grappled} and removed from the ground while above 0 hp, the unstable root bulb bursts. Each creature within 10 feet of the podthrower must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 12 ({@damage 5d4}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with cold iron weapons

trait.name: Beguiling Aura
entries: The mere sight of the pùca is a temptation for road-weary travelers. Whenever a creature that can see the pùca starts its turn within 30 feet of the pùca, that creature succeed on make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} for 1 minute. A {@condition charmed} creature must take the {@action Dash} action and move toward the pùca by the safest available route on each of its turns, trying to get within 5 feet of the fey horse to mount it. Each time the creature takes damage and at the end of each of its turns, the creature can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the pùca's Beguiling Aura for the next 24 hours.

trait.name: Nightmarish Ride
entries: If a creature mounts the pùca, the creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw or become {@condition paralyzed} and {@condition restrained} by chains on the pùca's back until the next dawn. While it has a rider captured in this way, the pùca takes the {@action Dash} or {@action Disengage} actions on each of its turns to flee with the rider. After it moves 100 feet, it disappears with the captured rider, magically racing along a nightmarish landscape. At the next dawn, it returns to the space where it disappeared or the nearest unoccupied space, the rider lands in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the pùca, and the pùca flees. At the end of the ride, the rider suffers one level of {@condition exhaustion}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The pùca makes two Chain Whip attacks and one Hooves attack.

action.name: Chain Whip
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}) if it is a Medium or smaller creature and the pùca doesn't have two other creatures {@condition grappled}. Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}.

action.name: Hooves
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.
A dark horse draped in thick silver chains emerges from the shadows between the trees, its eyes glowing with an eerie light.

This mysterious equine fey appears at night beside lonely forest roads and tempts weary travelers to ride it. Once upon its back, however, the púca takes the foolish individual on a nightmarish ride through the dark woods before returning them to where they started at dawn, exhausted. Those who have ridden a púca report seeing twisted, otherworldly forests and strange creatures.

entries.name: Redeemable
entries: The púca is a fey creature corrupted by shadow. If a rider who is pure of heart willingly climbs astride the púca, enjoys the ride, and thanks the púca when they return at dawn, the púca is cleansed of the shadow taint, and the chains fall away from its body. The púca is then free to aid lost travelers instead of terrorizing them.

entries.name: Púca in the Shadow Realm
entries: In the Midgard setting and the Shadow Realm, the púca can travel the shadow roads at will. During its nightmarish rides, it crosses in and out of these pathways, allowing riders to glimpse terrifying things. If a rider redeems a púca and asks for its aid, the rider can use the mount to gain access to the shadow roads.

trait.name: Keen Sight and Smell
entries: The quagga has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight or smell.

trait.name: Knife Dancer
entries: When the quagga hits a creature with a Twin Blade attack, it doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks} when it moves out of that creature's reach.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The quagga has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the quagga's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Trampling Charge
entries: If the quagga moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a Twin Blade attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the quagga can make one Hooves attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The quagga makes two Twin Blade attacks and one Hooves attack, or it makes four Javelin attacks.

action.name: Hooves
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d4 + 2}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Javelin
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Twin Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

bonus.name: War Surge
entries: When the quagga reduces a creature to 0 hp with a melee attack on its turn, the quagga can move up to half its speed and make a Twin Blade attack.

monster.name: Queen of Mammoths

trait.name: Elephantine Passivism
entries: No elephant, mammoth, or other elephantine creature can willingly attack the Queen of Mammoths. They can be forced to do so through magical means.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Queen of Mammoths fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The Queen of Mammoths has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The Queen of Mammoths's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Rejuvenation
entries: If she dies, the Queen of Mammoths's soul reforms on the Astral Plane. In {@dice 1d6} days, it inhabits the body of another elephantine creature on the Material Plane, which becomes the Queen of Mammoths with all of the hp and abilities thereof. Only killing every elephantine creature on the Material Plane will prevent this trait from functioning.

trait.name: Speak with Elephantines
entries: The Queen of Mammoths can communicate with any elephant, mammoth, or other elephantine creature as if they shared a language.

trait.name: Trampling Charge
entries: If the Queen of Mammoths moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Gore attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 20} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the queen can make one Stomp attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Queen of Mammoths makes three Battleaxe attacks or two Gore attacks, or she makes one Gore attack and one Stomp attack. She can replace one attack with one Trunk attack or Trunk Slam.

action.name: Battleaxe (Humanoid or True Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}20 ({@damage 3d8 + 7}) slashing damage, or 23 ({@damage 3d10 + 7}) slashing damage if used with two hands.

action.name: Gore (Elephantine or True Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}34 ({@damage 6d8 + 7}) piercing damage.

action.name: Stomp (Elephantine or True Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}34 ({@damage 5d10 + 7}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Trunk (Elephantine or True Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 15 ft., one creature. {@h}The target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 19}) if it is a Large or smaller creature. Until the grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, and the queen can't use her Trunk attack on another target.

action.name: Trunk Slam
entries: One Large or smaller object held or creature {@condition grappled} by the Queen of Mammoths is slammed into the ground or flung away. A creature slammed to the ground must make a {@dc 20} Constitution saving throw, taking 27 ({@damage 5d10}) bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. This doesn't end the {@condition grappled} condition on the target.

A creature flung away is thrown up to 60 feet in a random direction and knocked {@condition prone}. If a thrown creature strikes a solid surface, the target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it was thrown. If the target is thrown at another creature, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 19} Dexterity saving throw or take the same damage and be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Tusk Sweep (Elephantine or True Form Only) {@recharge 5}
entries: The Queen of Mammoths channels raw magic as she sweeps her tusks in a wide arc. Each creature in a 15-foot cube must make a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 35 ({@damage 10d6}) bludgeoning damage and is pushed up to 15 feet away from the queen. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't pushed away.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The Queen of Mammoths transforms into a Gargantuan {@creature oliphaunt|CCodex}, a Huge mammoth, a Medium female human with thick brown hair tied back in a braid, or back into her true Huge elephant-headed humanoid form. Her statistics, other than her size, are the same in each form. Any equipment she is wearing or carrying transforms with her.

reaction.name: Catch Weapon (Elephantine or True Form Only)
entries: When the Queen of Mammoths is hit by a melee weapon attack, she can reduce the damage by {@dice 1d10 + 17}. If this reduces the damage to 0, the queen can catch the weapon with her trunk if she is not using it to grapple a creature.

If the Queen of Mammoths catches a weapon in this way, she must make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check contested by the attacker's Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check (the target chooses the ability to use). The queen has disadvantage on the check if the wielder is holding the item with two or more hands.
If she wins, she disarms the creature and can throw the weapon up to 60 feet in a random direction as part of the same reaction.

legendaryHeader: The Queen of Mammoths can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The Queen of Mammoths regains spent legendary actions at the start of her turn.

legendary.name: Regenerative Hide
entries: The Queen of Mammoths regains 15 hp. She can't use this legendary action again until the end of her next turn.

legendary.name: Trunk
entries: The Queen of Mammoths makes one Trunk attack.

legendary.name: Shoving Stampede (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Queen of Mammoths charges, becoming an unstoppable stampede in a line up to 80 feet long and 15 feet wide. Each creature in the line must make a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure a creature takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) bludgeoning damage and is pushed up to 15 feet away and knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't pushed or knocked {@condition prone}. The Queen of Mammoths's movement along this line doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

legendary.name: Queen's Trumpet (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: The Queen of Mammoths raises her trunk and emits a loud trumpeting that is audible out to 300 feet. She chooses up to three creatures that can hear the trumpeting. If a target is friendly, it has advantage on its next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. If a target is hostile, it must succeed on a {@dc 20} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Earth Glide
entries: The qumdaq can burrow through nonmagical, unworked earth, sand, and stone. While doing so, the elemental doesn't disturb the material it moves through.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The qumdaq doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Final Gift
entries: When the qumdaq dies, it bursts in a shower of sand. A qumdaq of the dying qumdaq's choice within 10 feet of it regains 5 ({@damage 2d4}) hp, or the target gains 5 ({@damage 2d4}) temporary hp if it is at its hp maximum.

trait.name: Horde Tactics
entries: The qumdaq has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the qumdaq's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}. In addition, the qumdaq's weapon attacks deal an extra 2 ({@damage 1d4}) bludgeoning damage on a hit, if at least three of the qumdaq's allies are within 5 feet of the target and the allies aren't incapacitated.

trait.name: Sense Magic
entries: The qumdaq senses magic within 60 feet of it at will. This trait otherwise works like the {@spell detect magic} spell but isn't itself magical.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Desiccation Field (1/Day)
entries: The qumdaq extends its arms, sending sand and grit swirling around itself. Each creature that isn't a qumdaq within 10 feet of the qumdaq must make a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) bludgeoning damage and suffers one level of {@condition exhaustion} for 1 hour, or until it drinks at least 1 pint of water. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't exhausted. A creature can suffer no more than a total of three levels of {@condition exhaustion} from Desiccation Field, regardless of how many qumdaqs use the action.

monster.name: Rafflesian
languages: understands the languages of its host companion but can't speak

trait.name: Symbiote
entries: A rafflesian can bond with a willing Humanoid creature that remains in physical contact with it for 10 minutes. While bonded in this way, the host creature gains a +2 bonus to AC and saving throws and has resistance to all damage, and the rafflesian's speed is 0, moving with the host when it moves. Each time the host takes damage, the rafflesian takes the same amount of damage. If the host is subjected to an effect that would force it to make a saving throw, the host can use its saving throw bonuses or the rafflesian's. If both are subjected to the same effect, only one of them is affected, the rafflesian's choice. The rafflesian and the host otherwise retain their own statistics and take separate turns. The rafflesian and the host can't be separated, unless the rafflesian chooses to terminate the connection. The rafflesian can detach itself safely from a host over the course of 1 hour, or it can detach itself from the creature as an action, which immediately kills the host.

trait.name: Symbiotic Thought
entries: The rafflesian and its host can communicate telepathically while bonded.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The rafflesian makes three Vine Whip attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Blood Root.

action.name: Vine Whip
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d8 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Blood Root
entries: The rafflesian launches a root toward one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take 14 ({@damage 4d6}) necrotic damage as the rafflesian drains blood from it. The rafflesian or the host (the rafflesian's choice) then regains hp equal to the damage taken.

reaction.name: Last Resort
entries: When the host dies, the rafflesian can reanimate it for up to 10 days, provided the host's body is still intact. During this time, the host is protected from decay and can't become undead. The rafflesian can use a bonus action to cause the host to move as it could in life, but the host can't take any other actions. If the host could speak in life, the rafflesian can speak with the host's voice. A host body reanimated in this way can be restored to life by any spell capable of doing so.

monster.name: Rainforest King

immune.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with cold iron weapons

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Rainforest King casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 21}):

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Rainforest King fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The Rainforest King has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The Rainforest King's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Speak with Beasts and Plants
entries: The Rainforest King can communicate with Beasts and plants as if they shared a language.

trait.name: Tree Stride
entries: The Rainforest King can use 5 feet of his movement to step magically into one living tree within his reach and emerge from a second living tree within 500 feet of the first tree, appearing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the second tree. Both trees must be Large or bigger.

trait.name: Woodland Stride
entries: Difficult terrain composed of magical or nonmagical plants doesn't cost the Rainforest King extra movement. In addition, he can pass through magical or nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Rainforest King uses Blooms of Doom. He then makes three King's Spear attacks. He can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: King's Spear
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d6 + 5}) piercing damage, or 9 ({@damage 1d8 + 5}) piercing damage when used with two hands to make a melee attack, plus 27 ({@damage 6d8}) poison damage. If the Rainforest King makes a ranged King's Spear attack, the weapon flies back to his hand immediately after the attack.

action.name: Blooms of Doom
entries: The Rainforest King causes poisonous flowering vines to sprout in the space of a creature he can see within 30 feet of him. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw or take 14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage and become {@condition restrained} by the vines for 1 minute. While {@condition restrained}, a creature takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) poison damage at the start of each of its turns. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the {@condition restrained} condition on itself on a success.

action.name: Animate Trees (1/Day)
entries: The Rainforest King magically animates two trees he can see within 60 feet of him. These trees use the statistics of a {@creature treant}, except they have Intelligence and Charisma scores of 1, they can't speak, and they have only the Slam action option. They act as allies of the Rainforest King and obey his spoken commands. The trees remain animate for 8 hours, until the Rainforest King dies, or until he takes a bonus action to turn them back into inanimate trees.

reaction.name: Leaf Shield
entries: When the Rainforest King or a friendly creature he can see is hit by an attack, he can creature a magical barrier of leaves to disrupt the attack. The target halves the damage from the attack.

legendaryHeader: The Rainforest King can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The Rainforest King regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

legendary.name: Blooms of Doom
entries: The Rainforest King uses Blooms of Doom.

legendary.name: Tree Stride
entries: The Rainforest King moves up to his speed, stepping through at least one tree with his Tree Stride trait. This movement doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Rainforest King uses Spellcasting.
This umber-skinned fey lord holds audience on a living throne of branches and vines. His clothing and adornments are made from leaves, reeds, and animal tusks and bones.

King Kashama, Lord Protector of the Rainforests, Oba of the Aziza, and Ancient Father of the Brownies is the fey lord of the fey lands' jungles. The Rainforest King rules over a domain of hot, steamy jungles inhabited by {@creature aziza|ToB3}, {@creature brownie|ToB3|brownies}, hags, {@creature rainforest ogre|ToB3|rainforest ogres}, elves, trollkin and other fey who make their homes beneath the forest canopy.

entries.name: Forest Protector
entries: The Rainforest King has an affinity for all the creatures of the rainforest, from insects to apes, and for the abundant plant life that blankets his realm. Anyone damaging or threatening the forest incurs his wrath. He cares deeply for the jungles of the Material Plane coterminous with the fey lands. Stepping inside a giant kapok tree or entering a cave behind a waterfall might transport a wanderer to the Rainforest King's realm. He works to preserve both fey and mundane jungles from ax and fire, sending out his servants or calling upon druidic allies to safeguard its inhabitants. Sometimes, King Kashama himself may appear, reducing whole settlements to rubble and returning the land to fecund jungle.

entries.name: Reclusive Fey
entries: The Rainforest King attends gatherings of the fey lords and ladies only when he must. He respects the {@creature Lord of the Hunt|ToB} and finds {@creature Reynard|CCodex} the Fox Lord mildly amusing, but he is largely indifferent to the other fey leaders. Kashama considers the {@creature Countess of Garlands|ToB3} very frivolous despite sharing an affinity for plants with her, ignoring her numerous party invitations, much to her chagrin.

entries.name: Rainforest King's Lair
entries: King Kashama's lair is a grove of 300-foot-tall mahogany trees, deep in the heart of the rainforest. His court is attended by tiny {@creature aziza|ToB3} and {@creature brownie|ToB3|brownies}, and fearsome {@creature rainforest ogre|ToB3|rainforest ogres}. His primary nobles are his vizier, an awakened male orangutan named Arhaa, and his royal steward, an awakened female chimpanzee named Mulunda. When encountered in his lair, the Rainforest King has a challenge rating of 22 (41,000 XP).

trait.name: Devouring Charge (Boar Form Only)
entries: If the rainforest ogre moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a Tusk attack on the same turn, the target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the ogre can make one Bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: The rainforest ogre has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed}, and magic can't put the ogre to sleep.

trait.name: Speak with Beasts
entries: The rainforest ogre can communicate with Beasts as if they shared a language.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The rainforest ogre makes two Spear or Tusk attacks and one Bite attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d10 + 4}) piercing damage, and the ogre gains 5 ({@damage 2d4}) temporary hp.

action.name: Spear (Ogre Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 10 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) piercing damage, or 13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) piercing damage if used with two hands to make a melee attack.

action.name: Tusk (Boar Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The rainforest ogre transforms into a Large boar or back into its true form, which is an ogre. Its statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.
This fearsome, hulking brute has thick green skin, pointed ears, and a large mouth full of sharp teeth. Its bare-chested body is adorned with macabre piercings and bone jewelry.

Also known as negoogunogumbar, or "hunger of the forest," by locals, these terrifying jungle-dwellers stand around 11 feet tall and weigh over 1,000 pounds. Much smarter than common {@creature ogre||ogres}, rainforest ogres were once creatures of the fey realms, serving hags in deep forests or underworld caverns or working as bodygaruds for unseelie fey rulers. Banished to the mortal world for reasons long lost to time, rainforest ogres now stalk the jungles of the world, consuming whatever they can catch and decorating themselves with the bones.

entries.name: Humanoid Hunters
entries: Rainforest ogres are rightly feared as man-eaters. Although they will eat anything—even nuts and berries if they have to—they enjoy the taste of humanoid flesh above all else, making their homes in caves close to villages to ensure a steady supply of fresh meat. Hunting mostly at night, an ogre sneaks through the jungle as quietly as its large size allows, then then swiftly kills a sentry or snatches a villager. The ogre prefers to eat its victims raw and is often so hungry it doesn't wait to return to its lair before consuming its prey.

entries.name: Boar Affinity
entries: If an alarm is raised in a village where the rainforest ogre is hunting, it assumes the form of a {@creature giant boar} and furiously charges its assailants, bringing what remains back to its cave to feed the giant forest boars it keeps as pets and emergency food supplies. Humanoids living in jungles where rainforest ogres are found kill forest hogs on sight, but they avoid eating the meat because of the animal's associations with the ogre.

trait.name: Plant Camouflage
entries: The alliumite has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks it makes in any terrain with ample obscuring plant life.

trait.name: Tearful Stench
entries: Each creature other than an alliumite within 5 feet of the alliumite when it takes damage must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the start of the creature's next turn. On a successful saving throw, the creature is immune to the Tearful Stench of all alliumites for 1 minute.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The rapscallion makes two Thorny Rapier attacks or one Thorny Rapier attack and one Grappelvine Whip attack.

action.name: Thorny Rapier
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) slashing damage.

action.name: Grapplevine Whip
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) slashing damage. If the target is holding a weapon, it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or drop the weapon. If it is holding more than one weapon, it drops only one.

reaction.name: Grapplevine Escape
entries: When a creature the rapscallion can see hits it with a melee attack, the rapscallion can use its whip to swing up to 20 feet away from the attacker, provided the nearby terrain includes a feature the rapscallion can use to swing, such as a branch, chandelier, ledge, mast, railing, or similar. This movement doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

reaction.name: Pungent Retort
entries: When a creature the rapscallion can see within 60 feet of it starts its turn or casts a spell, the rapscallion issues forth a string of insults cleverly crafted to make a foe cry. If it can hear the rapscallion, the target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw or sob uncontrollably until the start of the rapscallion's next turn. A sobbing creature has disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls and must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw to cast a spell that requires the spellcaster to see its target. The spellcaster doesn't lose the spell slot on a failure.

trait.name: Distracting Rattle
entries: The troll's shell plates rattle constantly, creating a droning, distracting noise. When a creature casts a spell with a verbal component while within 30 feet of the troll, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or lose the spell.

trait.name: Night Hunters
entries: While in dim light or darkness, the rattleback trolls has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The troll regains 10 hp at the start of its turn. If the troll takes fire damage, this trait doesn't function at the start of the troll's next turn. The troll dies only if it starts its turn with 0 hp and doesn't regenerate.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The troll makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. While {@condition poisoned} this way, the target can't regain hp. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) slashing damage.

action.name: Venom Spray {@recharge 5}
entries: The troll sprays its venom in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 28 ({@damage 8d6}) poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition poisoned}. While {@condition poisoned} in this way, a creature can't regain hp. A {@condition poisoned} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

The sound of rattling echoes around a boulder as a creature rises behind it. Black diamond shapes decorate its scales. The creature's underbelly is yellow, its claws are black as coal, and its mouth opens to reveal two long fangs and a forked tongue—but it walks on two legs.

Rattleback trolls are a terror of the badlands where they live and hunt. They are primarily nocturnal and sleep away the days in rocky holes or small caves. Humanoids living near a rattleback troll's lair often leave slain game as tribute at the entrance while it sleeps, in hopes that it will not go hunting too close to their village that evening.

entries.name: Venomous Killers
entries: The rattleback troll's bite injects a debilitating venom that rots the flesh around the wound, preventing natural healing. When surrounded or attacked by multiple creatures, the troll expels a gout of this poison to drive off its attackers. Fortunately, the rattleback troll rarely enters a fray without shaking itself into a frenzy first, and the shell plates on its back give off a warning rattle before it strikes.

trait.name: Charge
entries: If the minotaur moves at least 10 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a gore attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 9 ({@damage 2d8}) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 10 feet away and knocked {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The minotaur has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Labyrinthine Recall
entries: The minotaur can perfectly recall any path it has traveled.

trait.name: Ravening Hunger
entries: When a ravening minotaur reduces a creature to 0 hp with a melee attack on its turn, the minotaur can take a bonus action to consume the creature's heart. The minotaur's hp maximum increases by 5 for every ten hearts it consumes in this way.

trait.name: Ravening Madness
entries: The ravening minotaur has disadvantage on Intelligence checks and saving throws. It considers every creature hostile and doesn't gain any benefit from the {@action Help} action or similar spells or effects that involve helping or receiving help from friendly creatures. This trait doesn't prevent a spellcaster from restoring hp to the minotaur or curing the ravening with magic.

trait.name: Reckless
entries: At the start of its turn, the minotaur can choose to have advantage on all melee weapon attack rolls it makes during that turn, but attack rolls against it have advantage until the start of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The minotaur makes one Bite and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) piercing damage, and the ravening minotaur gains temporary hp equal to the damage dealt. The target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or becoming infected with {@disease the ravening|ToB3}.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Gore
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) piercing damage.
Blood coats the claws and mouth of this wild-eyed minotaur. It holds the dripping remains of a heart in its claw.

This feral minotaur is driven by a monstrous hunger that can be sated only by consuming the hearts of its foes. Its fur stained with blood, it fights with teeth, sharp claws, and lethal horns—though its greatest weapon is its ferocity.

entries.name: Created by Disease
entries: The ancient minotaur clans once deliberately exposed their warriors to a disease called the ravening, hoping to strengthen them. This practice led to disaster as the highly contagious disease spread, creating large numbers of ravening minotaurs—wild creatures filled with uncontrollable fury and a lust for consuming the hearts of other creatures.

trait.name: Bladed Shell
entries: A creature that touches the crab or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) slashing damage.

trait.name: Pounce
entries: If the crab moves at least 15 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Shell Bash attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the crab can make one Claw attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The crab makes one Claw attack and one Shell Bash attack.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 13}). The crab has two claws, each of which can grapple only one target.

action.name: Shell Bash
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) bludgeoning plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) slashing damage.
With a thick exoskeleton studded with sharp ridges and low spines, this large crab harkens back to the primordial era before recorded history.

Razorback crabs are a rare form of crustacean that has evolved to live outside the water. In fact, the crabs' evolution has deprived it of its gills entirely, making it markedly unsuited for coastal or aquatic living. Instead, razorback crabs thrive in farmland and grassy plains and are the bane of farmers and other plains dwellers.

entries.name: Scuttling Tunnelers
entries: In places where a colony of razorback crabs has taken hold, the land is riddled with crisscrossing tunnels and moderately-sized burrows large enough to house three or four of the pests. The tunnels are large enough for a human to crawl through, or for a hound to walk in without difficulty, though the occasion of a dog touring the tunnels often ends in tragedy for the poor beast. Colonies of the crustaceans rarely number more than ten individuals. When a group grows larger, the heartiest young crab leaves with a few of its siblings to found a new colony nearby.

entries.name: Farmland Threat
entries: Razorback crabs are carnivorous and have a diet comprised primarily of insects and small animals. In their burrows, the crabs devour insects, annelids, and arthropods, but their favorite food is fowl, particularly chicken, partridge, and pheasant. Razorback crabs are not voracious, requiring only a few pounds of meat every week to survive, but a small colony of them can empty a chicken coop in a single night simply by burrowing into it. Farmers and their families take evidence of razorback crabs in an area very seriously, and they form search parties to track the creatures to their nests and put an end to them. A single crab is deadly, even to a group of armed farmers. When facing an infestation, the farmers prefer to keep their distance and burn the nuisances out without having to get close enough to come to harm.

entries.name: Succulent Flesh
entries: Like their aquatic counterparts, razorback crabs have tender, flavorful flesh, particularly when roasted. After a razorback crab colony has been put to the torch, the exultant farmers haul the carcasses away and invite their friends and neighbors for a feast of legs and pincers. A single razorback crab can easily feed a dozen people.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The raptor has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the raptor's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Pounce
entries: If the raptor moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the raptor can make one Bladed Feather attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The raptor makes one Bladed Feather attack and one Claw attack.

action.name: Bladed Feather
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/90 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) slashing damage, and the target's speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) slashing damage.
A large raptor stalks through the brush, chittering and chirping. Its vibrant emerald feathers gleam like steel, and its eyes reflect a malevolent intelligence.

Razorfeather raptors are the results of arcane experiments in creating a more lethal and intelligent {@creature velociraptor|MPMM}. They were imbued with blade-like feathers, armored skin, great intelligence, and an instinctive drive to hunt humanoids. The result was a terrifying pack hunter with an urge to hunt and eat humanoids. Now, they're a relentless scourge upon isolated communities and lost hunters.

entries.name: Escaped Experiment
entries: An arcano-paleontologist thought he had designed the perfect soldier: intelligent, social, lethal, and easy to control. The process went well until the first batch of raptors were purchased and deployed. Without warning or apparent cause, they slaughtered their handlers and escaped into the wilds, leaving a trail of bodies in their wake. The wizard stopped his experiments and destroyed the raptors he had, but he was already too late. Hundreds had escaped from half a dozen buyers, most of whom died during the escape. The wizard's reputation was ruined, and the razorfeather raptors were free.

entries.name: Intelligent Hunters
entries: Though lacking speech, razorfeather raptors are almost as intelligent as the humanoids they devour. They understand the speech of those around them, can work door handles, latches, and basic locks, and can set elaborate traps for their prey. Many adventurers who think they have cornered a raptor find they have stumbled into an ambush from the pack. Rumors abound of raptors that have learned literacy or how to use magic items, but they remain unconfirmed.

entries.name: Jungle Stalkers
entries: Razorfeather raptors dwell in thick jungles and wetlands. They live in networks of small packs, which share nests and protect each other's eggs but hunt and socialize separately. Though fully capable of living on prey animals, the raptors have a compulsive need to hunt humanoids. They stalk hunters, lost travelers, and even attack rural villages and isolated mines. Most of these attacks are months-long campaigns of terror, with isolated individuals or families disappearing in surprise attacks. When humanoids discover razorfeather raptors, or hunt them in retaliation, the attacks escalate and often lead to small villages being wiped from the map.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The reef drake deals double damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Tunneler
entries: The drake can burrow through coral and solid rock at half its burrowing speed and leaves a 10-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The drake makes one Bite attack and two Slam attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}26 ({@damage 3d12 + 7}) slashing damage.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d6 + 7}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Concussive Snap {@recharge 5}
entries: The reef drake snaps its jaws, emitting concussive force in a 90-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 35 ({@damage 10d6}) thunder damage, is pushed up to 15 feet away from the drake, and stops holding its breath, if was doing so. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage, isn't pushed, and doesn't lose its held breath. Constructs and objects and structures have disadvantage on the saving throw.

bonus.name: Reef Stealth
entries: If the reef drake is within 10 feet of a coral reef, it can take the {@action Hide} action.

bonus.name: Siege Follow-Through
entries: If a reef drake destroys an object or structure, it can make a bite attack against a creature it can see within 5 feet of that object or structure.
An enormous, scaled wyrm bursts from the reef in a flurry of bubbles and jagged rock. Its snout sports an enormous pair of scissor-like mandibles.

Reef drakes live in coral reefs and submerged cave systems. Long centuries under the sea have seen their forms flatten and their limbs shrivel, leaving a drake that resembles a centipede more than its terrestrial kin. This odd shape is uniquely suited to the drake's preferred terrain, allowing it to carve out and hide in narrow tunnels and choose its prey unseen.

entries.name: Amiable and Ravenous
entries: Reef drakes are, like many of their terrestrial cousins, intelligent creatures fully capable of conversing with others. Often, they will hold long conversations in Draconic with aquatic humanoids, coming off as jovial, amiable, and casual right up until the moment they bite their conversational partner in half. While pleasant creatures to talk to, reef drakes are far more interested in sating their hunger or testing their might than they are even the most pleasant conversation, and they socialize only briefly—even with other reef drakes—preferring their solitude and ambush-tunnels.

entries.name: Jaws of Death
entries: Reef drakes are ambush predators. They memorize shipping lanes and the migration patterns of large fish, nestle into a hiding spot along those routes, and wait for days, weeks, or even months. They strike when they spot their quarry, devouring their prey or shattering ship keels to get at the prey inside. When confronted with a quarry that fights back, a reef drake drags the fight into a coral reef, where it can hide among the rocks and ambush its foes again and again. On rare occasions, a reef drake takes great personal offense at a port in its territory or a ship that has escaped its attacks and attacks a settlement. The drake's concussive snap can collapse buildings and shatter ships in dock, but these chaotic attacks can be dangerous for the drake. While in a settlement, the drake has no places to hide and may find it difficult to retreat.

trait.name: Good Dog
entries: If a Humanoid that hasn't harmed the relentless hound in the last 24 hours takes an action to offer the hound a morsel of food or speak kind words to it, the hound must succeed on a {@dc 12} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by that Humanoid for 1 hour or until the Humanoid or its companions do anything harmful to the hound. In addition, the relentless hound has disadvantage on saving throws against the {@spell command} spell.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The relentless hound can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The relentless hound has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the hound's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The relentless hound doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The relentless hound makes two Spectral Bite attacks. If both attacks hit the same target, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or take 10 ({@damage 3d6}) necrotic damage and its hp maximum is reduced by that amount. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its hp maximum to 0.

action.name: Spectral Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) necrotic damage.

reaction.name: Multiply (3/Day)
entries: When the relentless hound takes damage while below half its hp maximum, it creates a spectral hound. The spectral hound uses the statistics of a shadow, except it doesn't have the Sunlight Weakness trait and it can't make new shadows when it kills Humanoids. It appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the relentless hound and acts on the same initiative as that hound. After the spectral hound finishes a long rest, it becomes a relentless hound.

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with silvered weapons

trait.name: Devil's Sight
entries: Magical darkness doesn't impede the rimepacted's {@sense darkvision}.

trait.name: Frigid Vortex
entries: The rimepacted emits a swirl of cold wind extending in a 15-foot radius around it. Each creature that enters the wind's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. The wind is nonmagical and disperses gas or vapor and extinguishes candles, torches, and similar unprotected flames in the area. At the start of each of its turns, the rimepacted chooses whether this wind is active. While the wind is active, the rimepacted has disadvantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The rimepacted has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Snow Burrower
entries: The rimepacted can burrow only through nonmagical snow and ice.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The rimepacted makes two Icy Claw attacks or three Frostbolt attacks.

action.name: Icy Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d10 + 4}) slashing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) cold damage.

action.name: Frostbolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 6} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d6 + 3}) cold damage, and the target's speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Freezing Smite {@recharge 5}
entries: The rimepacted raises its frigid claw, drawing upon fiendish energies, then smashes its rimed fist into the ground, causing a wave of freezing power to emanate outward. Each creature within 30 feet of the rimepacted must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking 21 ({@damage 6d6}) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If a creature fails the saving throw by 5 or more, it is {@condition restrained} by ice until the end of its next turn.

reaction.name: Fury of the Storm
entries: When a creature the rimepacted can see is knocked {@condition prone} by Frigid Vortex, the rimepacted can move up to half its speed toward the creature.

Dark, frostbitten skin pulls taut over bone, and ice rimes this humanoid's body. Massive ice on its forearms and hands forms monstrous, rending claws, and icicles on its back give it the semblance of a fiendish tail. A vile anger burns in its eyes.

When creatures lost in the coldest reaches of the world approach death from starvation or exposure, they often call out for aid from any entities that can hear them. When an {@creature ice devil} or similarly cold-based devil hears the cries whispered into the blizzard, it might form a pact to spare the mortal's life, resulting in {@creature frost-afflicted|ToB3}. If the devil arrives too late for a pact but before the mortal's spirit has fully left the body, it can reforge the fleeing soul from the freezing cold, creating a rimepacted devil.

entries.name: Servants of Ice
entries: Rimepacted devils typically serve the devils who created them, scouring the land and reaping souls for their infernal masters. Bonded with icy and frigid locales on the Material Plane, rimepacted rarely fight for higher station in devilish hierarchies, content to bring the power of winter down on warm-blooded mortals.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The riptide drake can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Echolocation
entries: The riptide drake can't use its {@sense blindsight} while {@condition deafened} or out of the water.

trait.name: Underwater Camouflage
entries: The riptide drake has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks while underwater.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The riptide drake makes three Sonic Pulse attacks or one Bite attack and two Slam attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}24 ({@damage 4d8 + 6}) piercing damage.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}20 ({@damage 4d6 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Sonic Pulse
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 7} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 4d8 + 3}) thunder damage. The riptide drake can use this action only while underwater.

action.name: Buffeting Blast {@recharge 5}
entries: The riptide drake exhales a powerful stream of water in a 60-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 38 ({@damage 7d10}) bludgeoning damage and becomes disoriented for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't disoriented. When a disoriented creature moves, it moves in a random direction. It can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Manipulate Currents
entries: While underwater, the riptide drake can change the flow of water within 60 feet of it. It can choose one of the following effects, which lasts until the start of its next turn.

items: A 20-foot cube of rushing water forms on a point the drake can see in the water. The cube's space is difficult terrain, and a creature that starts its turn swimming in the area must succeed on a {@dc 17} Strength saving throw or be pushed out of the cube directly away from the riptide drake.

The current shoots in a 60-foot-long, 10-foot-wide line from the drake in a direction it chooses. Each creature in the area must succeed on a {@dc 17} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from the drake in a direction following the line.
The drake takes the {@action Dash} action.

entries: This sinuous, eel-like dragon swims gracefully through the water, its current shifting and roiling at the dragon's command.
Riptide drakes are underwater predators who exert control over the flow of water and exploit this ability to aid in their hunts.

entries.name: Cruel Seas
entries: The drakes enjoy catching creatures unaccustomed to the underwater environment off guard. They delight in battering and disorienting their prey and watch as their victims drown or become lost in the deep. The carnivorous dragons feast on the bloated corpses of creatures that have succumbed to the dangers of the sea.

entries.name: Desirous of Leadership
entries: Riptide drakes seek worthy leaders and grow bored without direction. They follow powerful aquatic dragons, {@creature dragon turtle||dragon turtles}, or watery fiends. A riptide drake remains faithful to its superior, unless the leader betrays them, driving the creature to seek terrible vengeance and new leadership.

trait.name: Elusive
entries: The rochade has advantage on saving throws and ability checks made to avoid or escape an effect that would reduce its speed. In addition, nonmagical difficult terrain composed of natural rocks or cavernous terrain doesn't cost it extra movement.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the rochade has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) rolls that rely on sight.

action.name: Thieving Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage. Instead of dealing damage, the rochade can steal one item the target is wearing or carrying, provided the item weighs no more than 10 pounds, isn't a weapon, and isn't wrapped around or firmly attached to the target. For example, a rochade could steal a hat or belt pouch but not a creature's shirt or armor.

action.name: Rock
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 6} to hit, range 20/60 ft, one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage.

bonus.name: Relay
entries: The rochade teleports one object it is holding that weighs no more than 10 pounds into the empty hand of one friendly creature it can see within 30 feet of it. If the target isn't a rochade, it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw to catch the object, otherwise the object falls to the ground in a space within 5 feet of the target.

bonus.name: Short Step
entries: The rochade teleports to an unoccupied space it can see within 15 feet of it.

reaction.name: Switch
entries: When a creature the rochade can see targets it with an attack, the rochade can switch places with any creature it can see within 15 feet of it. An unwilling creature must succeed on a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw to avoid the switch. If the switch is successful, the switched creature becomes the target of the attack instead.

trait.name: Earth Glide
entries: The rock salamander can burrow through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone. While doing so, the salamander doesn't disturb the material it moves through.

trait.name: Stone Camouflage
entries: The rock salamander has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in rocky terrain.

trait.name: Stone Spider Climb
entries: The rock salamander can climb difficult stone surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Manipulate Stone
entries: If the rock salamander is touching stone, it can manipulate stone contiguous with its location to create one of the following effects:

items.name: Rumbling Earth.
items.entry: One creature touching stone within 10 feet of the salamander must succeed on a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

items.name: Softened Earth.
items.entry: One creature touching stone within 10 feet of the salamander must succeed on a {@dc 12} Strength saving throw or be {@condition restrained} by softened, mud-like stone until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Stone Armor.
items.entry: The salamander's AC increases by 2 until the start of its next turn.

variant.name: Rock Salamander Familiars
entries: Some rock salamanders are willing to serve a spellcaster as a familiar. Such salamanders have the following trait.

entries.name: Familiar
entries: The rock salamander can serve another creature as a familiar, forming a magic, telepathic bond with that willing companion. While the two are bonded, the companion can sense what the salamander senses as long as they are within 1 mile of each other. While the salamander is within 10 feet of its companion, the companion shares the salamander's Stone Camouflage trait. At any time and for any reason, the salamander can end its service as a familiar, ending the telepathic bond.

items.name: Familiar
entries: The rock salamander can serve another creature as a familiar, forming a magic, telepathic bond with that willing companion. While the two are bonded, the companion can sense what the salamander senses as long as they are within 1 mile of each other. While the salamander is within 10 feet of its companion, the companion shares the salamander's Stone Camouflage trait. At any time and for any reason, the salamander can end its service as a familiar, ending the telepathic bond.

trait.name: Stone Chomper
entries: The rockchewer ogre deals double damage to stone objects and structures with its bite attack.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The rockchewer ogre makes two Slam attacks or one Bite attack and one Slam attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 3d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Rock
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 8} to hit, range 60/240 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 3d10 + 5}) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Chew Rock
entries: The rockchewer ogre thoroughly crushes a rock in its large maw, reducing it to rubble.

action.name: Gravel Spray
entries: The rockchewer ogre spits out a 15-foot cone of crunched up rock. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 21 ({@damage 6d6}) bludgeoning damage, or half as much damage on a successful one. The rockchewer ogre can use this action only if it has used Chew Rock within the past minute.

bonus.name: Quick Chew {@recharge 6}
entries: The rockchewer ogre uses Chew Rock.

reaction.name: Rock Catching
entries: If a rock or similar object is hurled at the rockchewer ogre, the ogre can, with a successful {@dc 10} Dexterity saving throw, catch the missile and take no bludgeoning damage from it.

Rough stone forms the skin of this towering giant. It pulls a rock from the bag slung over its shoulder and bites down on it as one would a piece of fruit. When the giant speaks, loose gravel spurts from its mouth.
Rockchewer ogres roam hills, mountains, and badlands that contain exposed stone. They often share territory with stone giants, though the giants consider the ogres' presence an infestation.

entries.name: Absentminded Gnawers
entries: Rockchewer ogres eat natural and worked stone. They compulsively chew on it, which can include castles, towers, or walls. They can quickly devastate foundations, as they typically eat 50 pounds of stone each day. Considering the powerful force required to chew stone, the creatures fortunately forgo biting softer targets. Their fussiness about the experience of chewing tough materials extends to the speed at which they eat. Rockchewer ogres prefer to take their time with their food, rolling the remnants around in their mouths as they eat. Even in cases where they must quickly crush stone to spit devastating cones of debris in combat, the ogres are reluctant to hurry.

entries.name: Deadly Play
entries: The ogres enjoy chucking rocks at each other as a game. On most throws, they fail to catch the projectile, and their bodies withstand most of the impact. A typical match sees rocks thudding against the ogres' torsos while they laugh. Because the ogres greet each other with thrown rocks, they often toss rocks at any new "friends" they meet. They quickly apologize for any harm done, provided the response consists of harsh words and not drawn weapons.

entries.name: Tolerated by Stone Giants
entries: Rockchewer ogres feel a kinship to {@creature stone giant||stone giants} and often migrate to the giants' strongholds. Many {@creature stone giant||stone giants} believe the ogres are distant relatives and begrudgingly accept them into the midst. The ogres put the giants' patience to the test, however, as they thoughtlessly chew on homes, art, or important monuments. {@creature stone giant||Stone giants} prefer not to kill rockchewer ogres; instead, they lead the easily distracted ogres to quarries and mines away from {@creature stone giant} homes.

trait.name: Charge
entries: If the rockslide elemental moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a Slam attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 9 ({@damage 2d8}) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the elemental used Nimble Separation before making this Slam attack, the target has disadvantage on the saving throw.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The rockslide elemental doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Stone Camouflage
entries: The rockslide elemental has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in rocky terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The rockslide elemental makes three Slam attacks, or it makes two Skipping Stone attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Skipping Stone
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 9} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage, and the stone bounces to another creature within 10 feet of the target. That creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw or take 9 ({@damage 2d8}) bludgeoning damage.

bonus.name: Nimble Separation
entries: The rockslide elemental can briefly separate its components parts and take the {@action Dash} action. {@action opportunity attack||Opportunity attacks} against it have disadvantage until the start of its next turn.
Thousands of crimson and granite rocks swirl around, forming the familiar likeness of an earth elemental.
While most earth elementals are slow and methodical, the rockslide elemental harnesses the frenetic energy of a landslide.

entries.name: Composite Form
entries: A rockslide elemental consists of a collection of large rock fragments, with broad boulders and lumpy cobbles, in the shape of a humanoid. It can briefly separate these component parts to reposition itself for a better strike or to escape danger, rolling away and recombining into its humanoid shape.

entries.name: Fiercely Territorial
entries: Rockslide elementals relish destroying intruders. They fight any creature they find trespassing on their territory—even other rockslide elementals. They crash into their foes at speed, dealing deadly blows and bringing even the most boisterous creatures to the ground.

trait.name: Earth Glide
entries: The rockwood can burrow through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone. While doing so, the rockwood doesn't disturb the material it moves through.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The rockwood doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Persistence of Stone (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: When the rockwood is reduced to below half its hp maximum, Fossil Barrage recharges.

trait.name: Roiling Roots
entries: The stony roots of the rockwood make the ground within 15 feet of the rockwood difficult terrain for creatures other than the rockwood.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The rockwood deals double damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Towering Reach
entries: The rockwood doesn't have disadvantage on ranged attack rolls from being within 5 feet of a hostile creature, though it may still have disadvantage from other sources.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The rockwood makes two Whomping Slam or Rock attacks. If both Whomping Slam attacks hit one target, each creature within 5 feet of the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take 9 ({@damage 2d8}) bludgeoning damage and be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Whomping Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, and if the target is a Large or smaller creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Rock
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 8} to hit, range 60/180 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Fossil Barrage {@recharge 6}
entries: The rockwood sprays shards of stone in a 30-foot cone. feet around it Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 28 ({@damage 8d6}) piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The chimera's goat head casts one of the following spells, requiring only verbal components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 18}):

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the royal chimera fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Regal Bearing
entries: The chimera's AC includes its Charisma modifier.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The chimera makes one Bite attack, one Claws attack, and one Eldritch Horns attack, or it makes two Arcane Blast attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 3d6 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 3d6 + 5}) slashing damage.

action.name: Eldritch Horns
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d12 + 5}) force damage.

action.name: Serpent Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d8 + 5}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Arcane Blast
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 10} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 3d10 + 5}) force damage.

action.name: Searing Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The dragon head exhales a 30-foot cone of fire that burns and blinds. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 45 ({@damage 10d8}) fire damage and is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition blinded}.

legendary.name: Prideful Prowl
entries: The chimera moves up to its walking speed, or flies up to half its flying speed, without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

legendary.name: Serpent Strike
entries: The chimera makes one Serpent Strike attack.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The chimera uses Spellcasting.

legendary.name: Roar of the King (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The lion head lets out a bellow that spurs on its allies. Each friendly creature, including the chimera, within 30 feet of the chimera that can hear the roar gains 11 ({@damage 2d10}) temporary hp and can't be {@condition frightened} for 1 minute.
This chimera has a lion's mane plaited in gold, a goat head with magic sparking between its curved horns, a smoking dragon's head, and a hissing serpent for a tail.

Chimeras that discover the luxury of long life inevitably enjoy the servitude of lesser creatures. Declaring sovereignty—with few willing to dispute the claim—such chimera foster unceasing ambition for power, wealth, and status.

entries.name: Profane Magic
entries: Any chimera that lives long enough to claim royalty and be recognized as such soon gains magical power, often commanded by its goat head. With this power, the goat transfigures the chimera's tail into a snake, giving the chimera the knowledge of speech and greater capability for deception and manipulation. Though the transformation of the tail provides the knowledge of speech, the chimera can speak through any of its heads.

trait.name: Flesh-Eating Aura
entries: When a creature that doesn't have all of its hp starts its turn within 10 feet of the scarab, the creature takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) necrotic damage. In addition, any magical healing within 10 feet of the scarab is halved.

trait.name: Silent Steps
entries: No sound emanates from the ruin scarab, whether moving, attack, or ripping into a corpse. It has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks, and each creature within 5 feet of the scarab is {@condition deafened}.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The ruin scarab doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Unstoppable
entries: Moving through difficult terrain doesn't cost the ruin scarab extra movement, and its speed can't be reduced.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ruin scarab makes two Bite attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Gut Rip.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d10 + 3}) slashing damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4}) necrotic damage, and the target must make a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw. On a failure, the target is knocked {@condition prone} or pushed 5 feet away, the scarab's choice.

action.name: Gut Rip
entries: The ruin scarab tears into one {@condition prone} creature within 5 feet of it. The target must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the target takes 11 ({@damage 2d10}) slashing damage and 5 ({@damage 2d4}) necrotic damage and is {@condition incapacitated} for 1 minute. On a success, the target takes half the damage and isn't {@condition incapacitated}. An {@condition incapacitated} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

reaction.name: Relentless Pursuit
entries: When a creature within 10 feet of the ruin scarab moves away from it, the scarab can move up to half its speed toward that creature.
The large beetle is bone white and moves in eerie silence, carving into a pile of corpses with incredible speed.
Ruin scarabs are undead preparers of the dead, stripping meat from bones with cleaver-like mandibles and sorting of dead matter for their masters.

entries.name: Undead Pets
entries: These undead beetles were created by lords of undeath with just enough intelligence to be trainable. They butcher corpses with ease and determination, they clean the bones, and they sort and store the various vital bits for later use. The scarabs make fine guards, and every so often, they employ both sets of skills simultaneously when unwanted intruders stop by. They are commonly found roaming important mausoleums and necropolises as guards and cleaners, and they serve as guards deterrents in the demesne of undead lords.

entries.name: Rare Parts Retrieval
entries: Occasionally, a necromancer needs a very specific component for a ritual, or for a fine supper—maybe an aasimar spleen, a {@creature basilisk} eye, a certain drake's wing, or a giant oyster's pearl. Whatever the need, ruin scarabs make excellent retrievers, going straight to the source for the freshest of specimens.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The story keeper casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}):

trait.name: Aura of Drunkenness
entries: The story keeper radiates an aura of drunkenness to a radius of 20 feet. Each creature that starts its turn in the aura must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} for 1 hour. A creature that has consumed alcohol within the past hour has disadvantage on the saving throw. While {@condition poisoned}, a creature falls {@condition prone} if it tries to move more than half its speed during a turn. A creature that succeeds on the saving throw is immune to the Aura of Drunkenness of all rum gremlins for 24 hours.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The story keeper has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Mug Slap
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Rum Splash
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 40/80 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Drinking Stories
entries: The story keeper tells a story of a rum gremlin celebration. Each story has an initial effect and an additional effect if the story keeper takes a bonus action on its subsequent turns to continue the story. It can stop telling a story at any time, and the story ends if the story keeper is {@condition incapacitated}. The story keeper can tell only one story at a time. It chooses from the following stories:

items.name: Tale of Liquid Courage.
items.entry: Each rum gremlin within 30 feet of the story keeper and that can hear it gains 5 temporary hp for 1 minute. While this tale continues, each rum gremlin that starts its turn within 30 feet of the story keeper has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}.

items.name: Tale of the Bar Room Rush.
items.entry: Each rum gremlin within 30 feet of the story keeper and that can hear it can use its reaction to immediately move up to its speed. While this tale continues, each rum gremlin within 30 feet of the story keeper can take the {@action Dash} or {@action Disengage} action as a bonus action on its turn.

items.name: Tale of the Great Shindig.
items.entry: Each rum gremlin within 30 feet of the story keeper and that can hear it can use its reaction to immediately shove a Medium or smaller creature. While this tale continues, each rum gremlin within 30 feet of the story keeper has advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks and can {@action shove} creatures up to two sizes larger than it.

entries: A potbellied gremlin dressed in a dirty tabard, stained ruff collar, tattered cloak, and puffy breeches takes a swig from a jug of foulsmelling rum.

entries.name: Drunken Celebrities
entries: Story keepers are renowned among {@creature rum gremlin|ToB|rum gremlins} for always being at the center of any major drunken debauchery. They travel to gremlin gatherings then retell them as stories to other gremlins. The stories often include besting rivals in drinking contests and chaotic hijinks for non-gremlins. {@creature rum lord|ToB2|Rum lords} lure story keepers to their courts with promises of rum and fun when seeking to increase their courts' prestige.

entries.name: Solo Artists
entries: Story keepers don't like to share the spotlight when telling stories. They are notorious for sabotaging and outright attacking other performers—even performers that aren't gremlins. Devious rum lords have been known to summon rival story keepers to court at the same time in hopes of sparking a drinking or storytelling duel for the court's entertainment.

trait.name: Divine Awareness
entries: The sacred hippopotamus knows if it hears a lie.

trait.name: Divine Jaws
