entries: The underworld sentinel doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The sentinel makes two Scythe or Death Knell attacks.

action.name: Scythe
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) slashing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Death Knell
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 7} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d8 + 3}) necrotic damage, or 22 ({@damage 3d12 + 3}) necrotic damage if the target is missing any of its hit points.

action.name: Grim Reaping {@recharge 5}
entries: The underworld sentinel spins with its scythe extended and makes one Scythe attack against each creature within its reach. A creature that takes necrotic damage from this attack can't regain hp until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Celestial Weapons
entries: The ursan archon's weapon attacks are magical. When the ursan archon hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 4d8} force damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Immortal Nature
entries: The ursan archon doesn't require food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The ursan archon has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Reckless
entries: At the start of its turn, the ursan archon can gain advantage on all melee weapon attack rolls it makes during that turn, but attack rolls against it have advantage until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Relentless (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: If the ursan archon takes 30 damage or less that would reduce it to 0 hp, it is reduced to 1 hp instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ursan archon makes two Greataxe attacks. When Thunderous Roar is available, it can use the roar in place of one Greataxe attack.

action.name: Greataxe
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d12 + 5}) slashing damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) force damage.

action.name: Thunderous Roar {@recharge 5}
entries: The ursan archon unleashes a terrifying roar in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw, taking 36 ({@damage 8d8}) thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. In addition, each hostile creature within 60 feet of the archon that can hear it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. Creatures in the 30-foot cone have disadvantage on this saving throw. A {@condition frightened} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

reaction.name: Rallying Roar
entries: When the ursan archon reduces a creature to 0 hp, it can utter a triumphant roar. Each friendly creature within 60 feet of the archon that is {@condition frightened}, {@condition paralyzed}, {@condition stunned}, or {@condition unconscious} has advantage on its next saving throw. A friendly creature without those conditions has advantage on its next attack roll. In addition, each friendly creature within 60 feet of the archon that can hear the roar gains 14 ({@damage 4d6}) temporary hp.

trait.name: Jungle Camouflage
entries: The constrictor has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in jungle terrain.

trait.name: Poisonous Leaves
entries: The skin of the constrictor is embedded with vines and poisonous leaves that burn on contact. A creature that isn't a Construct or Undead that touches the constrictor or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

trait.name: Urushi Blight
entries: A creature infected with this disease manifests symptoms within {@dice 1d4} hours, which includes the gradual spread of a painful, itching rash that develops into inflamed and blistered skin over the course of the first day. Until the disease is cured, the infected creature has disadvantage on Constitution saving throws made to maintain concentration on a spell, and it has disadvantage on Dexterity and Charisma saving throws and ability checks. At the end of each long rest, the infected creature must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. If it succeeds on two saving throws, it recovers from the disease.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The constrictor makes two Bite attacks or one Bite attack and one Poisonous Constriction attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d10 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Poisonous Constriction
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained} and takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) poison damage at the start of each of its turns, and the snake can't use Poisonous Constriction on another target. Each time the target takes this poison damage, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or contract the urushi blight disease (see the Urushi Blight trait).

trait.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: The swarm has advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its hit points.

trait.name: Blood Sense
entries: The swarm of vampire blossoms can pinpoint, by scent, the location of creatures that aren't constructs or undead within 30 feet of it. The swarm is otherwise blind.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the swarm of vampire blossoms remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a carpet of flower petals.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm of vampire blossoms can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny plant. The swarm can't regain hp or gain temporary hp.

action.name: Petal Slashes
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 0 ft., one creature in the swarm's space. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) slashing damage, or 9 ({@damage 2d8}) slashing damage if the swarm has half its hp or fewer. The target must make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw, reducing its hp maximum by an amount equal to the damage taken on a failed save, or by half as much on a successful one. This reduction lasts until the creature finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its hp maximum to 0.

monster.name: Vampiric Vanguard
languages: the languages it knew in life

trait.name: Bloodthirsty
entries: When the vampiric vanguard is below half its hp maximum, it has advantage on all melee attack rolls against creatures that aren't Constructs or Undead.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The vampire can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The vampiric vanguard doesn't require air.

trait.name: Vampire Weaknesses
entries: The vampire has the following flaws:
Forbiddance. The vampire can't enter a residence without an invitation from one of the occupants.
Harmed by Running Water. The vampire takes 20 acid damage when it ends its turn in running water.
Stake to the Heart. The vampire is destroyed if a piercing weapon made of wood is driven into its heart while it is {@condition incapacitated} in its resting place.
Sunlight Hypersensitivity. The vampire takes 20 radiant damage when it starts its turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, it has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The vampire vanguard makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks. If the vanguard hits one target with both Claw attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or take 7 ({@damage 2d6}) slashing damage, and the vampire regains hp equal to that amount.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) necrotic damage. The target's hp maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken, and the vampire regains hp equal to that amount. The reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its hp maximum to 0.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

bonus.name: Fleet of Foot
entries: The vampire takes the {@action Dash} action.

trait.name: Diving Pounce
entries: If the drake is flying and moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the drake can make one Bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Gaseous Ascension
entries: The vapor drake must regularly inhale swamp gases to maintain its flight. If the drake can't breathe or if it isn't in swampy terrain, it loses its flying speed. In addition, when the drake uses Poisonous Breath, it loses its flying speed until Poisonous Breath recharges.

trait.name: Speak with Beasts
entries: The drake can communicate with Beasts native to swampland as if they shared a language.

trait.name: Swamp Camouflage
entries: The drake has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in swampy terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The drake makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Poisonous Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The drake exhales poisonous swamp gas in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking 27 ({@damage 5d8}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If the drake is flying, its Gaseous Ascension immediately ends, it takes falling damage, as normal, and each creature that failed the saving throw is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. A {@condition poisoned} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
After huffing a gout of swampgas, this green wingless drake takes to the sky, diving at its prey with its razor-sharp claws and venomous bite.

Vapor drakes have long lived deep in the swamplands, and their scales are well suited to the boggy environment. These drakes react violently to any trespassers upon their hunting grounds, and they fight to the death if cornered.

entries.name: Flightless Flier
entries: By inhaling swamp vapors, the vapor drake can emulate its draconic cousins and fly for short periods of time. Many adventurers are surprised by this seemingly clumsy flyer, who rams into foes before tearing at their flesh with its ravenous maw.

entries.name: Venomous Bite
entries: After injecting the meat of its prey with its venom, the drake buries the meat in the mire, preserving it for future meals. Later consumption of preserved prey enhances the venomous quality of the drake's bite.

entries.name: Friend to Swamp Creatures
entries: The vapor drake can converse with the creatures that live within the fens, and most animals, particularly lizards, recognize the drake's authority within the bog's hierarchy. The vapor drake views these creatures as either prey or its wards, depending on the palatability of the creatures, and aggressively protects them from intruders.

trait.name: Aching Venom
entries: The venom drake produces a potent poison that causes its victims to feel pain from even the most benign contact, leaving them aching from the weight of their clothing or sore after a simple sword swing. When a creature that succumbs to this poison takes bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn as pain fills its body. This potent poison remains within the creature's body until removed by the {@spell greater restoration} spell or similar magic or until the creature finishes a long rest.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The venom drake has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Shifting Camouflage
entries: The venom drake's scales adapt to its current surroundings. The drake has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in nonmagical, natural terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The venom drake makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks or three Spit Venom attacks. If the drake hits one creature with two Spit Venom attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or succumb to the drake's venom (see the Aching Venom trait).

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) piercing damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or succumb to the drake's venom (see the Aching Venom trait).

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Spit Venom
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 6} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d8 + 3}) poison damage.

action.name: Venom Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The venom drake spits venom in a 60-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 36 ({@damage 8d8}) poison damage and succumbs to the drake's venom (see the Aching Venom trait). On a success, a creature takes half the damage and doesn't succumb to the venom.
The thin, serpentine body of a drake unfolds as its scales slowly shift in a menagerie of changing colors. Its head rears back as veiny frills extend, black bile dripping from its gaping maw.
Venom drakes are venomous jungle-dwelling predators that use their natural camouflage to ambush and hunt prey.

entries.name: Camouflaged Hunter
entries: The drake can change the colors of its scales providing it with the camouflage and helping it hunt in a multitude of environments. It is most commonly found in tropical rainforests, but it can also make its home in more temperate forests. Venom drakes claiming hunting territory in mountainous forests is less common.

entries.name: Crippling Venom
entries: The venom drake's venom is unique, causing hyperactivity in a creature's nerve endings. Survivors have reported increased tactile senses, to the point of feeling pain from any touch.

resist.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with cold iron weapons

trait.name: Fortissimo Fibber
entries: The veritigibbet can't be magically silenced or forced to tell the truth by any means, and it knows if it hears a lie.

trait.name: Ventriloquist
entries: The veritigibbet can make its voice sound as though it originates from any point it can see within 60 feet of it.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The veritigibbet makes two Repartee attacks.

action.name: Repartee
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) psychic damage.

action.name: Blather
entries: The veritigibbet asks a question of a creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must make a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the target must either answer the question truthfully and completely or have disadvantage on all attack rolls, saving throws, and ability checks for the next 1 hour, cumulatively increasing in duration each time the target fails the saving throw and chooses to take disadvantage. If the target chooses to answer the question, it can't dissemble or omit information relevant to the question being answered. On a success, the veritigibbet takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) psychic damage. Creatures that are immune to being {@condition charmed} aren't affected by this action.

action.name: Veiled Escape (1/Day)
entries: The veritigibbet teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space it can see, becoming {@condition invisible} when it arrives at the destination.

reaction.name: Liar, Liar
entries: When a creature lies while within 30 feet of the veritigibbet, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw or take 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage and it ignites. Until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, the target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

trait.name: Corpse Stride
entries: Once on its turn, the vetala can use 10 feet of its movement to step magically into one corpse or Undead within its reach and emerge from a second corpse or Undead within 60 feet of the first, appearing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the second corpse or Undead.

trait.name: Graveyard Walker
entries: Difficult terrain composed of tombstones, grave dirt, corpses, or other objects or features common to graveyards and necropolises doesn't cost the vetala extra movement, and the vetala can move through graveyard objects and structures, such as sarcophaguses and mausoleums, as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The vetala has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Master of Undeath
entries: A Humanoid killed by the vetala or an Undead under its control rises 1 minute later as a {@creature skeleton} or {@creature zombie} (the vetala's choice), unless the Humanoid is restored to life or its body is destroyed. The vetala can have no more than 20 total {@creature skeleton||skeletons} and {@creature zombie||zombies} under its control at one time.

trait.name: Shepherd of Death
entries: When an Undead under the vetala's control hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 4 ({@damage 1d8}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The vetala makes two Claw attacks.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) slashing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Raise Corpse
entries: One Humanoid corpse the vetala can see within 30 feet of it rises as a {@creature skeleton} or {@creature zombie} (the vetala's choice) under the vetala's control.

bonus.name: Command Corpse
entries: The vetala commands one Undead under its control that it can see to make one weapon attack as a reaction. If it does so, the Undead has advantage on the attack roll.

reaction.name: Corpse Detonation
entries: When an Undead under the vetala'a control is reduced to 0 hp, the vetala can force it to explode. Each creature that isn't an Undead or the vetala within 5 feet of the exploding Undead must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) bludgeoning (if zombie) or slashing (if skeleton) damage and 5 ({@damage 2d4}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
This shadowy figure has eyes like two cinders and loose, flowing hair that seems buffeted by a gentle breeze. Its chest cavity burns with hellish light, and its hands end in wicked claws.

Vetala lurk in graveyards and battlefields, where they claim corpses to serve as minions. Capricious and vain, vetala demons occasionally make bargains to protect nearby villages, requiring villagers to provide regular sacrificial goods, while the demon keeps the corpses of any killed. These bargains state the vetala may claim the residents if the village fails to meet its obligations.

entries.name: Celebrators of the Necropolis
entries: Vetala are strangely vain about their occupied graveyards, wanting the graveyards to be decorated with strings of flowers, treats, and votive offerings. They never claim these objects themselves, but they viciously attack anyone attempting to steal the items. This vanity extends to the demon's minions, which stop decaying while controlled and eventually appear less decayed.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The ogre has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Tentacle Senses
entries: The Void-blessed ogre can't use its {@sense tremorsense} while {@condition grappled} or {@condition restrained}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Void-blessed ogre makes one Tentacle Lash attack and one Void Bite attack, or it makes three Void Spit attacks.

action.name: Tentacle Lash
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) psychic damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Intelligence saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn, as jumbled visions and voices of the Void fill its mind.

action.name: Void Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d10 + 5}) piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Void Spit
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 6} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) necrotic damage.

reaction.name: Volatile Stomach
entries: When the Void-blessed ogre takes bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, it can regurgitate some of its stomach contents. Each creature within 5 feet of the ogre must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or take 4 ({@damage 1d8}) necrotic damage and be {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn. A pool of Voidinfused stomach contents forms in a space the ogre can see within 5 feet of it and lasts until the start of the ogre's next turn. A creature that enters the pool for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) necrotic damage.

A purple-skinned {@creature ogre} stands hunched over, using boulders to keep itself upright. Its slack jaw filled with yellowed teeth hangs open, with green drool falling out. A dark void sits where its eyes should be, and two tentacles extend from its shoulders. Each tentacle ends in three smaller tentacles, reaching and quivering and sometimes holding it upright.

Void-blessed ogres are {@creature ogre||ogres} born in areas filled with the power of the Void or inhabited by dark creatures from the space between the stars. The volatile Void energies blessed these ogres with supernatural senses and two powerful tentacles. These tentacles increase the ogres' senses further when bored into the ground. The tentacles weigh on the Void-blessed ogre physically and mentally, slowing its pace and assaulting its mind with whispers of the Void. These whispers bolster the ogre's mind against outside influences, making it harder to magically control than most of its cousins.

entries.name: Never-Ending Hunger
entries: Void-blessed ogres crave food constantly, and are never satisfied for long before seeking their next meal. They often fear their hunger and overeat in a futile attempt to suppress the hunger pains. During times of stress, a Void-blessed ogre regurgitates the volatile contents of its stomach, creating pools that rot anything they touch.

entries.name: Cultist Allurement
entries: Anyone who touches one of the ogre's tentacles hears the voices of the Void that swim in the ogre's mind. Many cults of the Void grant special places in their ranks for the ogres, feeding them in return for this opportunity to commune with the Void. Similarly, other creatures connected to the Void are drawn to Void-blessed ogres, feeling an innate connection and occasionally deference to the ogre.

monster.name: Void Constructor
languages: understands Void Speech but can't speak

trait.name: Mobile Grappler
entries: While grappling a creature, the constructor can move at its full speed, carrying the {@condition grappled} creature along with it.

trait.name: Tainted Aura
entries: A Void constructor that has completed a Void henge gains necrotic energy while within 1 mile of the henge. At the start of each of the Void constructor's turns, each creature within 10 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw or take 2 ({@damage 1d4}) necrotic damage. The Void constructor can choose for a creature it is grappling to be immune to the Tainted Auras of all Void constructors.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Void constructor makes two Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Tentacles
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 4d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 13}). Until this grapple ends, the constructor can't use its tentacles on another target.

monster.name: Void Fungus
languages: understands Common and Void Speech but can't speak

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the Void fungus remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a patch of glowing fungus.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The Void fungus sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Void fungus makes two Poisonous Mycelium or Psychic Blast attacks. If the Void fungus hits one creature with both Psychic Blast attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw or be {@condition charmed} for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Poisonous Mycelium
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Psychic Blast
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) psychic damage.

action.name: Consume Energy
entries: The hair-like tendrils dangling from the Void fungus's cap briefly flash as it draws psychic energy from a creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must make a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw, taking 18 ({@damage 4d8}) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fungus regains hp equal to the damage dealt.
A purple glow emanates from the translucent caps of a lavender fungus with pale strands dangling from it. The largest batch of the fungus moves, its dark mycelium reaching up beckoningly.

Void fungus is a semi-sentient cluster of phosphorescent mushrooms that psychically assault living creatures. Once their prey is helpless, they feast on their vital fluids through a fibrous mycelium network.

entries.name: Mobile Fungus
entries: Once a cluster of void fungus has been fed ample blood, one or more swollen mushrooms undergo a metamorphosis, breaking apart from the rest as a creature with enhanced power and mobility. These ambulatory Void fungi collect prey for the colony.

entries.name: Alien Origins
entries: Originating from some long-forgotten corner of the Void, the Void fungus resembles a sinister, otherworldly version of the pixie's parasol mushroom. How their spores spread across the planes is a subject of esoteric debate. This eldritch fungus has been known to turn up in the nightmares of spellcasters, usually alongside even greater threats.

trait.name: Call of the Void
entries: If a creature within 5 feet of the Void knight attempts to move away from it, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be unable to move away from the Void knight. If the creature uses magic to move, such as the misty step or freedom of movement spells, it automatically succeeds on this saving throw.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The void knight doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Immoveable
entries: The Void knight can't be moved against its will, except by magical means. In addition, the knight has disadvantage on Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) and Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks.

trait.name: Implosive End
entries: When the Void knight dies, it collapses in on itself, releasing a wave of Void energy. Creature within 5 feet of it must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 18 ({@damage 4d8}) force damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Void knight makes two Void Gauntlet attacks or three Void Bolt attacks.

action.name: Void Gauntlet
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d6 + 6}) slashing damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) force damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature it must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Void Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 7} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 4d8 + 3}) force damage.

action.name: Pull of the Void {@recharge 5}
entries: The Void knight sends Void tendrils at up to three creatures it can see within 60 feet of it that are not behind total cover. Each target must succeed on a {@dc 16} strength Saving throw or be pulled up to 30 feet toward the knight. Then each creature within 5 feet of the Void knight takes 36 ({@damage 8d8}) force damage.

trait.name: Erratic Movement
entries: {@action opportunity attack||Opportunity attacks} against the voidclaw zombie are made with disadvantage.

trait.name: Turn Resistance
entries: The voidclaw zombie has advantage on saving throws against any effect that turns undead.

trait.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: If damage reduces the zombie to 0 hp, it must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 5 + the damage taken, unless the damage is radiant or from a critical hit. On a success, the zombie drops to 1 hp instead.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The voidclaw zombie doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The zombie makes three Void Claw or Void Bolt attacks.

action.name: Void Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) slashing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) necrotic damage. If the target is a creature other than a Construct or Undead, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or take 3 ({@damage 1d6}) necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns as the wound burns with Void energy. Any creature can take an action to purge the tainted energy from the wound with a successful {@dc 12} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check. The energy also leaves the wound if the target receives magical healing, ending the effect.

action.name: Void Bolt
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 4d6 + 2}) necrotic damage.

bonus.name: Nimble Fighter
entries: The voidclaw zombie takes the {@action Dash} or {@action Disengage} action
Faint black waves of energy surround the zombie. It seems agitated and then is just gone.

Voidclaw zombies are suffused with the magic of the Void, which twists their bodies into odd proportions, including overly large clawed hands, a shortened frame, increased intellect, and surprising speed. They are created in rituals invoking the Void, and they channel its dark energy.

entries.name: Efficient Killers
entries: These zombies have proven to be surprisingly capable killers, quickly clearing areas of all life. Cults of the Void and other doomsday cults often utilize voidclaws in their machinations. Voidclaws show a zeal for killing, and lesser undead instinctively defer to them, following them into combat with almost the same zeal.

entries.name: Voice of the Void
entries: When voidclaws speak, it is with an authority incongruous with their bearing, and many cult leaders hold the words and opinions of the zombies in high regard, believing some aspect of the Void itself speaks through the zombies. Voidclaws sometimes have an inscrutable mission of their own, a singular goal imprinted on them at creation by unseen forces within the Void. Most cultists have learned it is better to not interfere, waiting for the mission's completion before requesting the zombie's assistance.

trait.name: Insanity
entries: The voidwarped has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

trait.name: Mortal Void Traveler
entries: The voidwarped doesn't require air or ambient pressure.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the voidwarped has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight.

trait.name: Void-Touched Weapons
entries: The voidwarped's weapon attacks are magical. When the voidwarped hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} cold damage and {@damage 1d6} force damage (included in the attack).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The voidwarped makes two Shortsword attacks and one Void Tendril attack.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) cold damage and 3 ({@damage 1d6}) force damage.

action.name: Void Tendril
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d6}) bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) cold damage and 3 ({@damage 1d6}) force damage. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or its hp maximum is reduced by the amount equal to the damage taken. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its hp maximum to 0.

action.name: Void Speech Rant {@recharge 5}
entries: The voidwarped spews a tirade of {@language Void Speech|ToB3}. Each creature within 40 feet of it that can hear the tirade must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 27 ({@damage 5d10}) psychic damage and is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Lava Bound
entries: The vorthropod can exist outside of lava or magma for up to 4 hours each day. If it starts its turn outside of lava and didn't take fire damage since the end of its previous turn, its shell begins to cool and harden. While its shell is hardened, it has a walking speed of 20 feet, an Armor Class of 18, and vulnerability to bludgeoning damage. If it starts its turn in lava or if it took fire damage since the end of its previous turn, its shell becomes molten again. If the vorthropod remains outside of lava for more than 4 hours, its shell becomes {@condition petrified}, transforming into basalt, and the vorthropod within goes into hibernation. Its body returns to normal if it is submerged in lava or magma for 1 round. It can stay {@condition petrified} in hibernation for up to {@dice 1d100 + 10} years, after which it dies.

trait.name: Lava Camouflage
entries: The vorthropod has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in lava and volcanic terrain.

trait.name: Molten Shell
entries: If the vorthropod's shell isn't hardened, a creature that touches the vorthropod or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The vorthropod makes two Claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Molten Tail Slap {@recharge 4}
entries: The vorthropod unfurls its tail and slaps it down, showering the area with searing sparks and superheated rock. Each creature within 15 feet of it must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw, taking 28 ({@damage 8d6}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Bloodthirsty Pounce
entries: If the wakwak moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Talon attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. If the target is {@condition prone}, the wakwak can make one Beak attack against it as a bonus action, gaining temporary hp equal to half the damage dealt.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The wakwak has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The wakwak has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the wakwak's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wakwak makes one Beak attack and one Talon attack.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Talon
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) piercing damage.

action.name: Wing Slap {@recharge 5}
entries: The wakwak slams its sharp-edged wings together in front of itself in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) slashing damage and is pushed up to 10 feet away from the wakwak and knocked {@condition prone}. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't pushed or knocked {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Acidic Fog
entries: A creature that starts its turn in the wandering haze's space takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) acid damage.

trait.name: Cloud Form
entries: The wandering haze can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing, and the haze can enter a hostile creature's space and vice versa. The wandering haze's space is heavily obscured.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the wandering haze remains motionless, or floats on air currents, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary cloud or fog bank.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The wandering haze doesn't require sleep.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wandering haze makes three Pseudopod attacks.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d4 + 6}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) acid damage.

action.name: Sheath of Chaos
entries: The wandering haze wraps its cloudy form tighter around those within it. Each creature in the wandering haze's space must make a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 27 ({@damage 6d8}) psychic damage and is disoriented until the end of its next turn as its mind fills with the gibbering terrors of the chaos that created the wandering haze's form. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't disoriented. When a disoriented creature moves, it moves in a random direction.

action.name: Acidic Cloudburst {@recharge 5}
entries: The wandering haze releases a deluge of acid. Each creature within 10 feet of it must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 35 ({@damage 10d6}) acid damage and its speed is halved for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and its speed isn't reduced. A creature with halved speed can make a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The warlock casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Elemental Weapons
entries: When the warlock hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@dice 3d6} acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage (included in the attack), the warlock's choice.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) piercing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage (the warlock's choice).

action.name: Elemental Burst
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage (the warlock's choice).

reaction.name: Genie Lord's Favor
entries: When the warlock takes acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage, the warlock has advantage on the next Elemental Burst attack it makes before the end of its next turn, provided the Elemental Burst deals the same damage as the damage the warlock took.

trait.name: Elemental Weapons
entries: The jinnborn's weapon attacks are imbued with its elemental power. When the jinnborn hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} cold damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Flamboyant Defense
entries: While the jinnborn pirate is wearing no armor and wielding no shield, its AC includes its Charisma modifier.

trait.name: Knows the Ropes
entries: The jinnborn pirate has proficiency with airships, sandships, or waterborne ships (the jinnborn's choice). It adds its proficiency bonus to any check it makes to control the chosen type of ship, and it adds its proficiency bonus to any saving throws made while on the chosen type of vehicle.

action.name: Rapier
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) cold damage.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, ranged 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) cold damage.

reaction.name: Parry
entries: The jinnborn pirate adds 2 to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it. To do so, the jinnborn must see the attacker and be wielding a melee weapon.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The waterkledde can hold its breath for 15 minutes.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The waterkledde makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks. If it hits one Medium or smaller creature with both Claw attacks, the waterkledde latches onto the creature with its Beak, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 15}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, the waterkledde can automatically hit the target with its Beak, and the waterkledde can't make Beak attacks against other targets.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Frightening Call {@recharge 5}
entries: The waterkledde screeches "Kludde! Kledde! Kleure!" in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 22 ({@damage 5d8}) psychic damage and is {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't {@condition frightened}. A {@condition frightened} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The waterkledde magically transforms into a Small or Medium Beast, or back into its true, fiendish form. Without wings, it loses its flying speed. Its statistics, other than its size and speed, are the same in each form. No matter the form, its eyes are always an unnatural green. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

bonus.name: Supernatural Speed
entries: The waterkledde takes the {@action Dash} or {@action Disengage} action.

walk.condition: (20 ft., swim 30 ft. in crocodile or hybrid form)

immune.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with silvered weapons

trait.name: Hold Breath (Crocodile or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: The werecrocodile can hold its breath for 15 minutes.

action.name: Multiattack (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: The werecrocodile makes two Tail Swipe or Khopesh attacks, or it makes one Bite attack and one Tail Swipe attack.

action.name: Bite (Crocodile or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d10 + 2}) piercing damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 12}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, and the werecrocodile can't bite another target. If the target is a Humanoid, it must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or be cursed with werecrocodile lycanthropy.

action.name: Tail Swipe (Crocodile or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target not {@condition grappled} by the werecrocodile. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d4 + 2}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Khopesh (Humanoid Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) slashing damage, or 7 ({@damage 1d10 + 2}) slashing damage if used with two hands.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The werecrocodile transforms into a Large crocodile or into a crocodile-humanoid hybrid, or back into its true form, which is Humanoid. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it. If the werecrocodile dies, it reverts to its true form.
This well-muscled human's smile is a little too wide and its teeth a little too sharp.
Werecrocodiles inhabit many cities and villages on the banks of great rivers and lakes.

entries.name: Big Grins
entries: In humanoid form, werecrocodiles are well-muscled and have a disconcerting smile that is just a bit too wide. They like to wield khopeshes and other brutal slashing weapons in battle.

entries.name: Mistaken for Merchants
entries: Werecrocodiles are often confused for {@creature subek|ToB}, which can lead to nasty surprises for those seeking knowledge or goods. The indifference that subek feel toward werecrocodiles doesn't help the situation, leaving those who bargain with them always on edge, never knowing if they're dealing with a {@creature subek|ToB} merchant or a duplicitous werecrocodile.

monster.name: Wereotter

walk.condition: (swim 40 ft. in otter or hybrid form)

senses: darkvision 60 ft. (otter and hybrid forms only)

immune.note: from nonmagical attacks not made with silvered weapons

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The wereotter can hold its breath for 10 minutes.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The wereotter has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wereotter makes three Javelin attacks, or it makes one Bite attack and two Javelin attacks. It can replace one Javelin attack with a Net attack.

action.name: Bite (Otter or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) piercing damage. If the target is a Humanoid, it must succeed on a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw or be cursed with wereotter lycanthropy.

action.name: Javelin (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d6 + 1}) piercing damage.

action.name: Net (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 3} to hit, range 5/15 ft., one target. {@h}A Large or smaller creature hit by the net is {@condition restrained} until it is freed. The net has no effect on creatures that are formless or creature that are Huge or larger. A creature, including the target, can take its action to free the {@condition restrained} target by succeeding on a {@dc 11} Strength check. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the net (AC 10) frees the target without harming it, ending the effect and destroying the net.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The wereotter transforms into a Medium otterhumanoid hybrid or into a Large otter, or back into its true form, which is Humanoid. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.
Its soft pelt glimmering, the bipedal otter climbs onto the river's shoreline, water cascading off its fur. Tugging a short rope, the creature hoists up a net, brimming with flapping fish.

Wereotters are gentle, thoughtful lycanthropes with an air of mischievousness. In humanoid form, they remain quite calm, even in the face of threats, though they can become fierce when protecting themselves or a loved one. Wereotters do their utmost to not spread their curse to others and will bite a humanoid only when no other options are present. When facing hostile humanoids, wereotters rely on their net to capture a foe, then release the attacker far from the lycanthrope's home.

entries.name: Family-Oriented
entries: Groups of wereotters are extended families with the founding member cursed and passing their lycanthropy on to their children. Thus, non-lycanthropes can be found in these small clusters, having married into or been adopted by the family. These family groups tend to live outside most civilized towns and village.

entries.name: River and Swamp Dwellers
entries: Encountered most often in swamps, on lakeshores, or by rivers deep in mountain ranges or forest, wereotters use their unique skills to fish and forage for food for their family clusters. Some build small stilt villages over or along the water, while others use what nature has provided, living in large cave networks.

trait.name: Blazing Maelstrom Form
entries: The wild sirocco can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing. A creature that touches the sirocco or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage. In addition, the sirocco can enter a hostile creature's space and stop there. The first time it enters a creature's space on a turn, that creature takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage and must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The wild sirocco doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Water Susceptibility
entries: For every 5 feet the wild sirocco moves in water, or for every gallon of water splashed on it, it takes 1 cold damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wild sirocco makes two Fiery Wallop attacks.

action.name: Fiery Wallop
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Scorching Winds {@recharge 5}
entries: The wild sirocco whips up scorching winds around it in a blaze. Each creature within 10 feet of the wild sirocco must make a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) fire damage, ignites, and is thrown up to 20 feet in a random direction and knocked {@condition prone}. If a thrown creature strikes a solid surface, the creature takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it was thrown. If it is thrown at another creature, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take the same damage and be knocked {@condition prone}. Until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, an ignited creature takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The crone casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Beast Passivism
entries: No beast with an Intelligence of 3 or less can willingly attack the crone. They can be forced to do so through magical means.

trait.name: Speak with Beasts and Plants
entries: The crone can communicate with Beasts and Plants as if they shared a language.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wilderness crone makes three Staff attacks.

action.name: Wild Staff
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) force damage.

action.name: Needle Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The crone exhales pine needles in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 28 ({@damage 8d6}) piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

reaction.name: Transmigratory Strike
entries: When the crone kills a Humanoid, she can immediately restore it to life as a {@filter Beast|bestiary|type=beast} with a challenge rating no higher the Humanoid's CR or level. This reaction otherwise works like the {@spell reincarnate} spell.

variant.name: Wilderness Crone Variants
entries: Wilderness crones vary, depending on their home. To create a wilderness crone of a specific environment, use the statistics presented here, except as described below. Their CR is unchanged.

entries.name: Arctic
entries: Often called "snow crones," crones of the boreal forests have icy skin and resistance to cold damage. A snow crone's Needle Breath is a barrage of ice shards that deals both cold and piercing damage.

entries.name: Underground
entries: Dubbed "stone crones," crones tending fungal forests underground have stony skin and resistance to poison damage. A stone crone's Needle Breath is a cloud of spores that deals poison damage.

entries.name: Volcanic
entries: Known as "cinder crones," crones seeding the soil of volcanic regions have red and orange skin and resistance to fire damage. A cinder crone's Needle Breath is a roar of flames that deals fire damage.
An elderly woman with gnarled, bark-like skin and a cloak of pine branches stands beneath the boughs at the forest's edge.
Wilderness crones are not hags or evil, but they are often mistaken for both. They are protective of their forests, but if approached respectfully, they can be amicable and welcoming.

entries.name: Wisdom of the Woods
entries: Many seek wilderness crones for their knowledge and magic, but crones have no use for gold. They share their knowledge or power only after petitioners complete a task to protect or expand the forest.

entries.name: Solitary and Reclusive
entries: Wilderness crones live alone in remote forests. While they befriend beasts and plants and tolerate most fey, they are highly suspicious of intruders. They frighten away woodcutters, hunters, or others they believe will harm their home and its inhabitants. If intruders don't flee, the crones resort to direct confrontation.

entries.name: Wild Crones
entries: Wilderness crones mirror their environments, leading to variations. The most common wilderness crone inhabits temperate forests and is known as a "pine crone."

items.name: Needle Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The crone exhales a roar of flames in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 28 ({@damage 8d6}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
An elderly woman with gnarled, bark-like skin and a cloak of pine branches stands beneath the boughs at the forest's edge.
Wilderness crones are not hags or evil, but they are often mistaken for both. They are protective of their forests, but if approached respectfully, they can be amicable and welcoming.

entries.name: Wisdom of the Woods
entries: Many seek wilderness crones for their knowledge and magic, but crones have no use for gold. They share their knowledge or power only after petitioners complete a task to protect or expand the forest.

entries.name: Solitary and Reclusive
entries: Wilderness crones live alone in remote forests. While they befriend beasts and plants and tolerate most fey, they are highly suspicious of intruders. They frighten away woodcutters, hunters, or others they believe will harm their home and its inhabitants. If intruders don't flee, the crones resort to direct confrontation.

entries.name: Wild Crones
entries: Wilderness crones mirror their environments, leading to variations. The most common wilderness crone inhabits temperate forests and is known as a "pine crone."
An elderly woman with gnarled, bark-like skin and a cloak of pine branches stands beneath the boughs at the forest's edge.
Wilderness crones are not hags or evil, but they are often mistaken for both. They are protective of their forests, but if approached respectfully, they can be amicable and welcoming.

items.name: Needle Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The crone exhales a barrage of ice shards in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 28 ({@damage 8d6}) {@footnote cold and piercing damage|Converter's recommendation: 14 ({@damage 4d6}) cold damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) piercing damage.} on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
An elderly woman with gnarled, bark-like skin and a cloak of pine branches stands beneath the boughs at the forest's edge.

items.name: Needle Breath {@recharge 5}
